Unless you already have the ability to play multiple combat actions, this usually makes it clear to the other player you are playi

Body Blow.Name: Body Blow
Body Blow.Text: This well-placed blow will give all but the mightiest warriors pause for thought.

Body Slam.Name: Body Slam
Body Slam.Text: If the creature playing Body Slam did not play a Combat Action last round Body Slam does +2 damage. Body Slam will not do +2 damage if played in the first round of combat.

Broken Limb.Name: Broken Limb
Broken Limb.Text: If damaged by this attack, the victim plays all of her Combat Actions at -2 Rage for the duration of the combat.

Brutal Kick.Name: Brutal Kick
Brutal Kick.Text: No matter where you get kicked, it's bound to hurt.

Bum Rush.Name: Bum Rush
Bum Rush.Text: Play at the beginning of any combat round before combat action cards are exchanged. Each member of the character's pack comes forward to join the combat for the upcoming round only. At the end of the combat round, the pack withdraws from combat, leaving only the original character.
Bum Rush.Errata: Creates a Pack action. It creates either a pack attack or pack defense. Play before the first round of combat or between rounds. Creatures that came forward for a Bum Rush ESCAPE at the end of round rather than withdraw. (Revised 2014)

Cleft in Twain.Name: Cleft in Twain
Cleft in Twain.Text: The character cleaving his opponent in twain must have a weapon. This Combat Action may not be bluffed.

Flower of Aphrodite.Name: Flower of Aphrodite
Flower of Aphrodite.Text: No one may challenge or attack the owner of this fetish.  The Flower of Aphrodite is discarded the moment its owner attacks anything.
Flower of Aphrodite.Errata: Errata: If the user steps in and replaces something in a combat, it will not end the combat. It does not have to discard the Flower when it steps in. If the user joins a pack attack, it will cause the Flower to be discarded (updated 2014)"

Fool's Gold.Name: Fool's Gold
Fool's Gold.Text: During the Equip/Ally Phase after a character equips or receives Fool's Gold, he can trade it to any other character in play in exchange for any 1 piece of Equipment. The character must still meet any requirements before he can use that piece of Equipment
Fool's Gold.Errata: Traded equipment you can't equip is discarded. 11/30/15

Frankweiler Sword.Name: Frankweiler Sword
Frankweiler Sword.Text: Weapon. The Frankweiler Sword protects its wielder from the tyranny of arrogant nobility. If his Renown is less than his opponent's, the bearer of the weapon gains a number of Rage equal to the difference between his and his opponent's Renown for the dura

Gooshy Gooze.Name: Gooshy Gooze
Gooshy Gooze.Text: Opponents facing a character equipped with Gooshy Gooze lose 1 Rage and 1 Gnosis for the duration of the current combat. An opponent can never be reduced below 1 Rage or Gnosis as a result of Gooshy Gooze. A character cannot be equipped with more than 1 G

Grand Klaive.Name: Grand Klaive
Grand Klaive.Text: Weapon. All damage done by the Grand Klaive is aggravated. When in Crinos form, the owner acts at +1 Rage. The owner of a Grand Klaive acts at -1 Gnosis.

Gungnir.Name: Gungnir
Gungnir.Text: Weapon. Gungnir does Aggravated Damage to Wyrm creatures, including Wyrm characters. Guided by Wotan himself, attacks made with Gungnir may not be dodged, though they may be blocked. Gungnir endows its wielder with +1 Rage while in Crinos form. Only 2 Gun
Gungnir.Errata: Yes, two may be in play at the same time."

Heart of Midnight.Name: Heart of Midnight
Heart of Midnight.Text: The owner of this fetish cannot be affected by any Gifts. He can, however, still use Gifts himself. All enemies (creatures in the Hunting Grounds) are at +1 Rage when fighting the owner of this fetish.
Heart of Midnight.Errata: The user cannot be the target of any Gifts. For Gifts that target or affect multiple creatures at the same time, all other targets are still affected, the owner of Heart of Midnight treats the Gift's text box as if it was blank. Heart of Midnight only pro"

Heavy Machine Gun.Name: Heavy Machine Gun
Heavy Machine Gun.Text: Weapon. Firearm. Only useable in Crinos form. Play up to 2 damage-dealing Combat Actions of Rage 5 or lower each round of combat. However, if the Rage of either of your Combat Actions equals your opponent's Combat Action(s), the gun jams and cannot be use
Heavy Machine Gun.Errata: Clarification: Check for jamming at the beginning of the establish-bluff step (including illegal cards and bluffs). The jam takes effect from the following round. (2014)

Helios Bauble.Name: Helios Bauble
Helios Bauble.Text: Discard before combat or between rounds of combat. The Helios Bauble does 1 point of aggravated damage to any single opponent you are in combat with. If the target is a vampire, it takes an additional point of damage. Use Helios Bauble to mark the damage.

Improvised Weapon.Name: Improvised Weapon
Improvised Weapon.Text: Weapon. A character need not equip with Improvised Weapon during the Equip/Ally Phase; play it instead during any combat. For 1 round, the character may play 2 damage-dealing Combat Actions. Discard Improvised Weapon after it is used in a combat.

Dodge.Name: Dodge
Dodge.Text: Avoids one attack. Choose one damage card played this round on the character who has dodged and discard it.

Dry Gulch.Name: Dry Gulch
Dry Gulch.Text: Your opponent is struck firmly in the throat.

Entrail Rend.Name: Entrail Rend
Entrail Rend.Text: Almost nobody can receive an Entrail Rend and walk away from it.

Evade and Strike.Name: Evade and Strike
Evade and Strike.Text: Kailindo. You can dodge all attacks this round, except those that normally ignore dodges.

Evasion.Name: Evasion
Evasion.Text: Avoids (dodges) all attacks during the current combat round.

Eyes Gouged.Name: Eyes Gouged
Eyes Gouged.Text: A victim damaged by this attack is temporarily blinded. The victim must attack with a random draw from his combat hand during the next combat round.
Eyes Gouged.Errata: Clarification: See rules on Random Play of combat cards.

Fancy Footwork.Name: Fancy Footwork
Fancy Footwork.Text: The character can either dodge any 1 attack (even those that cannot normally be dodged), or she can dodge 2 attacks during the same round. You may choose how and what your character dodges after you have seen the opposing Combat Actions.
Fancy Footwork.Errata: CLARIFICATION: Fast Striking and Undodgeable are two different effects. Fancy Footwork may only dodge Undodgable effects. 11/30/15

Fast Strike.Name: Fast Strike
Fast Strike.Text: This card deals damage before your opponent's combat card takes effect. The special effect of Fast Strike is ignored if the character cannot meet the Rage requirements of this card.
Fast Strike.Errata: Clarification: This card has the Fast Striking keyword only if it is NOT a bluff. (2014)

Feint.Name: Feint
Feint.Text: You may play any Combat Action in immediate response to your opponent's Combat Action. If your Feint is a bluff and your opponent's Combat Action is not, you may not play another card. Attacks that exceed your Rage are still considered bluffs.
Feint.Errata: See rules on Feinting. This card played after seeing opponents card targets the same creature as the Feint.  You may only play one card asa  follow up to Feint, even if you may play multiple combat actions. Errata: Feint may not be bluffed."

Fetal Position.Name: Fetal Position
Fetal Position.Text: This action blocks 1 attack of damage 6 or less.
Fetal Position.Errata: Errata: Fetal Position blocks 6 damage from any single damage card.

Flesh Wound.Name: Flesh Wound
Flesh Wound.Text: Sometimes a Flesh Wound is all it takes to finish off an opponent.

Flicker.Name: Flicker
Flicker.Text: Only usable in the Umbra. Dodge all attacks played against you this round.

Flying Tiger.Name: Flying Tiger
Flying Tiger.Text: Kailindo. Flying Tiger may never be used during the same round in which the character plays a defensive card or a Feint, nor may it be bluffed.

Forceful Wind.Name: Forceful Wind
Forceful Wind.Text: Kailindo. Combat immediately ends after both sides have dealt damage.
Forceful Wind.Errata: Clarification: Combat ends after combat cards played this round have resolved.

Sylvan-Ivanovich-Sylvan.Name: Sylvan-Ivanovich-Sylvan
Sylvan-Ivanovich-Sylvan.Keywords: Garou - Shadow Lords - Theurge - Homid - Gaia - Female
Sylvan-Ivanovich-Sylvan.Text: Sylvan can vote in Junta called by Shadow Lords, regardless of restrictions or allegiance, and has +2 renown when doing so.

Syntax.Name: Syntax
Syntax.Keywords: Garou - Glass Walkers - Ragabash - Homid - Gaia - Female
Syntax.Text: Syntax is an expert Monkeywrencher.  All Pentex enemies she or her pack faces in combat are considered -2 Rage.

T. F. MacNeil.Name: T. F. MacNeil
T. F. MacNeil.Keywords: Human - Pentex - Executive - Homid - Beast-of-War - Wyrm - Team #21 - Male
T. F. MacNeil.Text: The Leader of First Team #21. He can pack attack or defend with any and all other Team #21 members in his pack. You may draw 2 additional combat cards per additional Team #21 member in combat. T.F. can equip with any fetish or Bane fetish Equipment regard
T. F. MacNeil.Errata: T.F. Can use any Fetish/Bane equipment. He does not need to meet any Gnosis requirements. He still needs to meet other requirements to equip it.

Tamara Lovegrove.Name: Tamara Lovegrove
Tamara Lovegrove.Keywords: Human - Cults - 7th Generation - Homid - Eater-of-Souls - Medical Caste - Wyrm - Female
Tamara Lovegrove.Text: While in play, Tamara can choose any 1 Garou character and 'lock' them into whichever form they're in (Homid, Crinos or Lupus). This cannot be done until the second turn of play, but will last for as long as Tamara does.
Tamara Lovegrove.Errata: Once Tamara selects a target, she may not change it. If Tamara's target and Tamara simultaneously do lethal damage to each other, the targetted Garou still die without flipping to Crinos."

Tanzut.Name: Tanzut
Tanzut.Keywords: Garou - Silent Striders - Philodox - Lupus - Gaia - Kailindo - Female

Technician #7.Name: Technician #7
Technician #7.Keywords: Iliad - Fomori - Pentex - Homid - Beast-of-War - Wyrm - Male
Technician #7.Text: Technician #7 can play 2 Combat Actions when in his Battle form.
Technician #7.Errata: CLARIFICATION: He can play 2 Combat Actions PER ROUND when in his Battle form.

Teeth-of-Titanium.Name: Teeth-of-Titanium
Teeth-of-Titanium.Keywords: Garou - Glass Walkers - Ahroun - Lupus - Gaia - Male
Teeth-of-Titanium.Text: As much machine as animal, Teeth-of-Titanium rarely shows emotion and cannot frenzy.

The General.Name: The General
The General.Keywords: Human - Cults - 7th Generation - Homid - Defiler - Warrior Caste - Wyrm - Male
The General.Text: The General can pull any and all police out of the Hunting Grounds to assist him in a pack attack or defense. Although the controlling player draws no extra cards, she may choose which members of the 'pack' receive her opponent's damage cards.
The General.Errata: Police that The General brings into your pack are considered Allies (and are worth full VP to anyone who kills them). His ability to redirect damage only applies to himself and Police he brings into the pack attack/defence; he cannot redirect damage to or

Thomas Kachina.Name: Thomas Kachina
Thomas Kachina.Keywords: Garou - Wendigo - Ahroun - Homid - Gaia - Male
Thomas Kachina.Text: Thomas can automatically pack attack or defend with his twin sister Allison.  If Allison is in another player's pack, Allison joins at that player's option.

Thunder Tiger.Name: Thunder Tiger
Thunder Tiger.Keywords: Garou - Stargazers - Ahroun - Lupus - Gaia - Kailindo - Male

Tim Rowantree.Name: Tim Rowantree
Tim Rowantree.Keywords: Garou - Children of Gaia - Ahroun - Homid - Gaia - Male
Tim Rowantree.Text: If Tim's pack gets a caern, Tim gains +2 Rage and +1 Health.

Tracker By Moonlight.Name: Tracker By Moonlight
Tracker By Moonlight.Keywords: Garou - Fianna - Ahroun - Homid - Gaia - Male
Tracker By Moonlight.Text: Making his home in the Blue Ridge Mountains, Tracker-by-Moonlight hunts down his enemies wherever they run. As his alpha action, he may attack any wounded target in play. This attack may not be declined and is not considered a challenge.

True Silverheels.Name: True Silverheels
True Silverheels.Keywords: Garou - Children of Gaia - Ahroun - Homid - Gaia - Male
True Silverheels.Text: True may begin the game armed with a Klaive Equipment card.

Tsannik.Name: Tsannik
Tsannik.Text: Tsannik cannot easily be killed in the physical world. Once in his Battle form, the controlling player can choose to move him into the Umbra at the end of any combat round. This action cannot be stopped.

Typhoon the Unpure.Name: Typhoon the Unpure
Typhoon the Unpure.Text: Typhoon gains 1 Rage for every Child of Gaia in play. When fighting one of the Children of Gaia, Typhoon can automatically frenzy.

Uncle Freddy.Name: Uncle Freddy
Uncle Freddy.Keywords: Human - Cults - 7th Generation - Homid - Eater-of-Souls - Snatcher Caste - Wyrm - Male
Uncle Freddy.Text: Victims Uncle Freddy single-handedly kills are worth 1 additional victory point.

Virus-to-Wyrm.Name: Virus-to-Wyrm
Virus-to-Wyrm.Keywords: Garou - Glass Walkers - Ahroun - Homid - Gaia - Female
Virus-to-Wyrm.Text: Virus is +2 Rage when combating Wyrm opponents, but is still considered Rage 2 for changing to Crinos form.

Volcheka Ibarruri.Name: Volcheka Ibarruri
Volcheka Ibarruri.Keywords: Garou - Black Fury - Ahroun - Lupus - Gaia - Female
Volcheka Ibarruri.Text: No Wendigo will voluntarily initiate or join an attack against Volcheka.

Voragg the Unbound.Name: Voragg the Unbound
Voragg the Unbound.Text: Voragg can use any Get of Fenris Gifts.

Wahya-Ohni.Name: Wahya-Ohni
Wahya-Ohni.Keywords: Garou - Wendigo - Ahroun - Lupus - Gaia - Male
Wahya-Ohni.Text: When in the Umbra, Wahya-Ohni can regenerate one additional damage card during his Regeneration Phase.

Wailer.Name: Wailer
Wailer.Keywords: Iliad - Fomori - Pentex - Homid - Defiler - Wyrm - Male
Wailer.Text: When in combat against Wailer, opponents with less Gnosis than Wailer cannot play a Combat Action on the round following the Wailer's transformation into his Battle form.

Walks-with-Might.Name: Walks-with-Might
Walks-with-Might.Keywords: Garou - Silent Striders - Ahroun - Lupus - Gaia - Male
Walks-with-Might.Text: Walks-with-Might may escape from any combat after the fourth round.

Whispers-in-Pines.Name: Whispers-in-Pines
Whispers-in-Pines.Keywords: Garou - Wendigo - Galliard - Lupus - Gaia - Female
Whispers-in-Pines.Text: Whispers serves many totem spirits. She may have more than one personal totem at a time. Draw a sept card when she plays a personal totem.

Wind-Across-the-Hills.Name: Wind-Across-the-Hills
Wind-Across-the-Hills.Keywords: Garou - Fianna - Ragabash - Homid - Gaia - Male
Wind-Across-the-Hills.Text: Wind-Across-the-Hills is considered Renown zero for purposes of joining pack attacks or defenses.

Windchaser.Name: Windchaser
Windchaser.Keywords: Garou - Uktena - Philodox - Lupus - Gaia - Female
Windchaser.Text: Windchaser is familiar with the Rites of the Garou. For the purposes of using Rites, she is considered Renown 6.
Windchaser.Errata: Windchaser's ability should read 'Windchaser is +2 Renown for purposes of using Rites.'

Zachary Ellison.Name: Zachary Ellison
Zachary Ellison.Keywords: Garou - Shadow Lords - Philodox - Homid - Gaia - Male
Zachary Ellison.Text: Zachary cannot be in any pack unless a Shadow Lords is the highest-Renown character in the Pack.  Discard Zachary if he is forced into such a pack.

Zhyzhak.Name: Zhyzhak
Zhyzhak.Keywords: Garou - Black Spiral Dancer - Ahroun - Homid - Beast-of-War - Wyrm - Female
Zhyzhak.Text: Zhyzhak will never refuse a challenge. Zhyzhak is the Warder of the Trinity Hive Caern. If it belongs to her pack, Zhyzhak gains 1 Rage and 2 Health. Zhyzhak can never benefit from Past Lives or Personal Totems.

Zoe Sense-of-Snow.Name: Zoe Sense-of-Snow
Zoe Sense-of-Snow.Keywords: Garou - Silent Striders - Galliard - Homid - Gaia - Female
Zoe Sense-of-Snow.Text: Zoe's pack prevents all damage done to them by Gifts, and damage done to them cannot be increased by Gifts.

Amber Eyes-Like-Knives.Name: Amber Eyes-Like-Knives
Amber Eyes-Like-Knives.Text: At the end of the Regeneration Phase, Amber may target a creature.  Neither she nor the target may take any Actions other than Combat Actions during the next Resource Phase.  She may not choose the same target twice in a row.

Carl Bailor.Name: Carl Bailor
Carl Bailor.Keywords: Human - Pentex - Executive - Homid - Defiler - Wyrm - Male
Carl Bailor.Text: Defiler.  Carl is a master of trapping items in customs.  Every other Resource Phase he may target a piece of Equipment in play.  Place it face down on top of the owner's sept deck.  Loyalty:  Julia Lindelle.

Chiaka.Name: Chiaka
Chiaka.Keywords: Garou - Glass Walkers - Philodox - Homid - Gaia - Female
Chiaka.Text: During the Regeneration Phase, Chiaka may target a Character and prevent it from changing forms until the end of turn or until Chiaka dies, whichever is sooner.  She may not target a Character more than once.

Chuki.Name: Chuki
Chuki.Text: Chuki is dedicated to defending its clan.  Chuki may automatically join any Ajaba packmate in pack defense.  Chuki is a hermaphrodite, so is both male and female.

Dr. Michelle Backman.Name: Dr. Michelle Backman
Dr. Michelle Backman.Keywords: Human - Pentex - Executive - Homid - Eater-of-Souls - Wyrm - Female
Dr. Michelle Backman.Text: Eater-of-Souls.  Dr. Backman conducts deadly research.  Once per game she can target a Wyrm creature.  While she lives, that creature does aggravated damage but acts at -2 Rage.

Falling Sky.Name: Falling Sky
Falling Sky.Keywords: Garou - Stargazers - Ragabash - Metis - Gaia - Wyrm - Male
Falling Sky.Text: Falling Sky is so eager to purge the Wyrm from Africa that he is easily led astray.  He gains +2 Rage when fighting opponents of lower Gnosis.

Fukayna the Pathfinder.Name: Fukayna the Pathfinder
Fukayna the Pathfinder.Text: Once per turn, Fukayna may target a player.  He searches his sept deck from the top down and discards the first Fetish or Bane Fetish he comes to.  Shuffle the deck.

Henry Seeks-Wonder.Name: Henry Seeks-Wonder
Henry Seeks-Wonder.Text: Henry is easy-going and slow to anger.  Henry may defend his pack's Territories instead of his Alpha.

Ironjaw.Name: Ironjaw
Ironjaw.Text: If neither Ironjaw nor her target in combat have a weapon, her attacks do one additional damage.  Rivalry: Simba

Kolya Blood-of-Iron.Name: Kolya Blood-of-Iron
Kolya Blood-of-Iron.Keywords: Garou - Silver Fangs - Galliard - Homid - Gaia - Male
Kolya Blood-of-Iron.Text: During the Regeneration Phase, reveal the top 3 cards of your sept deck.  Place any Moots or Rites revealed in your hand and discard the rest.  Kolya dies if your Sept Deck is ever empty.  He may use Philodox Gifts.

Mtupeni.Name: Mtupeni
Mtupeni.Text: Mtupeni is a terrible bully and acts at +2 Rage if she is facing a creature in combat with Renown 1 or less.

Murgatroyd.Name: Murgatroyd
Murgatroyd.Keywords: Human - Pentex - Executive - Homid - Defiler - Wyrm - Female
Murgatroyd.Text: Defiler. Murgatroyd has the cash and connections to overcome any setbacks to her Congo operation.  Once per turn you may shuffle a Resource or Ally from your sept discards into your sept deck.

Njoki Scarface.Name: Njoki Scarface
Njoki Scarface.Text: Njoki must take one additional damage card beyond that normally required to kill her before she will die.  She may not play Combat Actions unless she is wounded.

Sand's Last King.Name: Sand's Last King
Sand's Last King.Text: Sand's Last King is using his position in the Ahadi to gain access to other Fera's allies.  He may recruit Allies which require Ajaba, Bastet or Silent Striders.

Swims-in-Mbembe's-Wake.Name: Swims-in-Mbembe's-Wake
Swims-in-Mbembe's-Wake.Text: As the discoverer of the lost Spear of Mokole-Mbembe, once per turn, Swims may allow one other Mokole to ignore an effect that would prevent it taking an action.

Thousand Cubs.Name: Thousand Cubs
Thousand Cubs.Text: Thousand Cubs has +2 Renown for voting on Moots called by Characters of lower Renown.  Loyalty: Ajaba.

'Wyrm Slayer', Ronin Garou\tUnlimited\tr.Name:
'Wyrm Slayer', Ronin Garou	Unlimited	rage.image.ally.wyrm.slayer.ronin.garou	[GC-C][GM-C][GU-U]	Ally	Regenerates	Ahroun - Philodox	Garou - Ronin	4	5	2	4					The Ronin Wyrm Slayer may not vote in moots. Only 1 Wyrm Slayer may be played per game. Wyrm Slayer may not refuse a challenge. This ally is considered to be in Crinos form.	
Akashic Brother	LegacyOfTheTribes	rage.image.ally.akashic.brother	[GM-C][GU-U]	Ally		Stargazers	Human - Mage - Kailindo	8	5	10	3					Kailindo. This mage is a master of mind magic; his Combat Actions are always resolved before his opponent's. He may use Stargazers, Philodox and Galliard Gifts.

Ally 3	Playtest	rage.image.ally.ally.3		Ally				3	3	3	3					Playtesting info available online at http://www.rainydaypaperback.com/rage.html	
Ally 4	Playtest	rage.image.ally.ally.4		Ally				4	4	4	4					Playtesting info available online at http://www.rainydaypaperback.com/rage.html

Original: Once per turn the Dreamspeaker can freely enter or leave the Umbra during any phase of the game. Once per turn, the Dreamspeaker can cancel any one Gift. The Dreamspeaker can use any Auspice Gifts.

Dreamweaver Kitsune.Name: Dreamweaver Kitsune
Dreamweaver Kitsune.Text: A master of illusion, the Kitsune (werefox) may replace his pack alpha at the beginning of any combat before Combat Actions are played. The Dreamweaver Kitsune may use Ragabash Gifts.

El Dorado.Name: El Dorado
El Dorado.Text: A Dreamspeaker and master of his own Realm, El Dorado may use any Gifts (including Wyrm Gifts). Only one El Dorado may be played per game.
El Dorado.Errata: He only has his printed abilities. He does not get the Dreamspeaker Mage's abilities on top of his own.

Enticer.Name: Enticer
Enticer.Text: Opponents who have less Gnosis than the Enticer cannot play a Combat Action during the first round of combat.  The Enticer cannot bluff with Combat Actions which exceed Rage 6.

Ewaipanoma.Name: Ewaipanoma
Ewaipanoma.Requires: Uktena - Black Furies - Mokole - Bastet - El Dorado
Ewaipanoma.Errata: Errata: If you have El Dorado as an Ally he may recruit Ewaipanoma as an Ally, even though Allies may not normally recruit Allies. He's special.  updated 10/13/2015"

Experimental Fomori.Name: Experimental Fomori
Experimental Fomori.Text: The Experimental Fomori can use Iliad Gifts of Gnosis 3 or less.

Faerie Kin.Name: Faerie Kin
Faerie Kin.Text: Summons a faerie spirit.  A pack can only have one faerie ally at a time.  The faerie can use Gifts from any breed or auspice.
Faerie Kin.Errata: Errata: You can only have one Faerie KIN at a time. You may have other Faerie allies. (2014)

Flame Spirit.Name: Flame Spirit
Flame Spirit.Text: The Flame Spirit can burn itself out in one damage 3 attack (aggravated).  The spirit may only be affected by Umbral attacks and Gifts.
Flame Spirit.Errata: Clarification: It may play normal Rage 1 or less combat actions and bluff as usual. See rules on Alternative Combat Actions. Use the Flame Spirit to mark damage. Rite of Binding will not work on a Flame Spirit that exploded. ""Umbral attacks"" are attacks p"

Fomori Dock Worker.Name: Fomori Dock Worker
Fomori Dock Worker.Text: For every Fomori in the Hunting Grounds add 1 Rage to this ally.

Fractured Nephandus.Name: Fractured Nephandus
Fractured Nephandus.Text: The Nephandus is an evil and extremely corrupt mage. He is able to use any aspect Gifts. While the Fractured Nephandus is in play, no other mages may use their special abilities or Gifts, including Enemy and Victim mages.
Fractured Nephandus.Errata: See definition of special ability in rules (under Character Cards)

Gaffling Pest.Name: Gaffling Pest
Gaffling Pest.Text: The Gaffling is a minor spirit which is easily summoned and controlled.  The Gaffling can use Gifts of any breed.

Gangrel Ally.Name: Gangrel Ally
Gangrel Ally.Text: The Gangrel is a vampire who, for whatever reason, has developed a friendship with 1 particular Garou.  The Gangrel can only act every other turn.  The Gangrel cannot act during the turn he is brought into play.
Gangrel Ally.Errata: Errata: The Gangrel Ally may play combat actions if it is attacked or part of a pack defense. See rules on what counts as an action vs not an action

Griffin Avatar.Name: Griffin Avatar
Griffin Avatar.Text: The Griffin Avatar is a retainer who serves the mighty Totem-spirit of the Red Talons. The Avatar must join any pack actions that his pack uses if he is eligible. Griffin Avatar is not a spirit.
Griffin Avatar.Errata: GA counts against the renown total of pack actions.

Haunter.Name: Haunter
Haunter.Text: The Haunter is a wraith  adept at inducing terror. It may use Fomori, Metis and Shadow Lords Gifts. Any Gifts of 4 Gnosis or lower it uses may cross the Gauntlet. The Haunter exists only in the Umbra.

Iglanotti The Wise.Name: Iglanotti The Wise
Iglanotti The Wise.Text: Iglanotti possesses great wisdom and insight. He may use any Gaia Gifts. Every other turn, during the Redraw Phase, the pack with Iglanotti as an Ally may search through their sept deck for any single Gift. Reshuffle your sept deck afterwards if you use t
Iglanotti The Wise.Errata: Errata: Iglanatti can use any Gifts requiring one of the 13 Gaian Garou tribes or an auspice.

Jack-O-Lantern.Name: Jack-O-Lantern
Jack-O-Lantern.Text: The Jack-O-Lantern cannot initiate combat.  Whenever 1 of your non-alpha characters is attacked, you may exchange it for the Jack-O-Lantern before combat begins.  Combat now continues with the Jack-O-Lantern.
Jack-O-Lantern.Errata: CLARIFICATION: When your non-Alpha character is attacked, you may redirect the attack to Jack-O-Lantern. Jack-o-Lantern cannot voluntarily join a pack attack, but may join a pack defense."

Joseph Herlech.Name: Joseph Herlech
Joseph Herlech.Text: Joseph Herlech found his way to the Dorado Realm through sheer force of will in 1933. Discard all Realms when Joseph enters play. No new Realms may be played while Joseph is in play. Only 1 Joseph Herlech may be played per game.

Junkyard 'Dog'.Name: Junkyard 'Dog'
Junkyard 'Dog'.Text: Unique. Animal. This Kinfolk can scrounge for Equipment. Once per turn before the Combat Phase, he may select a non-Unique piece of Equipment in your discard pile. Give this to a Bonegnawer who can equip the item.

Ka Spirit.Name: Ka Spirit
Ka Spirit.Text: The Ka Spirit (the soul of a Mummy) is truly immortal and can never be destroyed. If the Ka Spirit is killed, place it back into its owner's sept deck and shuffle. The Ka Spirit is not affected by Gifts which specifically affect spirits.
Ka Spirit.Errata: If ka Spirit is removed or discarded from play without being killed, it is discarded, not shuffled back into the deck.  You cannot use Deep Journey to search for Ka Spirit."

Kinfolk Environmental Activist.Name: Kinfolk Environmental Activist
Kinfolk Environmental Activist.Text: The Environmental Activist can remove 1 member of your pack from the Hunting Grounds per turn.
Kinfolk Environmental Activist.Errata: Clarification: No, this cannot return a character targetted by Skindancer or Expulsion to your pack (2014)"

Kinfolk Folk Singer.Name: Kinfolk Folk Singer
Kinfolk Folk Singer.Text: Once per turn, Kinfolk Folk Singer can discard a sept card that is causing one or more members of your pack to lose Gnosis.

Kinfolk Packleader.Name: Kinfolk Packleader
Kinfolk Packleader.Text: Kinfolk Packleader can automatically pack defend with your Kinfolk Allies. Draw a combat card when this occurs. The Kinfolk Packleader is in Lupus form.

Kinfolk Shaman.Name: Kinfolk Shaman
Kinfolk Shaman.Text: The Shaman can use any Theurge or Lupus Gifts. Spirits will not attack the Shaman, but they will defend themselves against him if attacked.

Kinfolk Small Town Cop.Name: Kinfolk Small Town Cop
Kinfolk Small Town Cop.Text: Once per turn, just before alphas are selected, the Cop can select any 1 character in Homid form to put in jail on false charges.  That character is removed from play until the beginning of the next Regeneration Phase. The Cop is considered to be armed with a .38 pistol. No equipment card is needed.
Kinfolk Small Town Cop.Errata: Errata: If his .38 is stolen or destroyed, his Rage becomes 1 for the duration of the combat. (2014)"

Kinfolk Soldier of Fortune.Name: Kinfolk Soldier of Fortune
Kinfolk Soldier of Fortune.Text: The Soldier of Fortune is considered to be armed with a shotgun.  No Equipment card is needed.  If the Soldier of Fortune is disarmed, his Rage becomes 3.

Kinfolk TV Reporter.Name: Kinfolk TV Reporter
Kinfolk TV Reporter.Text: Your pack gains 2 votes during all moots while the TV Reporter is in play, due to his ability to sway public opinion around events.

Kinfolk Veterinarian.Name: Kinfolk Veterinarian
Kinfolk Veterinarian.Text: During you Regeneration Phase, the Veterinarian can cure 1 additional damage card (including an aggravated wound) from 1 character.
Kinfolk Veterinarian.Errata: Clarification: This is considered healing a wound, not regenerating. It occurs after regular regeneration. (2014)"

Lasombra Mafioso.Name: Lasombra Mafioso
Lasombra Mafioso.Text: Opponents may not use Firearms when fighting the Lasombra Mafioso.  The Lasombra Mafioso can use Iliad Fomori and Black Spiral Dancer Gifts.

Mad Scientist.Name: Mad Scientist
Mad Scientist.Text: You may discard the Mad Scientist to bring any 1 enemy of 8 or less Renown from the Hunting Grounds into your pack as an ally.  The Mad Scientist can use Odyssey Gifts.
Mad Scientist.Errata: creatures which can't be bound or controlled can't be targeted by Mad Scientist.

Man in Black.Name: Man in Black
Man in Black.Text: The Man in Black can use Theurge and Black Spiral Dancer Gifts.  He may use Kailindo Combat Actions.

Meat Puppet.Name: Meat Puppet
Meat Puppet.Text: A Meat Puppet can pack attack or defend (at the controlling player's option) with any other Meat Puppets in the game.  The player controlling the Meat Puppet initially involved in the combat will get the victory points for any kills made.

Misfit Fomori.Name: Misfit Fomori
Misfit Fomori.Text: Experiments gone horribly wrong, most of these things are destined for the gas chambers. However, some still get appropriated by executives when they have to reinforce the Amazon front during a budget crunch.

Moon Sisters Cult.Name: Moon Sisters Cult
Moon Sisters Cult.Text: The Moon Sisters are a chapter of the Sisterhood, one of the camps of the Black Furies. While they are in play, no Territory that their pack controls may be attacked. They may use Black Furies Gifts.

Nameless Cannon Fodder.Name: Nameless Cannon Fodder
Nameless Cannon Fodder.Text: You may have an unlimited number of Nameless Cannon Fodder in your deck.

Orville.Name: Orville
Orville.Text: A defector Fomori, Orville knows things about things. When he is engaging a Battlefield the engaging Renown of the opposing side is reduced by 3. Orville can only reduce the opposing side of a Battlefield if it is of the Wyrm. Packs with Orville as an all
Orville.Errata: Unique. when Orville is in play, Wyrm Alphas are +3 Renown when defending a Battlefield. 12/6/15"

Pentex Executive and Limousine.Name: Pentex Executive and Limousine
Pentex Executive and Limousine.Text: The Pentex Executive has 3 votes which can be placed in a board meeting or in a moot.  The Pentex Executive can destroy 1 caern per game.

Plaintive Jaggling.Name: Plaintive Jaggling
Plaintive Jaggling.Requires: Any Gaia Theurge - Any Gaia Philodox
Plaintive Jaggling.Text: The Plaintive Jaggling is a spirit adept at tricking Wyrm spirits. Any pack with the Jaggling as an Ally may use Bane Fetishes as if they were Gaia Fetishes. If the Jaggling leaves play, discard any Bane Fetishes equipped by the pack.

Psychotic Stalker.Name: Psychotic Stalker
Psychotic Stalker.Text: When alpha, the Psychotic Stalker will only attack non-alpha opponents.  His challenges cannot be refused.  The Stalker cannot participate in pack actions.

Puppeteer.Name: Puppeteer
Puppeteer.Text: Restricted. Once per turn, the Puppeteer can replace the Combat Action of a lower Gnosis creature with a card from the Puppeteer's controller's hand, as long as the Puppeteer's pack is not in the combat.

Rambunctious Satyr.Name: Rambunctious Satyr
Rambunctious Satyr.Text: The Rambunctious Satyr is a faerie who enjoys a good time. She can use Ragabash, Galliard and Hellcat Gifts.

Red-headed Stepchild.Name: Red-headed Stepchild
Red-headed Stepchild.Text: When your pack alpha takes a wound that would kill him, you may switch him with your Red-headed Stepchild - killing the child instead.  A pack may not have more than 1 Red-headed Stepchild at a time.
Red-headed Stepchild.Errata: see rules on redirecting damage and death blow effects

Spirit. These destructive fire elemental.Name: Spirit. These destructive fire elementals burn out of control. Reduce your sept draw by 1 for each Salamander you control.�

Samedi Wretch.Name: Samedi Wretch
Samedi Wretch.Text: Horrific and decaying, the Samedi is a vampire who seeks mercenary employment. Unable to show his face in public, he stalks the darkness and hides among the refuse of humanity.

Shadow Walker.Name: Shadow Walker
Shadow Walker.Text: A long-standing enemy of Pentex, Shadow Walker has made many enemies among their Fomori ranks. When he is alpha, any Fomori alphas must declare their attack against him. Shadow Walker may use Homid, Ragabash, and Wendigo Gifts, and is considered to be in

Siberakh Relation.Name: Siberakh Relation
Siberakh Relation.Text: The Siberakh are a bloodline offshoot of the Silver Fangs who usually avoid all of the political endeavors of the Garou. The Siberakh Relation may use Silver Fangs and Wendigo Gifts and is considered to be in Crinos form.

Song of the Great Beast.Name: Song of the Great Beast
Song of the Great Beast.Text: The Song summons a legendary white bison. The bison may not take any actions during the turn it is brought into play.
Song of the Great Beast.Errata: Errata: Song of the Great Beast may play combat actions if it is attacked or part of a pack defense. See rules on what counts as an action vs not an action (2014)

Spectral Shade.Name: Spectral Shade
Spectral Shade.Text: The Spectral Shade has forgotten its identity. It shadows the character who recruits it; if that character is attacked, the Shade may replace it in combat. The Shade can be alpha, but can't declare an attack.

Spirit Tiger.Name: Spirit Tiger
Spirit Tiger.Text: This ferocious jungle spirit may not take any actions (other than defense) during the turn it is brought into play.
Spirit Tiger.Errata: The Spirit Tiger may play combat actions if it is attacked or part of a pack defense. See rules on what counts as an action vs not an action. Updated 10/13/2015

Stormcrow.Name: Stormcrow
Stormcrow.Text: Stormcrows are spirits that can automatically combine for pack attack or defense. Draw one additional combat card for every Stormcrow after the first that is drawn into combat. Stormcrows will not pack combine when fighting a Shadow Lords.
Stormcrow.Errata: Stormcrows may automatically pack attack/defend with any packmember in the Umbra.

Oracle of Sobek.Name: Oracle of Sobek
Oracle of Sobek.Text: Unique.  Animal.  Kinfolk.  Once per turn you may ask a player if he has a specific type of card in his sept hand (e.g. Gift).  If so, he discards one card of that type from his hand.  Oracle of Sobek can use Mokole Gifts.

Bastet Gossip Network.Name: Bastet Gossip Network
Bastet Gossip Network.Text: Kinfolk. Human. The Bastet Gossip Network can vote in any Junta regardless of restrictions or allegiance.  When Bastet Gossip Network is played, shuffle all other copies of it in play into their owners' sept decks.

Aeneid Fomori.Name: Aeneid Fomori
Aeneid Fomori.Text: The Aenid Fomori may use Iliad and Odyssey Gifts. He will not pack defend with other Fomori in the Hunting Grounds.
Aeneid Fomori.Errata: Can use Fomori, Iliad Fomori and Odyssey Fomori Gifts"

Anaconda Gafflings.Name: Anaconda Gafflings
Anaconda Gafflings.Text: The Anaconda Gafflings can use Defiler and Bane Gifts.

Angus, the White Howler\tTheWyrm\trage.i.Name: Angus, the White Howler	TheWyrm	rage.image.victim.angus.the.white.howler	[VU]	Victim			Garou	12	9	8	10					Angus can use Galliard, Homid, and Wendigo Gifts. If Angus survives in the Hunting Grounds for 3 turns he becomes an ally of the Gaia pack with the lowest total Renown. Only 1 Angus can be played per game.	When determining who has the least remaining renown, you count all packmembers, including Allies and creatures temporarily out of play.

Arkady.Name: Arkady
Arkady.Text: If you defeat Arkady, you can make him a pack ally instead of killing him. If you do this, you gain no victory points, but instead gain an ally. If recruited, Arkady will still be wounded from all but the killing blow.

Avenging Wraith.Name: Avenging Wraith
Avenging Wraith.Text: This dark spirit exists only in the Umbra. The Avenging Wraith removes the highest Gnosis Bane Fetish from play at the end of each Combat Phase. The Avenging Wraith can use Fianna Gifts.

Bane Morphling.Name: Bane Morphling
Bane Morphling.Text: Kailindo. The Bane Morphling is highly flexible. It may use Beast-of-War Gifts. The Bane Morphling may not be bound or controlled.

Bawn Guardian.Name: Bawn Guardian
Bawn Guardian.Text: This spirit is the guardian of a Caern's outer boundaries. If there are no Caerns in play, Bawn Guardian is immediately discarded.

Beat Cop.Name: Beat Cop
Beat Cop.Text: Police. The Beat Cop is armed with a .38 Pistol. If disarmed, his Rage becomes 1.

Black Spiral Dancer.Name: Black Spiral Dancer
Black Spiral Dancer.Text: A lone Black Spiral Dancer has stumbled into the Hunting Grounds. He may use any Metis, Ragabash, or Ahroun Gifts. The Black Spiral Dancer is considered to be in Crinos form.

If alphas are tied, the Good Ol' Boy will attack a Gaia alpha before attacking a Wyrm alpha. If a Wyrm alpha kills the Good	
Family of Five	TheWyrm	rage.image.victim.family.of.five	[VC]	Victim			Human	4	1	1	3					The Family of Five can play up to 3 Combat Actions per round of combat.	
Family Pet	TheWyrm	rage.image.victim.family.pet	[VC]	Victim			Animal	2	2	1	2					The Family Pet will automatically pack defend with the Family of Five or the Playground Full of Kids, if either of them are attacked.	
FBI Agent	TheWyrm	rage.image.victim.fbi.agent	[VU]	Victim			Human	6	4	1	4					While this Agent is in play, Black Spiral Dancers cannot be alpha.	
Fomori (1)	Unlimited	rage.image.enemy.fomori1	[EC]	Enemy			Fomori	4	2	0	4					All Fomori in Hunting Grounds automatically combine for pack defense.	The two fomori enemies in Limited/Unlimited should be called 'Pack Fomori.' Their ability to pack defend applies only to Pack Fomori.
Fomori (2)	Unlimited	rage.image.enemy.fomori2	[EC]	Enemy			Fomori	4	3	0	3					All Fomori in Hunting Grounds automatically combine for pack defense.	The two fomori enemies in Limited/Unlimited should be called 'Pack Fomori.' Their ability to pack defend applies only to Pack Fomori.
Fomori Cop	NewEngland-Gauntlet	fomoricop_	[EU]	Enemy			Fomori - Firearm - Police	5	5	2	4					Police. The Fomori Cop is armed with a firearm. If disarmed, his Rage becomes 3. At the end of the Combat Phase, the Cop discards a piece of non-fetish Equipment equipped by a Gaia creature (chosen by a random player).	
Furmling	Unlimited	rage.image.enemy.furmling	[EC]	Enemy			Spirit	5	4	3	4					An elemental spirit of balefire infests the area. All damage from a Furmling is aggravated.	Original: An elemental of balefire infests the area.  All damage from a Furmling is aggravated.
Garou Kinfolk	TheWyrm	rage.image.victim.garou.kinfolk	[VU]	Victim			Human - Kinfolk	4	4	2	4					When put into play, select 1 Garou in play who is related to this Kinfolk. If this Kinfolk is killed, that Garou may immediately attack the killer. A new combat will begin as normal.	
Glade Child	TheWyrm	rage.image.victim.glade.child	[VU]	Victim			Spirit	7	5	7	6					The Glade Child is a tree spirit. At the end of the Combat Phase the Glade Child removes ALL Mass Pollution cards from play. A Glade Spirit can use any Lupus, Metis, Bane, or Children of Gaia Gifts.	CLARIFICATION: "Glade Spirit" refers to the Glade Child. It can use can use any Lupus, Metis, Bane, or Children of Gaia Gifts. 10/24/2015

Glass Elemental.Name: Glass Elemental
Glass Elemental.Text: A spirit recently conceived from humankind's skyscraper cities, Glass Elementals are every bit as potent as their traditional brethren.

Greenpeace Assault Team.Name: Greenpeace Assault Team
Greenpeace Assault Team.Text: The Greenpeace Assault Team will destroy 1 Wyrm caern at the end of each Combat Phase.

Guardian Spider.Name: Guardian Spider
Guardian Spider.Text: These spirits are creatures of the Weaver. They are fiercely protective of their space in the Umbra.

Hapless Villagers.Name: Hapless Villagers
Hapless Villagers.Text: Although the Garou fight valiantly, it is villagers like these who pay the ultimate price for Pentex's greed.

Happy Tourists.Name: Happy Tourists
Happy Tourists.Text: Happy Tourists are always so unsuspecting.

High School Athlete.Name: High School Athlete
High School Athlete.Text: This victim gains 1 Rage for every additional High School Athlete in the Hunting Grounds.

Hogling.Name: Hogling
Hogling.Text: A spirit elemental of smog infests the area. Hoglings may use any Metis Gifts and are unaffected by non-fetish Equipment attacks.

Honest Senator.Name: Honest Senator
Honest Senator.Text: Human. This Kinfolk uses his political power to tie up minions of the Wyrm in endless investigations. Vampires in battleform can take no actions other than play combat cards if attacked and recruit Allies while the Senator is in play.
Honest Senator.Errata: Original: Members of the 7th Generation can take no actions (other than to Regenerate or defend themselves) while the Senator is in 'office.'

Incarna Avatar.Name: Incarna Avatar
Incarna Avatar.Text: The Incarna exists only in the Umbra. The Incarna can use any Gifts of any Gnosis and is unaffected by other Gifts.

Inquisitor.Name: Inquisitor
Inquisitor.Text: The Inquisitor attacks the lowest Renown character for two rounds at the end of the combat phase, including Wyrm characters. Alphas attacking the Inquisitor must withdraw after two rounds even if frenzied as they are driven back by his faith. If attacked,

Jannok.Name: Jannok
Jannok.Text: These Wyrm spirits are ferocious hunters and often travel in groups. For every additional Jannok in the Hunting Grounds, raise this enemy's Rage by 1.

Liberal Pop Singer.Name: Liberal Pop Singer
Liberal Pop Singer.Text: The Liberal Pop Singer counteracts the effects of 1 Mass Pollution Card while he is in play.

Lost Cub.Name: Lost Cub
Lost Cub.Text: If the Lost Cub survives for 2 turns in the Hunting Grounds, he becomes an ally of a Gaia pack. If there is more than 1 Gaia pack in play the Lost Cub will join the pack with the lowest remaining Renown. The Lost Cub is considered to be in Lupus form.
Lost Cub.Errata: When determining who has the least remaining renown, you count all packmembers, including Allies and creatures temporarily out of play."

Mage of the Celestial Chorus.Name: Mage of the Celestial Chorus
Mage of the Celestial Chorus.Text: The mage can use ANY Gifts. Being in the business of saving lives, the Mage will remove the lowest Renown victim from play at the end of any turn the Mage is in play. The Mage will never remove herself from play.
Mage of the Celestial Chorus.Errata: If there is more than one Mage of the celestial Chorus in play, they will not remove each other."

Mail Man.Name: Mail Man
Mail Man.Text: Lupus characters play Combat Actions against the Mail Man at +1 Rage.

Marauder.Name: Marauder
Marauder.Text: Unique. The Marauder can use any Gifts. While the Marauder is in combat, players not involved in the combat may play Gifts out of their discard pile for the Marauder as if they were in their sept hand.  Gifts played from a discard pile are removed from th

Metis Scapegoat.Name: Metis Scapegoat
Metis Scapegoat.Text: Ronin Garou. It can use Metis and Ahroun Gifts.

Morozhki.Name: Morozhki
Morozhki.Text: These frost spirits are often Wyrm-tainted. If the Morozhki is attacked in the physical world, combat cards played on its behalf are aggravated.
Morozhki.Errata: Morozhki deals aggravated damage to creatures in the physical world. This includes creatures that are in both the physical world and Umbra at the same time.

Movie Star.Name: Movie Star
Movie Star.Text: Heavily guarded, the Movie Star's bodyguards allow up to 3 Combat Actions to be played on her behalf.

Neighborhood Watch Group.Name: Neighborhood Watch Group
Neighborhood Watch Group.Text: No victims other than the Watch Group can be attacked until the Watch Group (or Groups) is destroyed.
Neighborhood Watch Group.Errata: CLARIFICATION: The ""watch Group"" in the text refers to the Neighborhood Watch Group. 10/24/2015"

Newspaper Vendor.Name: Newspaper Vendor
Newspaper Vendor.Text: They seem so unassuming, but can be quite vicious if threatened.

Nexus Crawler.Name: Nexus Crawler
Nexus Crawler.Text: Nexus Crawlers bend reality and are one of the Garou's greatest foes in the Umbra. When fighting a Nexus Crawler, characters play their combat cards randomly. Although it is a Wyrm spirit, the Nexus Crawler cannot be bound.
Nexus Crawler.Errata: Creatures facing a Nexus Crawler play Combat actions randomly. See rules on Forced Play and Random Play. Reversal: you may use combat events as normal while facing a Nexus Crawler

Nightmaster.Name: Nightmaster
Nightmaster.Text: Any characters who fight the Nightmaster and retreat or escape will lose their highest Gnosis fetish (if any). Place all lost fetishes under the Nightmaster. A character who kills the Nightmaster will get all of the fetishes for the duration of the game.

Nocturna.Name: Nocturna
Nocturna.Text: This Bane spirit attacks humans as they sleep, corrupting their dreams. At the end of each Combat Phase, the Nocturna will corrupt one Kinfolk ally in play (decide randomly if there is more than one Kinfolk ally). The Kinfolk is then moved to the Hunting

Outcast Bastet.Name: Outcast Bastet
Outcast Bastet.Text: Outcast from her tribe, this Bastet now lives on the run. After three turns in the Hunting Grounds, she will join a Gaia pack, selected at random, as an ally. If the pack she intends to join has Bastet characters in it, remove this victim from play. The O

Paradise.Name: Paradise
Paradise.Text: Unique. Paradise is a Metis Garou cub. If someone steps in to defend Paradise and survives, Paradise will join that creature's pack as an Ally. A Gaia pack gains 1 Victory Point as long as Paradise is its Ally.

Pattern Spider.Name: Pattern Spider
Pattern Spider.Text: For every Pattern Spider in play raise the Gauntlet of all Caerns by 1. Pattern Spiders are spirits that exist only in the Umbra.

Pentex First Team 43.Name: Pentex First Team 43
Pentex First Team 43.Text: First Team 43 is efficient and effective. They may play 2 Combat Actions per round of combat. If an attacker chooses to break off combat before killing the First Team, they will take a randomly selected piece of non-Fetish Equipment from him. The pack whi

Pentex Forestry Team.Name: Pentex Forestry Team
Pentex Forestry Team.Text: The Pentex Forestry Team will automatically destroy 1 caern in play each Regeneration Phase.  If there is more than 1 caern in play, decide randomly which caern is destroyed. The Forestry Team can play 2 combat cards per round.

Pentex Patrol.Name: Pentex Patrol
Pentex Patrol.Text: No Wyrm-defended Battlefield may be engaged while a Pentex Patrol is in play.
Pentex Patrol.Errata: No Battlefield which has ""Wyrm"" anywhere in defender text can be attacked. 11/30/15"

Pentex Refinery.Name: Pentex Refinery
Pentex Refinery.Text: A Pentex Refinery has set up shop in the area, infesting the region with Wyrm taint.  Until razed, the refinery prevents any shapechanger (Garou or otherwise) from regenerating, although Gifts like Mother's Touch still function as normal.
Pentex Refinery.Errata: Reversal: The Refinery's ability does NOT cross the Gauntlet.

Pentex Strip Miners.Name: Pentex Strip Miners
Pentex Strip Miners.Text: All Gaia characters are at -1 Gnosis while the Pentex Strip Miners are in play.

Pentex Supply Lines.Name: Pentex Supply Lines
Pentex Supply Lines.Text: While in play, Pentex characters and allies can regenerate during the Regeneration Phase.
Pentex Supply Lines.Errata: Pentex characters and Allies are those with the keyword Pentex. 11/30/15

Phantasmi.Name: Phantasmi
Phantasmi.Text: These spirits are the remnants of dead Fomori. The Phantasmi cannot be bound.

Plague Vermin.Name: Plague Vermin
Plague Vermin.Text: When you play Plague Vermin, you may search your deck for all copies of Plague Vermin and play them. The Rage and Health of the Plague Vermin is equal to the number of Plague Vermin in play. Ratkin Characters may recruit Plague Vermin (from your hand) as

Playground Full of Kids.Name: Playground Full of Kids
Playground Full of Kids.Text: Combat with the Playground will never (ever) last longer than 3 rounds. These kids, they scare easy.
Playground Full of Kids.Errata: The kids ESCAPE after the third. 10/24/2015

Pooka Childling.Name: Pooka Childling
Pooka Childling.Text: When Pooka Childling is attacked, decide randomly whether it escapes combat before the first round. The Pooka Childling can use Children of Gaia Gifts.

Priest.Name: Priest
Priest.Text: Human. The Priest removes the lowest Gnosis Bane Ally or Bane Enemy from play at the start of the Regeneration Phase. Bane Allies cannot declare attacks on the Priest or target it with sept cards or special abilities. Bane Allies cannot join pack attacks
Priest.Errata: Original: The Priest will remove the lowest Gnosis Bane from play during each Regeneration Phase. Banes cannot act against the Priest in any way. The Priest can use any Children of Gaia Gifts.

Progenitor Mage.Name: Progenitor Mage
Progenitor Mage.Text: A mage has entered the area and will directly attack the character with the highest Gnosis for 3 rounds at the end of each Combat Phase. The mage can use any Gifts. Furthermore, the mage is not affected by anyone else's Gifts.

Psychomachiae.Name: Psychomachiae
Psychomachiae.Text: These malevolent Bane spirits feed on pain and death. For every kill the Psychomachiae makes, it gains +1 Rage and +1 health. Place creatures killed by it under the card to note the stat increase. It may use Beast-of-War Gifts.

Pumpkin Man.Name: Pumpkin Man
Pumpkin Man.Text: Characters attacking the Pumpkin Man can retreat only after all their combat action cards have been played.  The Pumpkin Man can use any Theurge or Philodox Gifts.
Pumpkin Man.Errata: Errata: You can't withdraw until you've played your bluffs as well.  Allies can't withdraw against Pumpkin Man either.

Pup Pid.Name: Pup Pid
Pup Pid.Text: A dread revenant of unknown origin, Pup Pid is a soulless killer. He may use Homid and Theurge Gifts.

Ravnos Vampire.Name: Ravnos Vampire
Ravnos Vampire.Text: While the Ravnos Vampire is in play, no Faeries may use their special abilities or Gifts. The Ravnos may use 7th Generation and Ragabash Gifts.

Red Cap Raiding Party.Name: Red Cap Raiding Party
Red Cap Raiding Party.Text: The Redcaps can play two combat actions per round and Get of Fenris Gifts. They are in Crinos form. At the end of the combat phase, the Redcaps attack the creature that killed the lowest renown Faerie. They will attack Gaia packs who receive renown for ki
Red Cap Raiding Party.Errata: It will only attack Characters and Allies.

Reject.Name: Reject
Reject.Text: These Wyld spirits are always unpredictable. At the beginning of every Combat Phase, when the Reject is in play, randomly select one bound spirit in play and return it to the Hunting Grounds.

Renegade Werewolf Hunter.Name: Renegade Werewolf Hunter
Renegade Werewolf Hunter.Text: All damage from the Werewolf Hunter is aggravated. At the end of each Combat Phase, the Werewolf Hunter attacks the Black Spiral Dancer with the highest Renown for 3 rounds.

Ronin Garou.Name: Ronin Garou
Ronin Garou.Text: The Ronin can use Homid and Philodox Gifts.

Sabbat Fiend.Name: Sabbat Fiend
Sabbat Fiend.Text: The Sabbat Fiend is an ancient, powerful, and deadly vampire. His attacks do Aggravated Damage, and he may use 7th Generation, Fomori, Glass Walkers and Shadow Lords Gifts.

Samuel Haight.Name: Samuel Haight
Samuel Haight.Text: Samuel Haight, the Skindancer, is in the area. Samuel Haight is considered to be in Homid form, and may use Gifts of any breed or auspice. Only 1 Samuel Haight can be played per game.

Scarecrow.Name: Scarecrow
Scarecrow.Text: Scarecrow serves the Defiler by tainting the innocent. While Scarecrow is in play, all Victims are considered Enemies, though if any Victim-turned-Enemy attacks a member of a Wyrm pack, that pack still gains victory points. Only 1 Scarecrow may be played

Self-Serving Giovanni.Name: Self-Serving Giovanni
Self-Serving Giovanni.Text: This vampire will do anything within his power to further his own agenda. He may use Wyrm Gifts of any aspect.

Serpentine.Name: Serpentine
Serpentine.Text: The Garou often bind these animal spirits into service. Serpentine can use any Galliard Gifts.

Sidhe Knight.Name: Sidhe Knight
Sidhe Knight.Text: At the end of each Combat Phase, this fey spirit attacks the highest Renown Wyrm character for 3 rounds. The Knight can use any Ragabash Gifts or Ragabash Actions.
Sidhe Knight.Errata: CLARIFICATION: The Sidhe Knight may use Ragabash Actions even though it is a victim. 10/24/2015

Silhouette.Name: Silhouette
Silhouette.Text: Silhouette is a deadly assassin for Pentex.  Attacks from Silhouette cannot be blocked, dodged, or evaded.  Silhouette attacks the character with the highest Renown for 2 combat rounds at the end of each Combat Phase.  Only 1 Silhouette can be played per game.

Skull Pig.Name: Skull Pig
Skull Pig.Text: Skull Pigs wander through graveyards and eat the corpses of Wyrm-tainted humans to replenish their ghastly might. They will automatically pack defend with all other Skull Pigs in the Hunting Grounds.

Sower of Thunder.Name: Sower of Thunder
Sower of Thunder.Text: Sower-of-Thunder is a Wyrm-tainted Ronin Red Talons who is now horribly twisted and mutated by the Wyrm. He can play 2 combat cards per round and use any Ahroun or Red Talons Gift.

Street Bum.Name: Street Bum
Street Bum.Text: While in play, the Street Bum counteracts 1 Mass Pollution card in play.

Suburban High School Kid.Name: Suburban High School Kid
Suburban High School Kid.Text: Suburban High School Kids will automatically pack defend with each other and with any High School Athletes when attacked.
Suburban High School Kid.Errata: This creature pack defends with the High School Athlete when Athlete is attacked, but the Athlete won't defend with the SHSK if it is attacked."

Suicide Fomori Team.Name: Suicide Fomori Team
Suicide Fomori Team.Text: Attackers may not freely break off combat with the Suicide Fomori Team. If combat with this team lasts past 3 rounds, these Fomori explode, doing 2 aggravated damage to the highest Renown opponent in combat against them. These Fomori are worth no victory
Suicide Fomori Team.Errata: Creatures cannot withdraw or escape from combat with the Suicide Fomori Team. 12/5/2015

Survival Nut.Name: Survival Nut
Survival Nut.Text: The Survival Nut attacks the highest Renown Pentex character for 4 rounds at the end of each Combat Phase.

Swat Officer.Name: Swat Officer
Swat Officer.Text: Police. The SWAT Officer is armed with a 9mm Semi-Auto Pistol. If disarmed, he becomes Rage 2. Attacks from the SWAT Officer cannot be blocked.

Th' M'Gregors.Name: Th' M'Gregors
Th' M'Gregors.Text: The MacGregors are an inbred degenerate family of Fomori. They may play 2 Combat Actions per round. They will not pack defend with lone Fomori nor other MacGregors families.

The Piper.Name: The Piper
The Piper.Text: When the Piper is in play, all Garou act at -1 Rage and -1 Gnosis. Any Garou who has less than 1 Rage or Gnosis cannot act until the Piper is removed from play.
The Piper.Errata: Errata: Unique. See ""What is an action?"" rules. (Revised 2014)"

Toreador Poseur.Name: Toreador Poseur
Toreador Poseur.Text: You may not voluntarily withdraw from combat with the Toreador. If combat continues for more than three rounds with the Toreador and it is unwounded, the attacker cannot be alpha for the next two turns. The Toreador may use Homid Gifts.

Trash Scrag.Name: Trash Scrag
Trash Scrag.Text: These foul spirits feed on filth.  Creatures attacking the Trash Scrag must play their highest Rage Combat Action during the first round of combat, even if it is a bluff.

Twilight Operative.Name: Twilight Operative
Twilight Operative.Text: The Twilight Operative may play 2 Combat Actions as long as they are both Rage 2 or below. At the end of the Combat Phase, the Twilight Operative will attack the lowest-Renown Garou in the game, including Black Spiral Dancers. Wyrm packs gain victory poin

Uktena Wyrmfoe.Name: Uktena Wyrmfoe
Uktena Wyrmfoe.Text: A Bane spirit enters the Hunting Grounds. No Uktena may attack anything except the Bane spirit until it is killed.
Uktena Wyrmfoe.Errata: Errata: Uktena have a Rivalry with the Uktena Wyrmfoe. (2014)

Unlucky Lune.Name: Unlucky Lune
Unlucky Lune.Text: A Lune is a fragment of the great Incarna Luna. A Lune can use any Auspice Gifts. If a Full Moon is in play, the Lune's Rage becomes 6.

Unwitting Ghoul.Name: Unwitting Ghoul
Unwitting Ghoul.Text: The Ghoul is a servant to a vampire. While still mortal, he has a small quantity of his master's blood in his veins. He will pack defend with any vampire enemy in play.

Vagabond Skindancer.Name: Vagabond Skindancer
Vagabond Skindancer.Text: The Vagabond Skindancer has become a werewolf by wearing skins of Garou stolen from their original owners. He may use Homid and Ahroun Gifts.

Vigilante.Name: Vigilante
Vigilante.Text: At the end of the Combat Phase the Vigilante will automatically attack whoever killed the lowest Renown victim for 2 rounds. Decide randomly in the event of a tie.

Voracious Spectre.Name: Voracious Spectre
Voracious Spectre.Text: The Spectre is a spirit that exists only in the Umbra. If there are any Silent Striders in the Umbra during the Combat Phase, the Spectre will attack the 1 with the highest Gnosis at the end of the Combat Phase.

Wandering Gaffling.Name: Wandering Gaffling
Wandering Gaffling.Text: This Spirit can use Lupus and Philodox Gifts.

Wild Animals.Name: Wild Animals
Wild Animals.Text: Guided by Gaia herself, this pack of jungle creatures will attack the highest Rage Wyrm character at the end of each Combat Phase.

Wyldkin Kami.Name: Wyldkin Kami
Wyldkin Kami.Text: Manifest spirit servants of Gaia, the kami are increasingly rare these days. Wyldkin Kami may use breed and auspice Gifts and may play 2 Combat Actions per round of combat.

Wyldling.Name: Wyldling
Wyldling.Text: Wyldings are unpredictable and dangerous spirits. A Wyldling exists only in the Umbra.

Yunwi Amai'yine'hi.Name: Yunwi Amai'yine'hi
Yunwi Amai'yine'hi.Text: The Yunwi Amai'yine'hi is a Native American Faerie. She may use Fianna and Galliard Gifts, and she automatically escapes from any combat after the third round.

Zmei.Name: Zmei
Zmei.Text: These ancient dragon-serpents were summoned by the Baba Yaga in Russia. Zmei may play 3 Combat Actions per round, is not affected by any Gifts, may never lose the ability to play Combat Actions, and he is considered to be in Crinos form for the purposes o
Zmei.Errata: Cannot be affected by Gift or things that cause him to lose the ability to play Combat Actions or that cancel Combat Actions. It can be affected by things that reduce/redirect damage such as Surprise Attack, Beat Unmerciful, Passive Aggression, and Redire"

Dream Hunter.Name: Dream Hunter
Dream Hunter.Text: Spirit.  This spirit memory disguises itself as a harmless animal.  It exists only in the Umbra and can use Mokole and Get of Fenris Gifts.  If a Mokole kills this creature, he may search his deck for a Quest, Rite or Junta and put it in his hand; shuffle

Okavango Mists.Name: Okavango Mists
Okavango Mists.Text: Unique. During the rainy season, these Wyld spirits gather over the Okavango delta, charging the very air with raw spiritual energy.  While Okovango Mists is in the Hunting Ground, Victims gain 2 Gnosis. It can use Black Fury and Wendigo gifts.

Blightscape.Name: Blightscape
Blightscape.Text: The minions of the Wyrm are cunning and adaptable, and the Blightscape seems to pop up again even after it has been destroyed. The Blightscape may never be removed from play unless the controlling player chooses to discard it. Only 1 Blightscape may be in

Caern of Awakening.Name: Caern of Awakening
Caern of Awakening.Text: Immediately after any Redraw Phase, you may choose to discard all of your sept cards and redraw five more.  Only one Caern of Awakening can be in play at any time.

Caern of Bygone Visions.Name: Caern of Bygone Visions
Caern of Bygone Visions.Text: Every other turn during your Moot Phase, you may sort through your sept deck and equip one of your characters with a fetish Equipment card.  The equipped characters must still meet the Gnosis requirements of the fetish. Reshuffle your sept deck each time
Caern of Bygone Visions.Errata: Can be used to fetch Bane Fetishes.

Caern of Ichiyo Modoribashi.Name: Caern of Ichiyo Modoribashi
Caern of Ichiyo Modoribashi.Text: Once in play this caern cannot be removed from the controlling pack or destroyed in any way.  Only one Caern of Ichiyo Modoribashi can be in play at any time.
Caern of Ichiyo Modoribashi.Errata: Rite of Claiming cannot take this Caern, even if the defender is killed."

Caern of Rytthiku.Name: Caern of Rytthiku
Caern of Rytthiku.Text: Your pack may now attack enemies (in addition to victims) in the Hunting Grounds for victory points.
Caern of Rytthiku.Errata: CLARIFICATION: You earn Victory Points for Enemies your pack kills.

Caern of the Bloodfist.Name: Caern of the Bloodfist
Caern of the Bloodfist.Text: Members of a pack controlling this caern are at +2 Health when in the Umbra.  Only one Caern of the Bloodfist can be in play at any time.

Caern of the Blood God.Name: Caern of the Blood God
Caern of the Blood God.Text: You may discard any kill from your victory pile to allow one of your characters in combat to frenzy. You do not draw a frenzy hand equal to the size of your character's Rage. Instead your frenzy hand equals the Renown of the discarded kill.

Caern of the Crescent Moon.Name: Caern of the Crescent Moon
Caern of the Crescent Moon.Text: You may choose one pack member and double her Renown during any Moot Phase.  A character whose Renown is doubled this way may not act as alpha during the following Combat Phase.  Only one Caern of the Crescent Moon can be in play at any time.
Caern of the Crescent Moon.Errata: CLARIFICATION: doubling uses base renown, before other effects are applied."

Caern of the Painted Sands.Name: Caern of the Painted Sands
Caern of the Painted Sands.Text: You may play any Pack Totem card even if your pack does not meet the specified requirements.  Your pack may have more than one Totem.  Only one Caern of the Painted Sands can be in play at any time.
Caern of the Painted Sands.Errata: You may only play one copy of each totem. If the caern is destroyed, discard all but one Totem."

Caern of the Snow Leopard.Name: Caern of the Snow Leopard
Caern of the Snow Leopard.Text: When one of your characters is killed in the Umbra, you may choose to discard this caern and return the character to full health in the physical world.  Only one Caern of the Snow Leopard can be in play at any time.
Caern of the Snow Leopard.Errata: When the ability is used, combat ends. Clarification: The Character is not considered to have died."

Caern of the Tri-Spiral.Name: Caern of the Tri-Spiral
Caern of the Tri-Spiral.Text: All members of the pack that controls this caern gain 2 Gnosis for the purposes of using Gifts.  Only one Caern of the Tri-Spiral can be in play at any time.

Caern of the Unwashed Child.Name: Caern of the Unwashed Child
Caern of the Unwashed Child.Text: Opponents facing your pack lose either 2 Gnosis or 2 Rage for the duration of the combat (caern holder chooses which). This caern can never reduce an opponent's Rage or Gnosis below 1.
Caern of the Unwashed Child.Errata: Clarification: This effect is used in the Precombat step. 10/24/2015

Caern of the Waking Dream.Name: Caern of the Waking Dream
Caern of the Waking Dream.Text: Members of your pack gain the full victory points for any spirits the bind.  Only one Caern of the Waking Dream can be in play at any time.

Caern of the Weeping Daughter.Name: Caern of the Weeping Daughter
Caern of the Weeping Daughter.Text: When members of the pack controlling this caern are attacked, the attackers cannot frenzy and must play their combat actions at -1 Rage.  Only one Caern of the Weeping Daughter can be in play at any time.

Caern of the Western Eye.Name: Caern of the Western Eye
Caern of the Western Eye.Text: Any Kinfolk allies of the pack controlling this caern can now vote in moots.  Each ally has additional votes equal to his Renown.  Only one Caern of the Western Eye can be in play at any time.

The Council for Universal Trade.Name: The Council for Universal Trade
The Council for Universal Trade.Text: The Gauntlet of this caern can never be 'fluxed' higher than 6 or lower than 4.  This caern is unaffected by Pattern Spiders.  Only one Council for Universal Trade can be in play at any time.

Fist of the Comet.Name: Fist of the Comet
Fist of the Comet.Requires: Any Gaia Garou in the Umbra, or one who has been in the Umbra
Fist of the Comet.Text: The Fist of the Comet is dedicated to making the Umbra safe for travelers. Members of the pack controlling this Caern earn +1 victory points for Enemies they kill in the Umbra. Only 1 Fist of the Comet may be in play at any time.

Hell's Hand Hive.Name: Hell's Hand Hive
Hell's Hand Hive.Text: All Black Spiral Dancers can automatically pack defend (no additional cards are drawn). Only one Hell's Hand Hive can be in play at any time.

Hollow Heart Caern.Name: Hollow Heart Caern
Hollow Heart Caern.Text: Pack members can never be forced to lose Gnosis. Only one Hollow Heart Caern can be in play at any time.

Operation Blight.Name: Operation Blight
Operation Blight.Text: Pack members who cannot step sideways, can now step sideways. Only one Operation Blight can be in play at any time.
Operation Blight.Errata: This will allow Characters to use Umbral Quest that could not otherwise do so.

Sept of Gold.Name: Sept of Gold
Sept of Gold.Text: A pack controlling the Sept of Gold will never lose its Redraw or Regeneration Phases. A pack can only have 1 Caern in play at a time. Only one Sept of Gold may be played per game.

Sept of the Five Winds.Name: Sept of the Five Winds
Sept of the Five Winds.Requires: Gaia Pack with no Homid breed characters
Sept of the Five Winds.Text: The Sept of the Five Winds is a caern of War, dedicated to reinstating the Impergium. A pack with the Sept of the Five Winds may automatically pack attack or defend with up to 15 Renown worth of characters (including the alpha) against any Homid-form char

Sky River Caern.Name: Sky River Caern
Sky River Caern.Text: Non-alpha members of your pack cannot be challenged or sneak attacked. Only one Sky River Caern can be in play at any time.
Sky River Caern.Errata: It does not protect Allies against the Entrapment Quest.

The Under Barrows.Name: The Under Barrows
The Under Barrows.Text: Once per Equip/Ally Phase, a pack controlling the Under-Barrows may search through their sept deck and bring any faerie Ally into play. Only 1 Under-Barrows may be in play at any time.

Trinity Hive Caern.Name: Trinity Hive Caern
Trinity Hive Caern.Text: Any Black Spiral Dancers in your pack now do aggravated damage. However, these Black Spiral Dancers can only regenerate in the Umbra. Only 1 Trinity Hive Caern can be in play at any time.

The Wheel of Ptah.Name: The Wheel of Ptah
The Wheel of Ptah.Text: The controller of this caern can always choose which Moon Bridges affect her caern and which do not. At her discretion, other packs can use her caern to open Moon Bridges or step sideways into the Umbra. Only one Wheel of Ptah can be in play at any time.
The Wheel of Ptah.Errata: Wheel of Ptah can choose an alpha in another pack with a caern and any other pack with a caern. The alpha can attack any member of the other pack as an alpha action[; this attack can cross the Gauntlet]. This effect can be used any number of times per tur

Caern of White Water.Name: Caern of White Water
Caern of White Water.Text: The Fianna helped fend of an attack on this Uktena caern many years ago and have helped defend it ever since. Your Garou are +1 Gnosis while in the Umbra. They may play Umbra only Combat actions while in the real world.

Caern of the Sentinel.Name: Caern of the Sentinel
Caern of the Sentinel.Text: Unique.  This hotly contested caern is hidden deep in the Adirondack mountains.  Whenever a Victim is attacked by a member of another pack, draw three combat cards and one of your pack may change to Crinos form.

Dank Well Hive.Name: Dank Well Hive
Dank Well Hive.Text: Unique. This subterranean caern encourages unholy alliances between the Spirals and some of the less savory faeries. You may bind Faerie Allies and Prey as if they were spirits. You do not need to be in the Umbra to use this ability. Your Faerie Allies ar

Hive of the Jagged Maw.Name: Hive of the Jagged Maw
Hive of the Jagged Maw.Text: You may reveal a card from your sept hand. Each other player must discard one card of that type from either their hand or from play.  If they cannot, they discard the top 3 cards of their sept deck. This ability may not be used during combat and cannot be

Hive of the Dark Mother.Name: Hive of the Dark Mother
Hive of the Dark Mother.Text: The totem of this Caern takes good care of her Spirit children, no matter how foul and twisted they might be...  All your Spirit Allies gain the ability to regenerate and may regenerate aggravated damage.

Lake Nasser Wallow.Name: Lake Nasser Wallow
Lake Nasser Wallow.Text: The Caern lies beneath a deep artificial lake and is tied to a small Egyptian realm.  Rites and Gifts played by your pack may cross the Gauntlet.

.38 Special.Name: .38 Special
.38 Special.Text: Weapon. Firearm. Only usable by creatures in Homid form. This Firearm allows this creature to play combat cards of Rage 3 or less. .38 Special does not count as a Weapon for purposes of determining how many weapons a creature may have equipped. You may ha
.38 Special.Errata: Original: Only usable by characters in Homid form.  Allows the character to use up to Rage 3 combat cards.

9mm Semi-Auto Pistol.Name: 9mm Semi-Auto Pistol
9mm Semi-Auto Pistol.Text: Weapon. Firearm. Only usable by characters in Homid form.  Allows the character to use up to Rage 5 combat cards.

Anubis Stone.Name: Anubis Stone
Anubis Stone.Text: The owner of the Anubis Stone may draw up to her Renown worth of additional packmates into pack attacks. Vampires act at -2 Rage against the bearer of the Anubis Stone and her pack. Only 1 Anubis Stone may be played per game.
Anubis Stone.Errata: The user may attack with a total of 10 renown of Characters, not 10 Characters."

Bane Arrow.Name: Bane Arrow
Bane Arrow.Text: Weapon. During the Withdrawal step the owner of the Bane Arrow may discard Bane Arrow to do 3 damage to an Enemy she is in combat with. Use the Bane Arrow to mark this damage. If the Enemy is killed during this round, it is worth no victory points and is
Bane Arrow.Errata: Is not a combat action. It may not be blocked or dodged. Original: The Bane Arrow does 3 damage to any Wyrm creature (any enemy card) you are in combat with.  This does not count as a combat action and can be played in addition to any combat cards.  Once

Bane Sword.Name: Bane Sword
Bane Sword.Text: If the equipped character's Gnosis exceeds her opponent's, all damage done by the Bane Sword is aggravated.

Bivouac.Name: Bivouac
Bivouac.Text: The equipped character may heal 1 additional damage card per Regeneration Phase.
Bivouac.Errata: The healed card happens in regeneration (closed) after normal regeneration. It can heal aggravated damage 11/30/15

Blood Dagger.Name: Blood Dagger
Blood Dagger.Text: Weapon. A character equipped with the Blood Dagger acts at +1 Rage.

Blood Diamond.Name: Blood Diamond
Blood Diamond.Text: Diamonds mined with slave labor are used to purchase influence all over Africa. A character equipped with Blood Diamonds has 2 votes he can cast in every Junta, even those he could not normally vote in.

Bones of Shakir Hind.Name: Bones of Shakir Hind
Bones of Shakir Hind.Text: The owner may command any 1 enemy in the Hunting Grounds to fight in her place. However, the owner gains no Renown for any kills made, and any character may attack the owner as if she were in the Hunting Grounds. Only 1 Bones of Shakir Hind may be played per game.
Bones of Shakir Hind.Errata: Clarification: The owner of the Bones of Shakir Hind redirects an attack from himself to an enemy of his choice before combat begins. The owner of Bones may not decline challenges. (revised 2014)

Bottlecap of Shakey Mac.Name: Bottlecap of Shakey Mac
Bottlecap of Shakey Mac.Text: Once per turn, the owner can select 1 spirit in play and use it as an ally for the remainder of the turn.  Only 1 Bottlecap of Shakey Mac can be played per game.
Bottlecap of Shakey Mac.Errata: Clarification: Bottlecap can be used on spirit Allies, Enemies, and Victims. It will not work on spirits that cannot be bound."

Bureaucratic Blueprints.Name: Bureaucratic Blueprints
Bureaucratic Blueprints.Text: The character or Ally equipped with Bureaucratic Blueprints may discard them and immediately attack any Territory in play. This may be done outside of the Combat Phase. The owner of the target Territory may select which of his pack members will defend the
Bureaucratic Blueprints.Errata: The target selects only one defender. Both sides may use pack actions to add additional creatures.

Cellular Phone.Name: Cellular Phone
Cellular Phone.Text: A character equipped with a Cellular Phone can pack attack or defend with any other pack member also equipped with a Cellular Phone. This pack action can begin once 2 rounds of combat have passed. Draw 2 additional cards once the pack action has begun.

Chainsaw.Name: Chainsaw
Chainsaw.Text: Weapon. Allows the character to play up to Rage 10 Combat Actions. The Chainsaw is discarded immediately after its user plays a Combat Action of Rage 6 or higher.

Chrome Plated Hand Cannon.Name: Chrome Plated Hand Cannon
Chrome Plated Hand Cannon.Text: Weapon. Firearm. This gun may not be used in animal form. The user may play combat actions of Rage 3 or less. The owner is +1 renown when voting on Juntas called by Homids or Humans.

Chronicle of the Black Labyrinth.Name: Chronicle of the Black Labyrinth
Chronicle of the Black Labyrinth.Text: This legendary tome of Wyrm lore inspires fealty or wrath in those who possess it. If a Wyrm character controls the Chronicle, Victims it kills are worth +1 victory points. If a Gaia character controls the Chronicle, they may draw an additional combat car

Churjuroc's Tusk.Name: Churjuroc's Tusk
Churjuroc's Tusk.Text: Armor. When a Full Moon is in play the character can activate this fetish during the equip/Ally Phase, allowing you to search through your deck and bring a Nexus Crawler directly into play as an ally. The Nexus Crawler will remain in play for the duration
Churjuroc's Tusk.Errata: The summoned Nexus Crawler may come from your hand. When the summoned Nexus Crawler leaves play permanently, so does the character that summoned it. Whatever has happened to the summoner, the Tusk or the Nexus Crawler (unless the summoner is in a Victory"

Concertina Wire.Name: Concertina Wire
Concertina Wire.Text: A character equipped with Concertina Wire may discard it when any alpha declares an attack against any of his pack's Territories. That Territory may not be attacked and the alpha must select another alpha action.
Concertina Wire.Errata: Concertina Wire only works against one Alpha, but will protect all your territories from the targetted Alpha for the rest of turn."

Conduit.Name: Conduit
Conduit.Text: This object provides a link with the dead. The owner is +2 Gnosis for the purpose of stepping sideways or using combat cards with a gnosis requirement. Any creature equipped with the Conduit can use any Caern in play to step sideways, even if it is of a c

Conquistador's Sword.Name: Conquistador's Sword
Conquistador's Sword.Text: This card can be discarded to remove the Dorado Realm or El Dorado from play at any time. There can never be more than 1 Conquistador's sword played per game. Only one Conquistador's Sword may be played per game.

Corporate Credit Card.Name: Corporate Credit Card
Corporate Credit Card.Text: This character can equip at the beginning of the Combat Phase right after alphas are chosen, instead of during the Equip/Ally Phase.

Crystal Knife.Name: Crystal Knife
Crystal Knife.Text: The user acts at +1 Gnosis. The Crystal Knife may be discarded to cancel the effects of any single Gift that targets its owner. The Crystal Knife cannot be stolen or traded.

Dark Moon's Femur.Name: Dark Moon's Femur
Dark Moon's Femur.Text: The leg bone of a powerful Uktena Theurge, Dark Moon's Femur allows its user to ignore card effects that would force her to lose an action, including playing Action cards. Only 1 Dark Moon's Femur may be played per game.

Devilwhip.Name: Devilwhip
Devilwhip.Text: Weapon. This gory fetish is made from the tentacle of a Bane. The user may play an additional damage-dealing combat action of Rage 2 or less per round. A Bane may play actions up to Rage 3. The extra combat action played in this manner may not be blocked.

Dhul Fiqar.Name: Dhul Fiqar
Dhul Fiqar.Text: Unique. Weapon. The user cannot be damaged by Rage 1 or less combat actions. If a Silent Striders equips Dhul Fiqar, he gains +1 Rage.

Doc Chaney's Power Goggles.Name: Doc Chaney's Power Goggles
Doc Chaney's Power Goggles.Text: A character equipped with Doc Chaney's Power Goggles can see the Gauntlet part just before someone steps through it. When equipped with the Goggles, a character may attack another character who returns to the physical world from the Umbra as an alpha acti
Doc Chaney's Power Goggles.Errata: If one Character steps from the Umbra to the physical world in a turn, a Character with the Goggles can be selected as alpha that turn and can use his alpha action to directly attack the first Character, even if the first Character is not an alpha this tu"

Dr. Mordecai's Home Chemistry Set.Name: Dr. Mordecai's Home Chemistry Set
Dr. Mordecai's Home Chemistry Set.Text: The equipped character gains either 1 Rage, 1 Gnosis, or 1 Health for the duration of each turn. This bonus can be chosen or reassigned during each Equip/Ally Phase.

Elder Stone.Name: Elder Stone
Elder Stone.Text: The owner of this stone gains an additional 3 votes during any moot.  The owner can discard this stone during a moot and gain an additional 10 votes.  A character cannot have more than 1 Elder Stone at a time.

Enticer Cosmetics.Name: Enticer Cosmetics
Enticer Cosmetics.Text: Creatures with less than 7 Gnosis may not attack the owner of this Bane Fetish.
