Experimental Cybernetics.Name: Experimental Cybernetics
Experimental Cybernetics.Text: Only equippable by Wyrm creatures and Glass Walkers. The equipped character gains 2 Rage and 1 Health, but loses 2 Gnosis.

Eye of Luna.Name: Eye of Luna
Eye of Luna.Text: The Eyes of Luna are sacred stones that contain the residual power of spirits. The bearer of an Eye of Luna gains +1 Gnosis for each Eye in play. An individual may only have 1 Eye of Luna at any time.

Faerie Armor.Name: Faerie Armor
Faerie Armor.Text: Armor. This mystical armor protects its wearer from Rage 2 and 3 damage cards. Faerie Armor can be worn in any breed or form. A character can only have one Faerie Armor at a time.
Faerie Armor.Errata: Faerie Armor reduces the damage of Rage 2 and Rage 3 cards to zero. Otherwise the combat cards work as normal.

Fang Dagger.Name: Fang Dagger
Fang Dagger.Text: Weapon. The Fang Dagger allows its user to play 2 damage cards per round of combat.
Fang Dagger.Errata: Clarification: You can always play two combat cards with a Fang Dagger. Any that are not attacks (Combat Actions with a damage stat) are illegal. (2014)

Fang Necklace of Fenris.Name: Fang Necklace of Fenris
Fang Necklace of Fenris.Text: The Garou can freely change forms. The owner gains 2 Rage. Only 1 Fang Necklace of Fenris can be played per game.

Feather of the Phoenix.Name: Feather of the Phoenix
Feather of the Phoenix.Text: Spirits cannot attack the owner of this fetish.

Incarna Sigil.Name: Incarna Sigil
Incarna Sigil.Text: The owner of this fetish can freely interact with anything in the Umbra.  This includes using Gifts, attacking, or swapping Equipment.

Klaive.Name: Klaive
Klaive.Text: Weapon. A character equipped with a Klaive loses 1 point of Gnosis for as long as she is so equipped.  All damaged done by the character who owns the Klaive is aggravated.

Lander's Nylon Stocking.Name: Lander's Nylon Stocking
Lander's Nylon Stocking.Text: The character's frenzies cannot be canceled. Only 1 Lander's Stocking can be played per game.

Lion's Pelt.Name: Lion's Pelt
Lion's Pelt.Text: The character can decline ANY attack or challenge - even those which cannot normally be declined. If a character is equipped with the Lion's Pelt for more than 2 consecutive turns, discard the Pelt and move the character into the Hunting Grounds as an ene
Lion's Pelt.Errata: At the start of the Umbra phase, check to see if the character with the Lion's Pelt was equipped with the Lion's Pelt during the last two Resource phases. If so, discard the Pelt and move the character into the Hunting Grounds as an Enemy. 7/3/2015"

Lost Map.Name: Lost Map
Lost Map.Text: The character can act as defending alpha for ANY Battlefield in play.

Luna's Links.Name: Luna's Links
Luna's Links.Text: This delicate necklace, forged from an unknown metal, allows the Garou to use Gifts from any auspice.

Machete.Name: Machete
Machete.Text: Weapon. Only usable in Homid form. The equipped character plays Combat Actions at +1 Rage.

Mage's Talisman.Name: Mage's Talisman
Mage's Talisman.Text: The Mage's Talisman allows its user to use any Gifts, whether of Gaia or the Wyrm (the character must still meet the Gnosis requirements of the Gift in Question).

Manling Pendant.Name: Manling Pendant
Manling Pendant.Text: The bearer of the Manling Pendant carries a reminder of man's injustice to womankind. She may discard the Pendant in a combat with a male opponent and immediately enter frenzy. This frenzy may not be canceled, nor may it be prevented by any circumstance (

Mantle of El Dorado.Name: Mantle of El Dorado
Mantle of El Dorado.Text: When equipped by a character, her pack may draw 3 additional sept cards during the Equip/Ally Phase. The Mantle may not be used on the turn it is brought into play. Only 1 Mantle of El Dorado can be in play at a time.
Mantle of El Dorado.Errata: Unique. You can draw three sept cards in the Umbra phase. The ability can be used once per turn. 12/6/15

Medicine Bag.Name: Medicine Bag
Medicine Bag.Text: The bearer of the Medicine Bag will immediately heal a damage-dealing Combat Action the instant that Combat Action is played. The Medicine Bag will only heal a wound that would kill the bearer and it is discarded afterward. This must be used to heal a fat

Memory Ribbon.Name: Memory Ribbon
Memory Ribbon.Text: A character wearing a Memory Ribbon cannot frenzy. Opponents facing him in combat also cannot frenzy.
Memory Ribbon.Errata: CLARIFICATION: Opponents may not frenzy, but the user's packmates may frenzy."

Mimi Stone.Name: Mimi Stone
Mimi Stone.Text: The spirit bound into this fetish warns its owner of impending danger so he is never caught unawares.  While the user is in combat, Combat Actions with the Fast Striking keyword are now slow Slow Striking instead.

Mockmaw's Battle Axe.Name: Mockmaw's Battle Axe
Mockmaw's Battle Axe.Text: Weapon. The character draws 1 additional combat card when defending in combat. Only 1 Mockmaw's Battle Axe can be played per game.

Mokole Hide.Name: Mokole Hide
Mokole Hide.Text: The owner of this fetish gains +2 Health.  A character cannot have more than 1 Mokole Hide at a time.

Moon Sign.Name: Moon Sign
Moon Sign.Text: A character equipped with this fetish can force any 1 Garou to revert to her breed form.  The Moon Sign is played in place of a combat card during a combat round.  Discard the Moon Sign after use.
Moon Sign.Errata: Clarification: See rules for Alternative Combat Actions. It may not be dodged, blocked, or redirected, but may be cancelled. You cannot use Moon Sign if you are prevented from playing combat actions."

Naturae Boon.Name: Naturae Boon
Naturae Boon.Text: Naturae Boon reduces the Gnosis cost of any Gifts the owner uses by 2.
Naturae Boon.Errata: Clarification: This does not change the Gnosis of the Gift itself for purposes of other effects like Lesser Banishment or Suit of Mirrors.

Nerve Agent.Name: Nerve Agent
Nerve Agent.Text: Play on 1 target in combat with the equipped character. That target is removed from combat for 1 round. Discard Nerve Agent upon use.

Nightmare Coin.Name: Nightmare Coin
Nightmare Coin.Text: Once equipped, a character can discard this item as a Combat Event to immediately end any one combat in which she is involved.
Nightmare Coin.Errata: CLARIFICATION: This Combat Event can be used in the Precombat step, Beginning of Combat step or Between-Rounds step."

Oil of Corruption.Name: Oil of Corruption
Oil of Corruption.Text: A character with Oil of Corruption can equip with any piece of fetish Equipment providing she can still meet the Gnosis requirements of the fetish.

Orb of The Umbra.Name: Orb of The Umbra
Orb of The Umbra.Text: This legendary orb affects Banes profoundly. Bane spirits and Bane characters may never declare an attack against the bearer of this Fetish. Only 1 Orb of the Umbra may be in play at any time.

Pager.Name: Pager
Pager.Text: A character equipped with a Pager cannot be removed from play or from his pack unless he allows himself to be removed or plays a card that requires him to be removed from play. The Pager does not function in the Umbra.

Pandora's Box.Name: Pandora's Box
Pandora's Box.Text: Named after the legendary Greek scapegoat, Pandora's Box stores troubles. At any time other than during the Combat Phase, the owner of this Fetish may 'trap' a spirit with less Gnosis than she inside, removing it from play. Thereafter, that spirit may be
Pandora's Box.Errata: Outside the combat phase, Pandora's Box can remove a spirit from combat."

Phoebe's Veil.Name: Phoebe's Veil
Phoebe's Veil.Text: Anyone with Phoebe's Veil may remove herself from any combat in which she is involved, as long as she has more Gnosis than her opponent. Anyone equipped with this Fetish may not use Gifts or special abilities for the turn after she uses the Veil.

Pine Cone.Name: Pine Cone
Pine Cone.Text: While equipped with the Pine Cone, the Red Talons's senses are honed to razor-sharpness, making it easy to track wounded prey. As her alpha action, she may attack any character currently suffering any Damage cards.

Pine Dagger.Name: Pine Dagger
Pine Dagger.Text: Weapon. Pine Daggers are imbued with power to disrupt Bane spirits. When a character with a Pine Dagger hits a Bane or Bane character, the dagger explodes within the Bane's being, doing 3 points of Damage. When the Pine Dagger is used, transfer it to the

Pipe Bomb.Name: Pipe Bomb
Pipe Bomb.Text: Weapon. The Pipe Bomb does 1 point of aggravated damage to any 1 opponent you are in combat with right before the first round of combat. A character may not use more than 1 Pipe Bomb per combat. Once used, the Pipe Bomb becomes a damage card.
Pipe Bomb.Errata: CLARIFICATION: using the pipe bomb is optional. It must be used in the beginning-of-combat step. 7/3/2015

Portable Computer.Name: Portable Computer
Portable Computer.Text: Can only be used by a Glass Walkers. A portable computer gives its user +2 Gnosis. The owner can discard the computer at any time to look at an opponent's hand.
Portable Computer.Errata: Clarification: When you discard the computer, you may look at one opponent's sept hand or combat hand, not both. (2014)"

Realm Key.Name: Realm Key
Realm Key.Text: Anyone equipped with a Realm Key may reduce the Gauntlet by 2 whenever they attempt to enter the Umbra.

Red Hot Baby Powder.Name: Red Hot Baby Powder
Red Hot Baby Powder.Text: Discard to send any target you're in combat with into frenzy.
Red Hot Baby Powder.Errata: Clarification: Can target any creature in combat other than the user. 7/3/2015

Rocket Launcher.Name: Rocket Launcher
Rocket Launcher.Text: Weapon. Firearm. Only usable in Homid form. The equipped character may play 1 Combat Action, up to Rage 12. Discard the Rocket Launcher upon use.

Sands of Sleep.Name: Sands of Sleep
Sands of Sleep.Text: The owner can play this card on any opponent in combat, immediately removing the character from combat.  A target affected by the Sands of Sleep cannot take any actions for 2 full turns.  If attacked, the target immediately wakes up.  Discard the Sands of Sleep upon use.

Security System.Name: Security System
Security System.Text: A character need not equip with Security System during the Equip/Ally Phase; play it instead whenever a Sneak Attack is declared against any character in your pack. That Sneak Attack is canceled. Discard Security System when it is used.

Shotgun.Name: Shotgun
Shotgun.Text: Weapon. Firearm. Allows the character to use up to Rage 7 Combat Actions.

Silver Ammo.Name: Silver Ammo
Silver Ammo.Text: Play with any damage card from a firearm attack.  Damage is aggravated.  The character does not have to equip with Silver Ammo in order to use this card.

Skin of the Hellbound.Name: Skin of the Hellbound
Skin of the Hellbound.Text: Armor. A character equipped with the Skin of the Hellbound is not affected by any Damage Cards of Rage 6 or higher.
Skin of the Hellbound.Errata: Clarification: Combat cards, of Rage 6 of higher, do not deal any damage to the user in the resolution step. 10/24/2015"

Sniper Rifle.Name: Sniper Rifle
Sniper Rifle.Text: Firearm. Only usable by creatures in Homid form. Cannot be used in combat. Once per turn, the owner may play a Rage 2 or less Combat Action on any creature in the Hunting Grounds. Do not refill your combat hand after using Sniper Rifle;  do so only when y

Spear of Deceit.Name: Spear of Deceit
Spear of Deceit.Text: The Spear of Deceit allows its owner to end any fight in which she is engaged, provided at least two rounds of combat have been fought.

Spiral Boomerang.Name: Spiral Boomerang
Spiral Boomerang.Text: Select 1 character you're in combat with. That character is forced into the Umbra and the Hunting Grounds for 2 turns, after which she returns to the physical world and her pack. Discard the Spiral Boomerang upon use.
Spiral Boomerang.Errata: This can send creatures which cannot step sideways into the Umbra.

Spirit Drum of Calm-Thunder.Name: Spirit Drum of Calm-Thunder
Spirit Drum of Calm-Thunder.Text: Once per turn, the user of the drum may cancel any single frenzy. Using this ability will end the current combat, and the character whose frenzy was canceled may immediately attack the Fetish user.
Spirit Drum of Calm-Thunder.Errata: Clarification: The other abilities only work if the frenzy was cancelled. If you are in the real world and can affect the Umbra and use the drum to cancel the frenzy, the Umbral Character cannot attack you back as you are an invalid target. (Revised 2014)"

Sports Car.Name: Sports Car
Sports Car.Text: Armor. The character using the Sports Car may escape from any combat after the third round.

Submachine Gun.Name: Submachine Gun
Submachine Gun.Text: Weapon. Firearm. The character can play up to 2 Combat Actions of Rage 2 or lower each round of combat.
Submachine Gun.Errata: Reversal: There are no form restrictions on the Submachine Gun.  7/3/2015

Suit of Mirrors.Name: Suit of Mirrors
Suit of Mirrors.Text: Armor. The Suit of Mirrors  makes it difficult for opponents to use their powers against its owner. The user is not affected by Gifts of Gnosis 4 or less. This does not include Gifts cast by the user or her packmates.

Tabula Rasa.Name: Tabula Rasa
Tabula Rasa.Text: A character using Tabula Rasa is desperately calling for the Garou to unite under Gaia. As long as Tabula Rasa is in play, the Moot Phase occurs AFTER the Combat Phase instead of before it. Only 1 Tabula Rasa may be played per game.

Tambertail's heart.Name: Tambertail's heart
Tambertail's heart.Text: Tambertail was a Fianna Theurge and the Black Spiral Dancers have mummified his heart as an artifact; it warns them of impending danger. A character equipped with Tambertail's Heart may escape from any combat after the second round. Only 1 Tambertail's He

Tear Gas Cannister.Name: Tear Gas Cannister
Tear Gas Cannister.Text: Weapon. Play Tear Gas in between rounds of combat (as a Combat Event). All characters, allies, enemies, and victims in combat with 2 or less Health remaining in their current form act at Rage 1 for the next 2 rounds of combat.

The Silver Crown.Name: The Silver Crown
The Silver Crown.Text: The bearer of the Silver Crown gains +6 renown during Moots. Once per game, during the Moot Phase, you may look at another player's sept hand. You may then force them to play any 1 Action, Event or Gift immediately. Discard the Silver Crown after doing th

Thunder's Gauntlet.Name: Thunder's Gauntlet
Thunder's Gauntlet.Text: Thunder's Gauntlet enables the user to manipulate the forces of lightning and storms. Once per game, the owner of Thunder's Gauntlet may select 1 non-Shadow Lords character in play and smite him with a bolt of lightning; that character's Health is immedia
Thunder's Gauntlet.Errata: TG reduces the target's health to 1 in all forms, including all modifiers. If they had damage, it still only reduces their remaining health to 1. If forced to breed form immediately after TG was used, the Character still has 1 health remaining in breed fo"

Toga of Dionysius.Name: Toga of Dionysius
Toga of Dionysius.Text: A character equipped with the Toga cannot be affected by Gifts and loses 2 Rage (never reduced to less than 1). Any Gifts currently affecting a character who equips with a Toga of Dionysius are discarded.
Toga of Dionysius.Errata: The user cannot be the target of any Gifts. For Gifts that target or affect multiple creatures at the same time, all other targets are still affected, the owner of Toga of Dionysus treats the Gift's text box as if it was blank. Toga of Dionysus only prote"

Tongue of the Leech.Name: Tongue of the Leech
Tongue of the Leech.Text: At the beginning of the turn, you may choose one damage card and change its type; either from aggravated to normal, or from normal  to aggravated. Tongue of the Leech may not be used by vampires.

Tracer Rounds.Name: Tracer Rounds
Tracer Rounds.Text: A character equipped with Tracer Rounds is considered -1 Renown for the purposes of engaging a Battlefield.

Vampire Blood.Name: Vampire Blood
Vampire Blood.Text: Discard to remove (heal) the lowest damage card from the equipped character. This can be done at any time.

Veil of Gentle Words.Name: Veil of Gentle Words
Veil of Gentle Words.Text: This Character has an additional 2 votes in Juntas called by Homids. It may not be attacked by Homid form creatures while it is in Homid form.

Wanchese's Bow.Name: Wanchese's Bow
Wanchese's Bow.Text: Weapon. A character can make a sacrificial attack against any enemy in the Hunting Grounds. This character can, as a Combat Action, kill herself doing her full Renown in damage against her foe. This takes effect at the end of the combat round. If the enem
Wanchese's Bow.Errata: The Bow?s ability is used as a Slow Striking combat action, and thus may be dodged, blocked, or cancelled as normal. The damage of the attack is equal to the printed renown of the Character - Renown modifiers do not add to the damage done by the Bow. If t"

War Knife of Benning Simon.Name: War Knife of Benning Simon
War Knife of Benning Simon.Text: Weapon. The War Knife allows its user to do aggravated damage with Combat Actions of Rage 4 or less. Alternately, the character can use the War Knife to lock up any 1 piece of her opponent's Equipment. If locked, both the War Knife and the target Equipmen

War lodge.Name: War lodge
War lodge.Text: The owner of the War Lodge entreaties the spirits to help him in battle. He may then discard 1 spirit Ally or Fetish per turn; if he does so, he may immediately enter combat with the opponent of his choice.
War lodge.Errata: Will only let you attack targets in the same world, unless you have some other way of crossing the Gauntlet. You may only discard cards you control to use War Lodge. You may discard War Lodge to use War Lodge's ability."

War Paint of Wahya Ohni.Name: War Paint of Wahya Ohni
War Paint of Wahya Ohni.Text: When applied, the Garou heals twice as quickly.  Remove 1 additional damage card from the owner of this fetish during the Regeneration Phase.
War Paint of Wahya Ohni.Errata: Clarification: This will HEAL the lowest damage card on the user during regeneration phase. This occurs after regular regeneration has occurred. (Revised 2014)

Web Drive Interface.Name: Web Drive Interface
Web Drive Interface.Text: The user of the Web Drive Interface has access to vast amounts of information. As long as the Interface is in play, the player controlling it may see any Equipment cards in any player's sept hand at any time.

Whip of the Wicked.Name: Whip of the Wicked
Whip of the Wicked.Text: Weapon. Opponents facing the equipped character in combat must play any and all block- and dodge-related combat Actions before any other actions can be played.

William Wallace's Bones.Name: William Wallace's Bones
William Wallace's Bones.Text: Gathered from the far corners of Britain, William Wallace's Bones inspire Celtic solidarity. Before combat begins or between any 2 rounds, the owner of the Bones may call any Fianna packmate(s) into a pack attack or defense. Draw 1 card if you bring a pac

Windcutter.Name: Windcutter
Windcutter.Text: Weapon. Opponents/' Armor Equipment have no effect in combats with a creature using the Wind Cutter. Whenever the owner damages an opponent, that opponent discards an Armor Equipment card (opponent's choice).

Wyldstone.Name: Wyldstone
Wyldstone.Text: This fetish adds 1 to a Garou's Gnosis for the purpose of using Gifts. Alternately, the character can discard this stone at any time and immediately change into Crinos form.

Wyrm Skin.Name: Wyrm Skin
Wyrm Skin.Text: Armor. Enemies in the Hunting Grounds fight at -2 Rage when in combat against the owner of this fetish.
Wyrm Skin.Errata: Errata: This only affects Attacks targetting the user of Wyrm skin. (Revised 2014)

Animal Mummy.Name: Animal Mummy
Animal Mummy.Text: Unique.  This animal was once mummified as a sacrifice to the gods, and the Fera now use it to pray for spiritual intervention.  Once per turn you may try to destroy any one sept card in play.  Decide randomly if the card is discarded or not.  If it is di

Assegai.Name: Assegai
Assegai.Text: Weapon. Armor. This short spear can only be used in Homid or Crinos form.  The user blocks 1 damage from all Combat Actions targeting it.

War Mask.Name: War Mask
War Mask.Text: This Fetish only work in Homid form.  Opponents in combat with the user act at -1 Rage and may not use Firearms.

The Battleground.Name: The Battleground
The Battleground.Text: When defending, your character can replay any one exchange of cards, once per combat. This must be done immediately after the exchange in Question. The cards being replayed are discarded. Your pack can only have one Realm in play.
The Battleground.Errata: When cards are revealed, you may state you will discard all combat actions played this round and go back to the Play Card step. You do not need to have played a card this round to use this effect. Any creatures involved in the combat may now select new co"

Wolfhome.Name: Wolfhome
Wolfhome.Text: Opponents attacking a pack which controls Wolfhome revert to breed form (unless doing so would kill them) and may not use Gifts during the combat. The pack which controls Wolfhome may not use any techno-Equipment (non-fetish Equipment).

Dorado Realm.Name: Dorado Realm
Dorado Realm.Text: Any Gaia pack may use this Realm. As long as it is in play, any attacks declared against any members of the pack controlling the Dorado Realm are considered challenges and may be declined as such. Your pack may only have one Realm in play at a time. Only

Flux.Name: Flux
Flux.Text: When defending, any time your opponent plays a card equal to your combat action's Rage it is treated as a bluff - regardless of your opponent's Rage. Your pack can only have one Realm in play.

Legendary.Name: Legendary
Legendary.Text: While defending, your character's lowest attribute (Rage, Gnosis, or Health) is increased by 2. This will not affect a character's change into Crinos form if her original Rage is reached. Your pack can only have one Realm in play.

Malfeas.Name: Malfeas
Malfeas.Text: While defending, Wyrm characters must receive 1 additional damage card above and beyond the killing blow before they will actually die. Your pack can only have 1 Realm in play.

Pangea.Name: Pangea
Pangea.Text: While defending, your characters are not affected by any non-fetish Equipment. Your defending characters also gain 1 Gnosis. Your pack can only have one Realm in play.

Summer Country.Name: Summer Country
Summer Country.Text: When an opponent attacks one of your characters, that opponent cannot frenzy and plays combat actions at -1 Rage. Your pack can only have one Realm in play.

(In)Human Resources Division.Name: (In)Human Resources Division
(In)Human Resources Division.Text: All your Homid form packmembers may use Homid Gifts while (In)Human Resources Division is in play.

Abandoned Shed.Name: Abandoned Shed
Abandoned Shed.Text: The Abandoned Shed may be used to store Equipment; items may be placed here face-down during the Equip/Ally Phase. This Equipment may be given to characters at the beginning of the Combat Phase before alphas are chosen. If this Territory is destroyed, all

Ancestral Burial Mounds.Name: Ancestral Burial Mounds
Ancestral Burial Mounds.Text: The mounds are a source of Rage for the Wendigo, who look to them when they feel the chill of Harano. Once per turn, a pack controlling the Burial Mounds may ignore any single card effect that would force them not to take an action. Only 1 Ancestral Buria

Austere Temple.Name: Austere Temple
Austere Temple.Text: A pack controlling the Austere Temple is nourished by the temple's discipline and righteousness. Members of the pack gain +1 Health. Only 1 Austere Temple may be in play at any time.

Backwoods Kinfolk Hideaway.Name: Backwoods Kinfolk Hideaway
Backwoods Kinfolk Hideaway.Text: Your Kinfolk Allies have +1 Health and may regenerate.

Balkan Fief.Name: Balkan Fief
Balkan Fief.Text: The Balkan Fief provides rest and respite. A pack controlling the Balkan Fief may regenerate an additional damage card during the Regeneration Phase. Only 1 Balkan Fief may be in play at any time.
Balkan Fief.Errata: One member of the pack can regenerate one additional damage card.

Bawn.Name: Bawn
Bawn.Text: The Bawn is the area around a Caern that serves as a line of defense. A pack with the Bawn may not have their Caern taken from them or destroyed as long as the Bawn remains in play.
Bawn.Errata: You can discard your own caern while bawn is in play. If you bring another caern into play after this, the Bawn will still protect the new caern."

Black Dog Game Factory.Name: Black Dog Game Factory
Black Dog Game Factory.Text: The Game Factory spreads a subtle evil into the world. All Gaia characters of Homid breed lose 1 Gnosis while the Game Factory is in play. Only 1 Black Dog Game Factory may be in play at any time.

Camp Farris.Name: Camp Farris
Camp Farris.Text: A pack that controls Camp Farris may automatically pack attack or defend with up to 15 Renown worth of characters (including the alpha). Draw no additional cards if pack attacking or defending in this manner. Only 1 Camp Farris may be in play at any time.

Catskill Protectorate.Name: Catskill Protectorate
Catskill Protectorate.Text: Unique. Your Spirit and Faerie Allies have +2 Gnosis while Catskill Protectorate is in play.

Chantry.Name: Chantry
Chantry.Text: At the beginning of the turn, during the Redraw Phase, the pack controlling the Chantry selects 1 mage in play. Mages in the Umbra may be selected as well. That mage is removed from play for the duration of the turn and returns during the next Redraw Phas

Dead Zone.Name: Dead Zone
Dead Zone.Text: A Dead Zone is an area devoid of the normally ubiquitous spirits. A pack controlling the Dead Zone is not affected by any Gifts, and they may not use Gifts or Fetish Equipment. Only 1 Dead Zone may be in play at any time.
Dead Zone.Errata: A pack with a dead zone may not be the TARGET of Gifts. Opponents in combat may still benefit from Gifts targetting themselves. Gifts that have Global effects and do not target a specific pack will affect a pack with a Dead Zone. The pack with the Dead Zo

Fountain of 1000 Grieving Battles.Name: Fountain of 1000 Grieving Battles
Fountain of 1000 Grieving Battles.Text: Unique. The water from this fountain allows people to view past battles. Once per turn, you may discard a combat card from your hand to take a Combat Action from your combat discard pile and place it in your combat hand.

Hellhole.Name: Hellhole
Hellhole.Text: During combat, you may discard this Territory to send all Bane characters you control in this combat into full frenzy. Your highest Gnosis packmember can step in and replace any Bane in the Hunting Grounds even when not alpha.

Hereditary Castle.Name: Hereditary Castle
Hereditary Castle.Text: As long as the castle is in play, alphas may declare an attack against the castle owner's pack only if she permits it. The castle may be attacked as a Territory normally. Only 1 Hereditary Castle may be in play at any time.
Hereditary Castle.Errata: If a creature is forced to attack a member of the pack controlling Hereditary castle and castle?s owner says they may not attack, the original target is considered an illegal target. If a legal target is available, the creature may attack the new target a"

Hidden Lair.Name: Hidden Lair
Hidden Lair.Text: A pack with a Hidden lair is adept at weathering the storm of Garou relations. As an action, a Character in the pack that controls this Territory may seek cover at the Lair, thereby removing herself from play. While in the Hidden Lair, a character may tak
Hidden Lair.Errata: If the Hidden Lair is attacked outside the combat phase, creatures may come out of it to defend it. If attacked in the combat phase, it is destroyed."

Homestead.Name: Homestead
Homestead.Text: The Homestead is an excellent place in which to forge alliances. Once per game, the pack controlling the Homestead may select 1 Victim in play and make them an Ally. If the pack loses control of the Homestead, the Victim returns to the Hunting Grounds. On
Homestead.Errata: Victims which cannot be bound or controlled are not affected by Homestead.

Kinfolk Den.Name: Kinfolk Den
Kinfolk Den.Text: Their Kinfolk are one of the few things that Lupus Garou hold dear. While the Den is in play, the pack that controls it may automatically attack anyone who kills a packmate. This ability, if it is used, must be executed immediately after the packmate dies
Kinfolk Den.Errata: The entire pack joins the attack (except those prevented from joining pack actions). It is similar to Bum Rush. This ability must be used at the time the creature dies (even if the creature is full frenzy). If the creature dies in a combat which has not e

Kithain Freehold.Name: Kithain Freehold
Kithain Freehold.Text: The Fey will often aid Garou if their aims coincide. Faeries and changelings in the Hunting Grounds will join the pack which controls the Kithain Freehold as Allies at the end of any Combat Phase in which they are in play. If the pack loses control of the

Lodge of the Silver Record.Name: Lodge of the Silver Record
Lodge of the Silver Record.Text: While this Territory is in play, once per Moot Phase your may reveal a moot card from your hand and place it face down in your Victory Pile, where it is worth 1 VP.

Longwalker's Glade.Name: Longwalker's Glade
Longwalker's Glade.Text: Many Garou seek out Longwalker's Glade for the peace and tranquility it offers. The pack controlling Longwalker's Glade may recruit any spirit Allies, regardless of that Ally's requirements. The pack may not recruit spirit allies that are only Wyrm Allies

Monster Joe's Truck-n-Tow.Name: Monster Joe's Truck-n-Tow
Monster Joe's Truck-n-Tow.Text: Monster Joe's is a veritable fount of black-market goods. The pack that controls Monster Joe's may, during the Equip/Ally Phase, search through their deck for any single piece of non-Fetish Equipment. The sept deck must be reshuffled after this is done. O
Monster Joe's Truck-n-Tow.Errata: Place the piece of Equipment you searched for in your hand.

National Park.Name: National Park
National Park.Text: The National Park is a valuable resource, as it is one of the ever-dwindling regions of Gaia's presence. Members of a pack controlling the National Park gain +1 Gnosis.
National Park.Errata: Wyrm packs may use this Territory.

Niagara Protectorate.Name: Niagara Protectorate
Niagara Protectorate.Text: Unique. While facing your pack in combat, your opponents' bluffs always fail. Once per Resource Phase, you can discard a Unique Territory from your hand to draw a sept card.

North Country Protectorate.Name: North Country Protectorate
North Country Protectorate.Requires: Get of Fenris - Red Talons - Silver Fangs
North Country Protectorate.Text: Unique. As the home base of King Albrecht, North Country Protectorate is one of the most famous protectorate's in the world. You may recruit any Kinfolk Ally, regardless of requirements.

Police Precinct.Name: Police Precinct
Police Precinct.Text: A pack that controls the Police Precinct may arrest (remove from play) any single Homid-form character armed with a firearm until the beginning of the next turn. This ability is used in the Combat Phase before alphas are chosen. Only 1 Police Precinct may

Serene Wilderness.Name: Serene Wilderness
Serene Wilderness.Text: Nonalpha Allies of a pack controlling Serene Wilderness may refuse any challenges, even those challenges that may not normally be refused. They also may not be Sneak Attacked or otherwise engaged in combat unless they wish to be. Only 1 Serene Wilderness

Spirit Circle.Name: Spirit Circle
Spirit Circle.Text: Gaia favors those with the Spirit Circle under their sway. While Spirit Circle is in play, the Gifts of the pack that controls it may not be canceled. Only 1 Spirit Circle may be in play at any time.

This spring brings visions of the impend.Name: This spring brings visions of the impending Apocalypse. At the end of turn you may place one Sept card from your hand back in your Sept deck. Shuffle your Sept deck.

St. Vincent's Cathedral.Name: St. Vincent's Cathedral
St. Vincent's Cathedral.Text: St. Vincent's Cathedral is of the last repositories of unshakable faith that still stands. The pack that controls it may not be attacked by spirits or Wyrm characters. St. Vincent's Cathedral may be attacked as a Territory normally. Only 1 St. Vincent's C
St. Vincent's Cathedral.Errata: Does not protect against Wyrm Allies or Enemies.

Stonehenge.Name: Stonehenge
Stonehenge.Text: A pack controlling Stonehenge may never be forced to lose Gnosis. Only 1 Stonehenge may be in play at any time.

The Black Room.Name: The Black Room
The Black Room.Text: The bouncers at this club don't take any lip from anyone, even Crinos werewolves. While your pack controls the prestigious nightclub, attacks may only be declared against your pack by Homid-form attackers. The Black Room may be attacked as a Territory nor

The Calyx.Name: The Calyx
The Calyx.Text: Located in the Black Furies' native Greece, the Calyx is a seat of consolidated Black Fury power. Members of the pack who control the Calyx may use Ahroun Gifts. Only 1 Calyx may be in play at any time.

The Docks.Name: The Docks
The Docks.Text: A pack controlling the Docks may influence Equipment traveling into their area. If a member of another pack wants to equip with a piece of non-Fetish Equipment she must ask permission from the owner of the Docks. If the owner refuses, he himself may not e
The Docks.Errata: Errata: If the owner of the Docks prevents someone from equipping, the owner may not equip non-Fetish items this turn; he can still equip Fetish and Bane Fetish items.  While the Docks is out, all players except the owner of the Docks must equip all their"

The Naysayer's Hovel.Name: The Naysayer's Hovel
The Naysayer's Hovel.Text: At the beginning of the turn, during the Redraw Phase, the pack controlling the Naysayer's Hovel selects 1 Territory in play. That Territory offers no benefits to the pack that controls it for the current turn. Only 1 Naysayer's Hovel may be in play at an

The Pit.Name: The Pit
The Pit.Text: The Pit is a seedy dive in the thrall of the Wyrm. The pack controlling The Pit gains +1 victory points for any Victims they kill. If The Pit is removed from this pack's control, it still gains the victory point bonus for any kills made while the pack con
The Pit.Errata: The bonus applies only to Victims, not Enemies."

Tibetan Monastery.Name: Tibetan Monastery
Tibetan Monastery.Text: All characters in your pack are considered to know Kailindo while the Monastery is in play. Only 1 Tibetan Monastery may be in play at any time.

Tribal Road.Name: Tribal Road
Tribal Road.Text: Outside the Combat phase, this pack can only be attacked by Garou that are of the same tribe as one of the Garou in the pack. They may be attacked and challenged as normal in the Combat phase.

Termite Mounds.Name: Termite Mounds
Termite Mounds.Text: On the open plains, even a few feet of height can let a hunter see for miles.  Once per turn, the pack controlling Termite Mounds may look at the top 3 cards of target player's combat deck.

Ambush.Name: Ambush
Ambush.Text: The character can use a Gift that targets an opponent(s) in a combat this character is not directly involved with. The Gift user can never gain victory points for a combat he ambushes.

Clever Diversion.Name: Clever Diversion
Clever Diversion.Text: An attacker may play Clever Diversion after an attack and a defense have been declared on a Battlefield but before combat begins. She may remove the lowest Renown defender from the Battlefield and the combat. If this leaves no defender, the Battlefield (a
Clever Diversion.Errata: CLARIFICATION: If the last opponent is removed from combat (including a self-defending battlefield), this ends the combat. No VP are given for the Battlefield. 10/13/2015"

Dominance.Name: Dominance
Dominance.Text: This character cancels any Action card as it is played by a character of equal or lesser renown. Place this card in your Victory Pile where it is worth 1 renown.

Fast Shift.Name: Fast Shift
Fast Shift.Text: Your character can immediately enter the Umbra (ignore Gauntlet) during any phase of the game. However, this character must act as alpha during the next Combat Phase. If more than one pack member uses a Fast Shift in a turn, decide randomly which will be
Fast Shift.Errata: May also be used to leave the Umbra.

Fooled You!.Name: Fooled You!
Fooled You!.Text: Play when an enemy or victim in the Hunting Grounds is attacked. Your character immediately steps in and fights in place of the enemy or victim.

Friends in High Places.Name: Friends in High Places
Friends in High Places.Text: You call in a favor from some powerful friends. You may end any one combat that does not involve a frenzy. A pack may not play more than one Friends in High Places per turn. A character can only play one Friends in High Places per game.
Friends in High Places.Errata: Original: You may end any 1 combat or board meeting. Friends in High Places cannot stop a combat which involves a frenzy.

Kids Love Arson.Name: Kids Love Arson
Kids Love Arson.Text: The character playing this card is removed from play for 1 turn. He destroys any single Territory of his choice immediately. Kids Love Arson must be played before alphas are chosen if it is played in the Combat Phase.

Legal Chicanery.Name: Legal Chicanery
Legal Chicanery.Text: Restricted. Any Wyrm character may play this card to remove any single Homid form victim or Gaia pack ally from play. The target is embroiled in frivolous lawsuits and passport problems. The target of this card is not worth any victory points and is place
Legal Chicanery.Errata: Restricted. Chicanery affects Homid form Victims and Gaia Allies. 11/30015

Lend a Hand.Name: Lend a Hand
Lend a Hand.Text: Play this card during any combat that doesn't involve any of your characters. The character playing Lend A Hand joins the combat on the defender's side for one round. If the attacker frenzies, however, your character must remain in the fight until the fre
Lend a Hand.Errata: Errata: The Character using Lend a Hand escapes in the next withdraw step in which no creatures are frenzied. (2014)

Moon Bridge Attack.Name: Moon Bridge Attack
Moon Bridge Attack.Text: A character with a caern can use a Moon Bridge to immediately attack any character or ally in play whose pack also owns a caern.

Noble Sacrifice.Name: Noble Sacrifice
Noble Sacrifice.Text: Play when a creature this one is Loyal to takes a wound that would kill it. Redirect the damage to this character instead who replaces the original target in combat. Place this card in your Victory Pile where it is worth 2 Victory Points.

Play in combat. Draw a combat card..Name: Play in combat. Draw a combat card.

While your pack has more Firearms in com.Name: While your pack has more Firearms in combat than opposing creatures do, your opponents in combat may not take actions other than Combat Actions and may not redirect damage. Discard O

Attach to the user. Moots this Character.Name: Attach to the user. Moots this Character calls are treated as a Board Meetings in all respects: targeting, who they effect, who may vote in them, etc. Your characters can vote in Board Meetings called by this character.
Attach to the user. Moots this Character.Expansion: The Character who played Pentex Infiltration treats all Moots in hand as if they were Board Meetings and as if his pack was a Wyrm pack for determining if they can be played or not. Your pack and pack members are not otherwise considered Wyrm creatures, a"

Poignant Parable.Name: Poignant Parable
Poignant Parable.Text: For the duration of the Moot Phase, the Galliard telling the Poignant Parable may double his Renown (before any modifiers) for the purposes of voting.

Praise the Malformed.Name: Praise the Malformed
Praise the Malformed.Text: A Ragabash may play this card before voting begins. All Metis in play must vote as you choose.

Quoting the Litany.Name: Quoting the Litany
Quoting the Litany.Text: Play this card during any Moot to give a Philodox you control double his Renown for 1 vote only.

Reckless Stunt.Name: Reckless Stunt
Reckless Stunt.Text: Play right before your character begins combat. Decide the following randomly, equal chance for each: A - You draw 2 additional combat cards for the duration of this combat. B - You must randomly discard 2 combat cards from your hand.
Reckless Stunt.Errata: CLARIFICATION: this is played in the Beginning of Combat step.

Renown Admonishment.Name: Renown Admonishment
Renown Admonishment.Text: Play Renown Admonishment after a character makes a kill while frenzied. The character is berated for losing control and for having poor willpower. No victory points are earned by the character for any kills during the frenzy in Question.
Renown Admonishment.Errata: Works on Battle Fervor. RA can only be used if the frenzied creature delivered the killing blow. If a packmate makes the actual kill, Renown Admonishment won't work."

Shapeshift.Name: Shapeshift
Shapeshift.Text: This card can be played at any time by a character in your pack.  The character playing this card immediately changes from breed form to Crinos form or from Crinos form to breed form.
Shapeshift.Errata: Errata: May be used by any creature capable of changing shape (e.g., any two-sided Character)."

Sneak Attack.Name: Sneak Attack
Sneak Attack.Text: The character can circumvent the normal combat protocol and engage any character, ally or enemy in play.  If played during the Combat Phase of a turn, Sneak Attack can interrupt the normal alpha attack cycle, but cannot interrupt a combat that has already begun.
Sneak Attack.Errata: Clarification: If a Prey Creature is attacked outside the combat phase, no one may step in and replace it (as there are no Alphas). If Sneak Attack is played during the combat phase to attack Prey, Alphas may step in as normal."

Stand like a Fool.Name: Stand like a Fool
Stand like a Fool.Text: A Ragabash can play this card on any opponent she is facing in combat. For the next round the opponent cannot play any Combat Actions. If the opponent survives to the following round, she immediately enters Frenzy.

Step Sideways.Name: Step Sideways
Step Sideways.Text: Play during the Combat Phase, before alphas are chosen. Your character steps sideways into the Umbra against an initial Gauntlet of 3 (which is affected by any game modifiers).
Step Sideways.Errata: May also be used to leave the Umbra.

Streamlining.Name: Streamlining
Streamlining.Text: You study your opponents' tactics and come up with a strategy that will let your pack defeat them faster. You may look through your Sept deck and remove up to three cards from it. Remove these cards from the game. Shuffle your Sept deck.

Taunt.Name: Taunt
Taunt.Text: A Ragabash can play this card on any Garou who has just refused a challenge.  That Garou immediately accepts the challenge and enters frenzy.
Taunt.Errata: Clarification: Any Ragabash can play Taunt, even if he was not involved in the original challenge. Only Garou can be Taunted."

There You Are!!!.Name: There You Are!!!
There You Are!!!.Text: Play when an attacker declares that they will not continue combat. Combat ends, cards are redrawn as normal. A new combat begins between your character and the former attacker, who is now defending.
There You Are!!!.Errata: CLARIFICTATION:  You may play this even if you were not part of the original fight.

Throat Bare.Name: Throat Bare
Throat Bare.Text: Play this action any time before a character receives a mortal wound. The character taking this action cannot play any more Combat Actions for the duration of the combat. However, if killed, the attacker receives no victory points for the kill. This card
Throat Bare.Errata: Throat Bare works if you are the attacker OR the defender. Throat Bare is cancelled if the character using Throat Bare plays a combat action. Forced Play effects can still force you to play a combat action, which will cancel Throat Bare. 11/30/15"

'Giant Dogs Besiege City!'.Name: 'Giant Dogs Besiege City!'
'Giant Dogs Besiege City!'.Text: Newspaper hype makes people suspicious about the Garou. All Gaia Garou (no other shapechangers are affected) must revert to breed form unless doing so would kill them.

Alaskan Wolf Hunt.Name: Alaskan Wolf Hunt
Alaskan Wolf Hunt.Text: Humans are exterminating native wolves. Red Talons are caught in the hunt. All Red Talons characters must go into the Hunting Grounds until the next Regeneration Phase. Only 1 Alaskan Wolf Hunt may be played per game.

Assets Seizure.Name: Assets Seizure
Assets Seizure.Text: Target player must discard all non-fetish Equipment cards from his Sept hand. If target player cannot discard any such card from his hand, he discards one piece of his Equipment of his choice from play and one card from the top of his sept deck.

Bear.Name: Bear
Bear.Text: Bear is wise during peacetime and savage during war. The character who devotes himself to Bear may use Theurge Gifts, but loses 5 Renown during Moots. Characters with Personal Totems may no longer benefit from a Pack Totem.

Beast-of-War.Name: Beast-of-War
Beast-of-War.Text: All pack members gain 3 Rage and lose 1 Gnosis. This does not affect a character's transformation into Crinos or Battle form. A pack may not have more than 1 Pack Totem at any time.

Boar.Name: Boar
Boar.Text: Your pack members are +2 health in Homid and Animal form. Metis packmembers are +1 health.

Brave New World.Name: Brave New World
Brave New World.Text: Play Brave New World when there are 3 or more Mass Pollution Events in play. All Mass Pollution cards are immediately discarded. Only 1 Brave New World may be played per game.

Bull.Name: Bull
Bull.Text: Bull is the Totem of headstrong fury and impetuous action. Characters who have taken Bull as their Personal Totem draw an additional combat card during any combats in which they are involved. A character with Bull must play a Frenzy Combat Event immediate
Bull.Errata: Bull is used by Lupus Gaia Garou, not Lupus OR Gaia Garou. It applies to all frenzies."

Cataclysm.Name: Cataclysm
Cataclysm.Text: Discard all other Events in play. Cataclysm remains in play for 1 full turn, during which time no other Events may be played. Cataclysm cannot be played until there is a collective total of 30 Gnosis (or higher) worth of fetish and Bane fetish Equipment i

Checking the Classifieds.Name: Checking the Classifieds
Checking the Classifieds.Text: You may search through your sept deck and immediately bring any Territory into your hand.

Chimera.Name: Chimera
Chimera.Text: Increases your sept hand size by 1. A pack may not have more than 1 Pack Totem at any time.

City Father.Name: City Father
City Father.Text: Your pack members may decline attacks from Prey and from Animal form characters as if they were challenges.   Whenever you accept a challenge, draw a combat card.

Clashing Boom Boom.Name: Clashing Boom Boom
Clashing Boom Boom.Text: This totem of war manifests as a stealth bomber.�

While in Crinos, packmembers may use Equ.Name: While in Crinos, packmembers may use Equipment as if they were in Homid form.�

Packmembers don't discard Flak Jackets d.Name: Packmembers don't discard Flak Jackets due to changing form.

Close Gauntlet.Name: Close Gauntlet
Close Gauntlet.Text: For the duration of the turn, all characters, allies, and enemies in play cannot step sideways. Those in the Umbra must remain there until the next turn.

Cockroach.Name: Cockroach
Cockroach.Text: Twice per game you may look at the top 5 cards of any player's sept or combat deck. You may then choose to reshuffle that deck. A pack may not have more than 1 Pack Totem at any time.
Cockroach.Errata: Clarification: If you used Cockroach's ability twice, discard Cockroach, and then play a new one, you may still use the NEW copy of Cockroach's twice per game ability. (2014)"

Congressional Hearing.Name: Congressional Hearing
Congressional Hearing.Text: Members of the 7th Generation cannot use their special abilities for 1 full turn.

Connections.Name: Connections
Connections.Text: It's not who you are, it's who you know. For the remainder of the turn, the pack using its Connections does not have to meet any prerequisites to recruit Allies during the Equip/Ally Phase.

Corporate Take-over.Name: Corporate Take-over
Corporate Take-over.Text: Each Pentex character in play must discard 1 piece of Equipment.

Crescent Moon.Name: Crescent Moon
Crescent Moon.Text: Play at the beginning of any turn or to cancel and supersede any New or Half Moon just played. All spirits gain 1 Rage and all Theurges gain 1 Gnosis while Crescent Moon is in effect. Discard this card when a new Lunar Phase is played.

Daybreak.Name: Daybreak
Daybreak.Text: Unique. All Vampires have -2 Rage. Vampires may not play Gifts unless they are in combat. If they are involved in a combat, they may not play Gifts until after the first combat round. Discard Daybreak at the end of the turn.

Defiler.Name: Defiler
Defiler.Text: You may discard the Defiler to take any 1 caern in play and make it your own. Ignore the target Caern's requirements (it has been defiled). A pack may not have more than 1 Pack Totem at any time.

Distracting Spirits.Name: Distracting Spirits
Distracting Spirits.Text: Reduce the engaging Renown of all Battlefields in play by 2. Distracting Spirits lasts for 1 full turn. No more than 1 Distracting Spirits Event may be in play at a time.

Dragon.Name: Dragon
Dragon.Text: If there are more Wyrm characters in play than Gaia characters, the Dragon allows packs which take him as their totem to take 1 additional alpha action at the end of the Combat Phase. A pack may not have more than 1 Pack Totem at any time.

Drunken Revelry.Name: Drunken Revelry
Drunken Revelry.Text: All Fianna lose 1 Gnosis for the rest of the game. No more than 3 Drunken Revelries can be in effect at any time.

Eater-of-Souls.Name: Eater-of-Souls
Eater-of-Souls.Text: Your pack can now equip with fetish Equipment. They must still meet the Gnosis requirements.

Eminent Domain.Name: Eminent Domain
Eminent Domain.Text: Each Gaia player selects one Territory he controls and gives it to the Wyrm player of his choice. The Wyrm pack does not need to meet the requirements to use the Territory. If there are no Gaia players, each Wyrm player may search his deck for one Territo

Environmental Action.Name: Environmental Action
Environmental Action.Text: Removes 1 Mass Pollution or Garbage Food Poisoning card in play.

Ethereal Wind.Name: Ethereal Wind
Ethereal Wind.Text: Once per turn select a Creature in play. It gains the ability to use Kailindo combat actions until the end of the turn.

Falcon.Name: Falcon
Falcon.Text: Each pack member gains 1 Renown for moot voting. A pack may not have more than 1 Pack Totem at any time.

FBI Investigation.Name: FBI Investigation
FBI Investigation.Text: All Black Spiral Dancers miss their next turn. They can take no actions other than to regenerate and to defend themselves.

Fenris.Name: Fenris
Fenris.Text: All pack members gain 1 Rage when in Crinos form. A pack may not have more than 1 Pack Totem at any time.

Foreclosure.Name: Foreclosure
Foreclosure.Text: Someone must have forgotten to mail the rent check... Select one Territory in play. The pack that controls the Territory must discard 1 of their pieces of Equipment or discard their Territory.

Full Moon.Name: Full Moon
Full Moon.Text: Play at the beginning of any turn or to cancel and supersede any Gibbous Moon just played. Any Garou can freely enter Crinos form, and all Ahroun gain 1 Gnosis while Full Moon is in effect. Discard this card when a new Lunar Phase is played.

Half Moon.Name: Half Moon
Half Moon.Text: Play at the beginning of any turn or to cancel and supersede any Crescent or Gibbous Moon just played. All Rites cost -1 Renown and all Philodox gain 1 Gnosis while Half Moon is in effect. Discard this card when a new Lunar Phase is played.

Harano Gloom.Name: Harano Gloom
Harano Gloom.Text: Choose 1 Silver Fangs. The character will not initiate combat or join in pack attacks while suffering from Harano Gloom. If the character is attacked, Harano Gloom is discarded. A character may not be affected by more than 1 Harano Gloom per game.

Hidden Supplies.Name: Hidden Supplies
Hidden Supplies.Text: When in play, you may play Equipment from your sept hand face down, under the Hidden Supplies card. You may then equip any character in your pack with one or all of these supplies at the beginning of ANY phase of the game. Equipment can also be deposited
Hidden Supplies.Errata: Equipment can be placed here FACE DOWN during Resource Phase, but can be taken out at the beginning of any phase. Characters putting equipment under Hidden Supplies trade it to the Hidden Supplies, following standard trading rules. Items under Hidden Supp"

Hyperion.Name: Hyperion
Hyperion.Requires: Garou who has completed an Umbral Quest.
Hyperion.Text: Members of your pack can regenerate aggravated damage. Flame Spirits will not attack members of your pack. A pack may not have more than one Pack Totem at any time.

Inbred Disorder.Name: Inbred Disorder
Inbred Disorder.Text: Choose 1 Silver Fangs. The character suffers from a slight mental abnormality. She acts at -2 Gnosis for the rest of the game. A character may not be affected by more than 1 Inbred Disorder.

Indecision.Name: Indecision
Indecision.Text: If more than one player has the same character in play, all copies are removed from play until the end of turn.

Iron Will.Name: Iron Will
Iron Will.Text: Iron Will affects a single character. It allows that character to overcome the effects of any Combat Action or Gift that would normally force her to lose her next Combat Action. The affected character cannot be made to forfeit her intended action during t
Iron Will.Errata: Play Iron Will at any time and attach it to the character. You may discard Iron Will to ignore the effects of a Gift or Combat Action that would prevent you from playing a combat action; any other effects from the Gift or Combat action (ex: damage) still

Isolation.Name: Isolation
Isolation.Text: Players may not draw more than 4 Sept cards during the turn. This includes the Redraw Phase and all card effects that allow you to draw cards. This Event is permanent until cancelled.

Jaguar.Name: Jaguar
Jaguar.Text: Any pack with the Jaguar as its totem may discard the Jaguar to remove any Battlefield from any victory pile and return it to the Hunting Grounds. A pack may not have more than 1 Pack Totem at any time.

Journey Onward.Name: Journey Onward
Journey Onward.Text: Choose another player. The player must select 1 Stargazers from her pack to discard. The discarded character is worth no victory points. Only 1 Journey Onward can be played on each pack.

Journey to the East.Name: Journey to the East
Journey to the East.Text: Play this card on any Garou you control, except on a Garou in combat. The Garou goes to the mythic East to learn the mystical art of Kailindo. Remove the character from play for 2 full turns, at which point she will return knowing Kailindo. Keep this card with the character. Garou on a Journey to the East may still regenerate.

He grants his children the Gift of stealth. This character's challenges cannot be refused. A character can only have 1 personal totem at a time. Characters with personal totems may no longer benefit from a pack totem.

The discarded character is worth no victory points. Only 1 Leadership Challenge can be played per game.

Lunar Eclipse.Name: Lunar Eclipse
Lunar Eclipse.Text: Remove any 1 Lunar Phase from play. No Lunar Phase may be played while Lunar Eclipse is in play. Remove all auspice Gifts in play. No auspice Gifts may be played while Lunar Eclipse is in play. Discard Lunar Eclipse after the next Redraw Phase.
Lunar Eclipse.Errata: Clarification: This prevents Gifts from being played using Auspice requirements. If played through some other requirement, even if it lists an Auspice as a possible user, it will not prevent the Gift being played, nor cancel it."

Mass Pollution.Name: Mass Pollution
Mass Pollution.Text: All Wyrm characters gain 1 Gnosis. Non-Wyrm characters (those weak servants of Gaia) lose 1 Gnosis.

Matriarch Mourning.Name: Matriarch Mourning
Matriarch Mourning.Text: The Black Furies have lost 1 of their elders. All Black Furies can take no actions for the next 2 turns, though they may defend themselves if attacked. Only 1 Matriarch Mourning may be played per game.

Monsoon.Name: Monsoon
Monsoon.Text: For the current turn, every character in play is considered +2 Renown for the purposes of joining Battlefields and pack actions.

Nacho Snax.Name: Nacho Snax
Nacho Snax.Text: Discard any single Garbage Food Poisoning in play.

Nemesis.Name: Nemesis
Nemesis.Text: Select two characters from different packs. These characters now have a Rivalry.
Nemesis.Errata: This creates a rivalry between 2 specific Characters, not all Characters of the same name. If a Character is somehow temporarily removed from his pack and joins another one, Nemesis may be played while he is in the other pack. When he returns to his origi"

New Moon.Name: New Moon
New Moon.Text: Play at the beginning of any turn or to cancel and supersede any Crescent Moon just played. No one may frenzy and all Ragabash gain 1 Gnosis while New Moon is in effect. Discard this card when a new Lunar Phase is played.
New Moon.Errata: Errata: New Moon prevents all creatures from frenzying. (Changed 2014)

Nuclear Sauna.Name: Nuclear Sauna
Nuclear Sauna.Text: Play just after alphas are chosen. All regenerating Wyrm creatures in play immediately take an additional regeneration, healing their lowest non-aggravated damage card. Discard this Event once its effects take place.

Owl.Name: Owl
Owl.Text: You may look at 1 opponent's hand (combat or sept) once every other turn. A pack may not have more than 1 Pack Totem at any time.
Owl.Errata: Clarification: You can use Owl's ability, discard Owl, and play a new copy of Owl and use its ability immediately."

Panthesilea.Name: Panthesilea
Panthesilea.Text: Once per turn, you can search through your deck and bring Tribal Warriors into play. Shuffle your deck when done. These Tribal Warriors can now pack attack or defend with any Ghost Raptor.

Peace of Nature.Name: Peace of Nature
Peace of Nature.Text: All Children of Gaia play combat cards at -1 Rage for the rest of the game. If any Children of Gaia character goes into frenzy, that player may select any 1 Peace of Nature card in play to be discarded.

Pegasus.Name: Pegasus
Pegasus.Text: Each pack member gains 1 Gnosis. A pack may not have more than 1 Pack Totem at any time.

Pervis and Pig-Fed.Name: Pervis and Pig-Fed
Pervis and Pig-Fed.Text: Play this Event before alphas are chosen. Something good comes on television, distracting some of the characters. Homid form characters may not be selected as alpha during the current Combat Phase, though they may defend themselves if attacked and they ma

Phoebe.Name: Phoebe
Phoebe.Requires: Garou who has completed an Umbral Quest.
Phoebe.Text: You can search through your deck and place any Lunar Phase into play at any time (reshuffle your deck each time this is done). No new Lunar Phase can be played unless you permit it.  A pack may not have more than one Pack Totem at any time.

Pure Breeding.Name: Pure Breeding
Pure Breeding.Text: Select 1 character; that character's ancestors were all highly exemplary members of their particular tribe. You may search through your sept deck for the Past Life of the character's tribe and immediately play it on that character. Reshuffle your sept dec

Ragnarok.Name: Ragnarok
Ragnarok.Text: The Get of Fenris believe that the Final Battle is upon them. All Get of Fenris go to the Hunting Grounds until the next Regeneration Phase. Only 1 Ragnarok can be played per game.

Rat.Name: Rat
Rat.Text: Each pack member gains 1 Health. A pack may not have more than 1 Pack Totem at any time.

Raven.Name: Raven
Raven.Text: In combats outside of the Combat Phase, your pack members' Combat Actions gain the Fast Striking keyword during the first round of combat. You may never voluntarily bluff during the first round of ANY combat.

Red Alert.Name: Red Alert
Red Alert.Text: Any Wyrm character entering combat can assume their Battle form (Crinos form) during the Red Alert. Discard the Red Alert after 2 turns. Characters in Battle form may remain in that form.
Red Alert.Errata: Clarification: Red Alert gives a triggered effect, whenever the Character joins combat in the Declaration step or joins a pack combat. It is not an Action. 10/24/2015"

Retaking the Field.Name: Retaking the Field
Retaking the Field.Text: Select one Battlefield in a victory pile. That Battlefield can now be attacked as though it were in the Hunting Grounds. Only the pack it belonged to may defend it. If that defense is defeated, the Battlefield moves to the new victor's victory pile.
Retaking the Field.Errata: CLARIFICATION: Treat the defender requirements for the Battlefield to now read as ""current owner"" for Defender requirements instead of what was originally listed. This Battlefield will NOT self defend. 11/30/15"

Rezoning Committee.Name: Rezoning Committee
Rezoning Committee.Text: Territories give their owners no benefits for the remainder of the turn. Discard Rezoning Committee at the end of the Combat Phase of the turn in which it was played.

Saving Face.Name: Saving Face
Saving Face.Text: Play on a Garou who called a Moot that did not pass. That Garou must act as pack alpha during the upcoming Combat Phase.

Scouting Mission.Name: Scouting Mission
Scouting Mission.Text: Choose a pack containing at least 1 Silent Strider. The player controlling the pack must select 1 Silent Strider to be sent into the Hunting Grounds for 1 full turn. If the character returns alive, his pack gains 3 victory points. Only 1 Scouting Mission may be played per game.

Spirit Backlash.Name: Spirit Backlash
Spirit Backlash.Text: Spirits bound into fetishes and Bane fetishes revolt and break free. All fetish and Bane fetish Equipment requiring Gnosis 5 or greater to equip are immediately discarded. Only 1 Spirit Backlash can be played per game. Discard the Event after its effect t

Spiritual Revelation.Name: Spiritual Revelation
Spiritual Revelation.Text: All Bane characters in play are automatically moved into the Umbra.

Stag.Name: Stag
Stag.Text: No faerie spirits can attack any members of the pack. You may draw 2 additional combat cards whenever you attack any enemy in the Hunting Grounds. A pack may not have more than 1 Pack Totem at any time.
Stag.Errata: Errata: Draw the 2 cards when the attack is declared, before Wyrm Alphas have an option of stepping in. Stag prevents ALL Faeries from attacking you. (2014)"

Stuck Sideways.Name: Stuck Sideways
Stuck Sideways.Text: Select one character stepping sideways. That character is trapped until the next redraw Phase and can be attacked by any alpha in play. The character can take no actions during this time, but can play combat actions (except Umbral Escape) if attacked.
Stuck Sideways.Errata: Stuck Sideways cancels a character's attempt to step sideways. This will not end a combat.

Telemarketing Campaign.Name: Telemarketing Campaign
Telemarketing Campaign.Text: All other packs skip their next Redraw Phase. The player using Telemarketing Campaign has his Sept hand size reduced by three next turn. The same player may not play Telemarketing Campaign two turns in a row.
Telemarketing Campaign.Errata: Original: Select 1 pack in play. This pack must skip its next Redraw Phase.

The Bat.Name: The Bat
The Bat.Text: The Bat is a totem of strength. Characters following the Bat gain 1 Rage and 1 Health. They can never fight for more than 3 rounds of combat and will escape from any combat after the third round. A character can only have 1 personal totem at a time. Chara

The Dark Fungus.Name: The Dark Fungus
The Dark Fungus.Text: The Dark Fungus's children are blessed with surreal visions. Once per turn, you may look at the top three cards of any player's Sept deck. You may place one of those cards on the bottom of that player's Sept deck; replace the other two on top of the Sept

The Green Dragon.Name: The Green Dragon
The Green Dragon.Text: This pack's alpha does aggravated damage and is +2 Rage. Your pack members can ignore form restrictions on combat cards. Your packmembers cannot withdraw or be forced to withdraw from combat. Discard this Totem if its pack ever contains a Metis. If Zhyzha

The Wolves are out there.Name: The Wolves are out there
The Wolves are out there.Text: Governmental investigations force characters into hiding. Play The Wolves Are Out There at the end of any Moot Phase, and all characters skip the Combat Phase, immediately returning to the Redraw Phase.

Toga Party.Name: Toga Party
Toga Party.Text: Play this card for your pack at any time before alphas are chosen. Your alpha has no attack this turn. Each member of your pack is at +2 Rage if attacked.
Toga Party.Errata: On the turn it is played, your alpha cannot declare an attack as an alpha action. He may still take other Alpha actions that are NOT attacks. Toga Party is permanent (this only gives the Rage bonus while defending) updated 9/9/2015"

Town Meeting.Name: Town Meeting
Town Meeting.Text: Play during the Moot phase. Wyrm packs may call Board Meetings even if there is only one Wyrm pack in the game. Human Kinfolk Allies of any pack may vote in any Board Meetings called this turn. Draw a sept card.

Uktena.Name: Uktena
Uktena.Text: No spirit may attack pack members. A pack may not have more than 1 Pack Totem at any time.

Umbral Wave.Name: Umbral Wave
Umbral Wave.Text: All characters in the Umbra revert to their breed forms. Discard all Gauntlet Fluxes in play.

Unicorn.Name: Unicorn
Unicorn.Text: One pack member may regenerate 1 additional damage card during your Regeneration Phase. A pack may not have more than 1 Pack Totem at any time.

Airt Gateway.Name: Airt Gateway
Airt Gateway.Text: The Bane can automatically step sideways into the Umbra, regardless of the Gauntlet. The Bane automatically assumes its Battle form. Discard this Gift after its effect takes place.

Airt Mastery.Name: Airt Mastery
Airt Mastery.Text: Select 1 target in the Umbra. That target becomes hopelessly lost and is removed from play until the next Regeneration Phase. Discard this Gift after its effect takes place.

Alias.Name: Alias
Alias.Text: The character creates an exact duplicate of himself, including both Crinos and Breed form. The duplicate has the same printed stats and special abilities as the Gift user. It may vote only in Juntas that the Alias user can not vote in. The Alias will last
Alias.Errata: Original: The caster uses minor spirits to create a duplicate of himself. The duplicate functions and fights just as well as the original. The Alias may be affected by anything that normally affects spirits, and will last until cancelled or killed. The Al"

Allies Below.Name: Allies Below
Allies Below.Text: The Gift user can create a temporary pack out of enemies in the Hunting Grounds. Up to 10 Renown worth of enemies can be brought into the pack. You may draw 1 additional combat card per enemy recruited. This lasts for 1 combat only. Allies Below may be us
Allies Below.Errata: CLARIFICATION: Play during combat. The Enemies temporarily join your pack as Allies until the end of this combat.updated 9/9/2015

Attunement.Name: Attunement
Attunement.Text: The Bone Gnawer 'sniffs out' the area. Until this Gift is cancelled, Attunement duplicates any single Territory in play (treat the Gift as a Territory), even if the Territory is unique. Declare which Territory the Bone Gnawer duplicates when this Gift is

Aura of Confidence.Name: Aura of Confidence
Aura of Confidence.Text: The Shadow Lords gives himself a spiritual aura of prestige, increasing his Renown by 1. This Renown increase does not affect the character's victory point worth. This Gift lasts until canceled.

Awe.Name: Awe
Awe.Text: The Silver Fangs's regal manner increases her Renown by 1, except during moots, where her Renown increases by 2. This Renown increase does not affect the character's victory point worth. This Gift lasts until canceled.

Balefire.Name: Balefire
Balefire.Text: When in the Umbra, Combat Actions played by the Gift user are aggravated. Discard this Gift after the end of the current Combat Phase.

Balor's Gaze.Name: Balor's Gaze
Balor's Gaze.Text: The eyes of the user glow a horrifyingly baleful red. For the current combat, any opponents with less Rage than the user are crippled with wracking pains and may take no actions. Discard this Gift after its effect takes place.
Balor's Gaze.Errata: Errata: Balor's Gaze will prevent all opponents of lower Rage from playing combat actions for one ROUND of combat, not the whole combat."

Bane Infestation.Name: Bane Infestation
Bane Infestation.Text: The character can corrupt and take any 1 piece of fetish Equipment from any target in the game. The Bane must still meet the Gnosis requirements of the targeted Equipment otherwise this Gift will have no effect.
Bane Infestation.Errata: The Gift user may equip the targeted Fetish. If they cannot meet the other requirements, discard it instead. 11/30/15"

Bane Moonbridge.Name: Bane Moonbridge
Bane Moonbridge.Text: The Gift user can attack any character belonging to a pack with a caern. This does not grant the character an extra attack and must be done as the Bane's alpha action. The defending character cannot refuse this challenge. This Gift is permanent until canc
Bane Moonbridge.Errata: CLARIFICATION: This is a moon bridge. updated 9/9/2015

Battle Song.Name: Battle Song
Battle Song.Text: The Galliard's pack plays Combat Actions at +2 Rage for the current turn. Discard this Gift after its effect takes place.

Beastmind.Name: Beastmind
Beastmind.Text: The target of this Gift finds his mind reduced to that of an animal for 1 turn. The target may not use any Equipment during this time. Discard this Gift after its effect takes place.

Bellow.Name: Bellow
Bellow.Text: The Gift user forces all opponents in combat against him to flee, ending combat. The targeted opponents act at -2 Rage until their next Regeneration Phase. Discard this Gift upon use.

Benefactor's Boon.Name: Benefactor's Boon
Benefactor's Boon.Text: When played, pick Rage, Gnosis or Health. The user gains +3 to that stat. Pick a different stat: the user suffers -2 to it. If you pick Gnosis to decrease, the character must still be able to play  this Gift with the adjusted Gnosis. This Gift is permanen

Blissful Ignorance.Name: Blissful Ignorance
Blissful Ignorance.Text: The user can make herself invisible as long as she takes no actions. The character cannot be attacked, participate in moots, or have Gifts used upon her. The character still regenerates as normal.

Blur of the Milky Eye.Name: Blur of the Milky Eye
Blur of the Milky Eye.Text: When this creature declares an attack it may not be redirected and no one may step in to replace the target. This Gift is permanent until canceled.

Body Wrack.Name: Body Wrack
Body Wrack.Text: The target of this Gift is enfeebled by spasmic pains coursing through her entire body. The target may take no actions during the next combat round and takes 1 damage from the intense pain. Place this card on the target for the purposes of recording damage.
Body Wrack.Errata: Clarification: Healing the damage will not stop the Gift's effect.

Born to Nature.Name: Born to Nature
Born to Nature.Text: Use this Gift to make any spirit miss its next round of combat. This Gift is discarded after its effect takes place.

Breath of the Defiled.Name: Breath of the Defiled
Breath of the Defiled.Text: All characters of Renown 1 and 2 are removed from play. Discard this Gift after its effect takes place.

Burrow.Name: Burrow
Burrow.Text: The user burrows into the ground to escape combat. The Metis may not be attacked or called out until she comes out, and may not take any actions during this time. The Metis comes out at the beginning of the next Regeneration Phase. Discard this Gift after its effect takes place.

Call of the Wyrm.Name: Call of the Wyrm
Call of the Wyrm.Requires: Silver Fangs - Get Of Fenris - Galliard
Call of the Wyrm.Text: The Gift user emits an unholy shout that attracts minions of the Wyrm. Wyrm pack alphas must declare their attacks against the character using this Gift for the duration of the Combat Phase. If multiple Calls of the Wyrm are used during the same turn, Wyr

Camouflage.Name: Camouflage
Camouflage.Text: The Wendigo may avoid combat with any Homid form characters. This Gift lasts until canceled.
Camouflage.Errata: Clarification: Homid form characters cannot declare attacks on the Gift user. (2014)

Catfeet.Name: Catfeet
Catfeet.Text: The user of this Gift becomes extremely nimble. For the duration of the current combat, he is agile enough to dodge all Combat Actions except those which specifically ignore dodges. The character may only play Combat Actions of Rage 1 while Catfeet is in play. Discard this Gift after its effect takes place.

Chant of Morpheus.Name: Chant of Morpheus
Chant of Morpheus.Text: The target of this Gift is removed from play until the end of the next phase and may not frenzy for the rest of the turn.

Distractions.Name: Distractions
Distractions.Text: The user may interrupt any Combat Action with this Gift. The target is distracted and may not take his intended action. The target's Combat Action is immediately discarded. Only 1 Distractions may be played per target, per combat. Discard this Gift after its effect takes place.
Distractions.Errata: Clarification: Distractions targets the creature playing the combat action. Distractions can only cancel one Combat Action no matter how many a creature can play per round. Distractions is played at the end of the Establish-Bluff step (i.e. after all bluf

Doppleganger.Name: Doppleganger
Doppleganger.Requires: Glass Walkers - Black Spiral Dancer - Cults
Doppleganger.Text: Target a Garou or human in play. Until the end of the current phase, the Gift user gains any special abilities of the target. The target may still use its own abilities as normal. Discard this Gift at the end of the phase.

Dragon's Breath.Name: Dragon's Breath
Dragon's Breath.Text: The Gift user can do 2 points of aggravated damage to any opponent she's in combat against. Once used, this card becomes a damage card.

Dreamspeak.Name: Dreamspeak
Dreamspeak.Text: The user's Gifts may cross the Gauntlet. This Gift is permanent until cancelled.

Ectoplasmic Extrusion.Name: Ectoplasmic Extrusion
Ectoplasmic Extrusion.Text: The Gift user can lash out and hold any 1 opponent in combat. For the next round of combat neither the Gift user nor the opponent can play a Combat Action. This Gift will not affect a target character whose Rage exceeds the Gift user's by 3 or more. Disca

Every Day is Halloween.Name: Every Day is Halloween
Every Day is Halloween.Text: The Gift user can force any 1 victim in the Hunting Grounds to attack a character or ally of his choice. Combat proceeds at the Gift user's discretion. The Gift user gains no Renown for any kills made by the victim. Discard this Gift after its effect take
Every Day is Halloween.Errata: Clarification: the user decides if or when the Victim withdraws.  updated 9/9/2015

Exorcism.Name: Exorcism
Exorcism.Text: The Theurge may remove from play any spirit whose Gnosis is less than her own Gnosis score. Discard this Gift after its effect takes place.
Exorcism.Errata: Errata: If a spirit is Exorcised while in combat, it is not killed, it is discarded. No one gains Victory Points for killing it."

Eye of the Cobra.Name: Eye of the Cobra
Eye of the Cobra.Text: May not be used during combat. This character makes any one character a part of his pack, even if she could not normally join a pack of that allegiance. Remove the target from her original pack. The target has Loyalty to all creatures in the original pack
Eye of the Cobra.Errata: Original: the user may pull any 1 character from any pack into his own for 1 full turn. The charmed character and user function as a pack for the purposes of attacking and defending, although no additional combat cards are drawn. This Gift may be played a"

Eyes of Hate.Name: Eyes of Hate
Eyes of Hate.Text: The Gift user can force any 1 opponent she is in combat with to become terribly frightened. For the duration of the combat the affected character must always play her lowest Rage, lowest damage Combat Action during each round of combat. Discard this Gift
Eyes of Hate.Errata: See rules on Forced Play. You are forced to play your lowest Rage card first. If there is a tie for lowest Rage, you then select the lowest damage card among tied cards. If there is still a tie, you may choose between the tied cards. 11/30/15"

Falcon's Grasp.Name: Falcon's Grasp
Falcon's Grasp.Text: This Gift grants the user a bone- crushing grip. The Gift user's Equipment may not be destroyed or stolen, but he may still voluntarily trade or discard it. Equipment can still be discarded by its own ability.
Falcon's Grasp.Errata: Anything YOU do that would cause your equipment to be discarded/destroyed won't be stopped by Falcon's Grasp. This includes using it, breaking the rules for using it (such as going to Crinos), etc. Anything your opponent does that causes discard/destroy/s"

Fatal Flaw.Name: Fatal Flaw
Fatal Flaw.Text: The Gift user assesses the weakness of his target by looking into her soul. For the current combat, the user of Fatal Flaw acts at +1 Rage. Discard this Gift after its effects take place.

Feline Grace.Name: Feline Grace
Feline Grace.Text: For the duration of the current combat, the Gift user dodges any of her opponent's Combat Actions whose Rage equals her Combat Action's Rage.
Feline Grace.Errata: Play in combat. discard at end of combat. 11/30/15

Fenris's Bite.Name: Fenris's Bite
Fenris's Bite.Text: The character using this Gift may not be in Homid form. The user's next non-weapon attack is a powerful bite which maims her opponent. In addition to the normal damage, the victim acts at -5 Rage until damage from that attack is healed. A character's Rage may never drop below 1 as a result of this Gift. Discard this Gift after its effect takes place.

Fetish Sundering.Name: Fetish Sundering
Fetish Sundering.Text: The Gift user may destroy any single piece of Fetish or Bane Fetish Equipment with a Gnosis lower than her own. Discard this Gift upon use.

Fortuna.Name: Fortuna
Fortuna.Text: The Gift user grants 1 target incredible luck. For 1 complete turn the target can choose to cancel and replay any 1 exchange of Combat Actions. The Combat Actions are discarded and new ones must be played in their place. Discard this Gift after its effect
Fortuna.Errata: Fortuna must be played before combat cards are revealed. You do not need to play a combat card that round to use Fortuna. 12/5/2015

Gaia's Favored Messenger.Name: Gaia's Favored Messenger
Gaia's Favored Messenger.Text: Play this Gift any time the user is not involved in a combat. The user of this Gift invokes the patronage of Gaia. Under Her care, the Gift user is immediately healed of all damage he currently suffers, including Aggravated Damage. The character using thi

Gaia's Grace.Name: Gaia's Grace
Gaia's Grace.Text: The Gift user communes with Gaia, swearing fealty to Her cause. Until this Gift is canceled, the user is considered +2 Gnosis for the purposes of equipping fetishes.
