Gaia's Vengeance.Name: Gaia's Vengeance
Gaia's Vengeance.Text: Combat Restricted. Play Gaia's Vengeance during the Withdrawal step of combat. Gaia's Vengeance does 8 damage to an Enemy that the Gift user is facing in combat. Use this Gift to mark the damage.
Gaia's Vengeance.Errata: Original: This Gift may only be used by a character who is engaged in combat with an enemy in the Hunting Grounds. Gaia's Vengeance does 10 damage to that enemy as Gaia Herself causes avalanches, rains of stones, and earthquakes, and animates vegetation t"

Gaia's Will Corrupted.Name: Gaia's Will Corrupted
Gaia's Will Corrupted.Text: Combat Restricted. Play Gaia's Will Corrupted during the Withdrawal step of combat. Gaia's Will Corrupted does 5 damage to a Victim that the Gift user is facing in combat. Use this Gift to mark the damage.
Gaia's Will Corrupted.Errata: Original: The ground cracks open, seeping noxious vapors and spewing foul toxins. Any 1 victim card in combat with the Gift user takes 5 damage as a result of this Gift. Transfer this Gift as a damage card when used."

Gateway of the Hyena.Name: Gateway of the Hyena
Gateway of the Hyena.Text: The Garou can use this Gift to make any one Moon Bridge automatically close. This Gift is discarded after its effect takes place.
Gateway of the Hyena.Errata: Gateway of the Hyena can be used to cancel Moon Bridge Escape/Attack/Assault, to prevent the use of Wheel of Ptah until the end of turn or to end any combat initiated with those cards."

Geas.Name: Geas
Geas.Text: The user of this Gift makes any other character immediately enter combat with anything in the Hunting Grounds. The combat must last at least 3 rounds before the Geas target may withdraw. Geas will end any combat in which the target is currently engaged and force him into the combat with the creature in the Hunting Grounds. Discard this Gift after its effect takes place.
Geas.Errata: If a Geas combat is cancelled, Geas is discarded. Geas can only be played during combat if it will also end the combat. Errata: Geas may only be played by Characters."

Ghost Lance.Name: Ghost Lance
Ghost Lance.Text: The Gift user forges a bolt from Wind-spirits and War-spirits and casts it toward an opponent in combat. Targets affected by Ghost Lance may not bluff for the duration of the combat. Discard this Gift after its effects take place.

Ghouling.Name: Ghouling
Ghouling.Text: Only Humans and animals may use this Gift. In order to play this Gift there must be a vampire in the Hunting Grounds or in the Gift user's pack. The user gains +1 Rage,  +1 Health, Loyalty: Vampires and the ability to regenerate. The Gift user must step i

Ghouling (2).Name: Ghouling (2)
Ghouling (2).Text: In order to play this Gift there must be a vampire in the Hunting Grounds or in the Gift user's pack. The user gains +1 Rage,  +1 Health, Loyalty: Vampires and the ability to regenerate. The Gift user must step in to defend vampire Enemies if it is alpha.

Gift of the Porcupine.Name: Gift of the Porcupine
Gift of the Porcupine.Text: The user's fur becomes sharp and bristled. The next opponent who hits the character will take 2 damage. Transfer this card to the opponent to record damage.
Gift of the Porcupine.Errata: Errata: Gift of the Porcupine only triggers off Attacks played by opponents in combat. GotP is regenerated as a damage card, it does not add damage to another. (Revised 2014)"

Glib Tongue.Name: Glib Tongue
Glib Tongue.Text: This Gift gives the user +5 Renown for the current moot vote. Discard this Gift after its effect takes place.

Gnaw.Name: Gnaw
Gnaw.Text: The Gift user strengthens his jaws so he can bite through virtually any substance. If he damages an opponent during the first round of any combat, the opponent must discard one of its pieces of Equipment (opponent's choice). This Gift cannot be used in Ho

Greater Banishment.Name: Greater Banishment
Greater Banishment.Text: The user cancels any Gift. Remove the target Gift from play. Discard this Gift after its effect takes place.

Guidance From Below.Name: Guidance From Below
Guidance From Below.Text: The Gift user may supersede any alpha action and take her own alpha action to immediately attack a Battlefield. This Gift is permanent until canceled.

Heart of Fury.Name: Heart of Fury
Heart of Fury.Text: The target of this Gift may not frenzy. If this Gift is used on a character in frenzy, that frenzy will be canceled and the target will remain unable to frenzy. This Gift lasts until canceled.

Heightened Senses.Name: Heightened Senses
Heightened Senses.Text: The user of this Gift can refuse any challenges, even those that normally cannot be refused. If attacked outside the Combat Phase, the Gift user may draw one additional combat card at the start of combat.  This Gift is permanent until canceled.

Higher Calling.Name: Higher Calling
Higher Calling.Text: For 1 full turn, no Gaia pack characters may attack other Gaia pack characters. Discard this Gift after its effects take place.

Horns of the Impaler.Name: Horns of the Impaler
Horns of the Impaler.Text: The user gains 2 additional Rage. This does not affect their original Rage for the purposes of turning into their Crinos or Battle form. A character cannot use more than 1 Horns of the Impaler at a time. This Gift is permanent until canceled.

Icy Chill of Despair.Name: Icy Chill of Despair
Icy Chill of Despair.Text: The user appears to become larger, looming, shadowy and terrible. No creature of lower Renown than the user may initiate an attack against the user. This Gift lasts until canceled.

Infectious Touch.Name: Infectious Touch
Infectious Touch.Text: The target of this Gift loses 1 Rage and 1 Gnosis. An individual target cannot be affected by more than 2 Infections. This Gift is permanent until canceled.
Infectious Touch.Errata: CLARIFICATION: A creature may have be the target of two Infectious Touch. updated 9/9/2015

Insightful Eyes.Name: Insightful Eyes
Insightful Eyes.Text: Combat Actions played by the user of this Gift may not be dodged. This Gift lasts until canceled.

Inspiration.Name: Inspiration
Inspiration.Text: Play before any round of combat. The user and his packmates are +1 Rage and +1 Gnosis for the following round of combat. Discard this Gift after use.

Invoke Harano.Name: Invoke Harano
Invoke Harano.Text: The Wendigo using this Gift selects 1 character and forces them to feel the hopelessness of Harano. That character may not take any actions other than defending herself for 1 full turn. If it is used during the Combat Phase, Invoke Harano must be used bef

Jam Technology.Name: Jam Technology
Jam Technology.Text: The character may disable any 1 non-fetish item. The chosen item is immediately discarded. Discard this Gift after its effect takes place.

Kiss of Helios.Name: Kiss of Helios
Kiss of Helios.Text: The Gift user immolates herself with magical fire. The Gift user may do +2 damage to the next opponent that hits him (transfer this card as a Damage card) OR he may ignore 1 single Combat Action delivered by a Flamethrower. Discard this Gift after its eff

Kiss of Life.Name: Kiss of Life
Kiss of Life.Text: The Gift user can remove any 1 damage card from a target. Up to a damage 6 card can be healed. Discard this Gift once its effect takes place.

Kiss of the Wyrm.Name: Kiss of the Wyrm
Kiss of the Wyrm.Text: Removes any 1 damage card (up to damage 7) from the target. Discard this Gift after its effect takes place.

Knife Wind.Name: Knife Wind
Knife Wind.Text: The user directs a harsh gust of arctic wind at any target, causing 1 damage. Place this card on the target for the purpose of recording damage.

Leap of the Kangaroo.Name: Leap of the Kangaroo
Leap of the Kangaroo.Text: The Gift user can join her pack in any Battlefield conflict. This character does not count toward the total Battlefield Renown. Also, no additional cards are drawn for this character. Discard this Gift after its effects take place.

Lesser Banishment.Name: Lesser Banishment
Lesser Banishment.Text: The user cancels any 1 Gift that requires Gnosis 5 or less. Remove the target Gift from play. Discard this Gift after its effect takes place.

Lord of the Battlefield.Name: Lord of the Battlefield
Lord of the Battlefield.Text: The Gift user can reduce the number of combat cards drawn by an opposing player in a Battlefield by 1. This Gift is permanent until canceled.

Lord of the Jungle.Name: Lord of the Jungle
Lord of the Jungle.Text: The Gift user can attack any character or ally in play immediately but will not interrupt a combat that has already taken place (like a Sneak Attack). This does not count as an alpha action. Discard this Gift upon use.

Lord of the Realm.Name: Lord of the Realm
Lord of the Realm.Text: The user can remove any and all characters from the Umbra (at the Gift user's discretion), forcing them into the physical world. Discard this Gift after its effect takes place.
Lord of the Realm.Errata: Clarification: this counts as stepping sideways. updated 9/9/2015

Luna's Armor.Name: Luna's Armor
Luna's Armor.Requires: Children of Gaia - Shadow Lords - Silver Fangs
Luna's Armor.Text: The user of this Gift gains 2 Health. This Gift lasts until canceled.

Master of the Pack.Name: Master of the Pack
Master of the Pack.Text: Play as this creature attacks an Enemy. The Garou are called to fulfill their sacred duty as Gaia's Warriors. The Gift user recruits one Garou in play for each point of Renown that he has. They form a temporary pack with the Gift user and join in the atta
Master of the Pack.Errata: Original: The user of this Gift forms a temporary pack for 1 turn. He may recruit 1 Garou in play for each point of Renown that he has. The pack may then act against 1 enemy in the Hunting Grounds. The player draws 2 additional combat cards. The Silver Fa

Merciful Blow.Name: Merciful Blow
Merciful Blow.Text: The user cuffs any 1 opponent she is in combat with. This is played as an attack. It does no damage, but removes the victim from the current combat. This Gift will fail if the victim has a higher Rage than the user.
Merciful Blow.Errata: Errata: Play as an Alternative Combat Action. It has no Rage or damage stat. It may not be blocked or dodged. The target is removed from from combat when it resolves; attacks that resolve at the same speed will work normally. (Revised 2014)

Messenger's Fortitude.Name: Messenger's Fortitude
Messenger's Fortitude.Text: The user outruns his opponent(s) before combat begins. The character acts at -1 Renown until he engages in combat with at least 1 of the attackers from whom he escaped. Discard this Gift after its effect takes place.

Might of Thor.Name: Might of Thor
Might of Thor.Text: The user of this Gift gains 2 Rage in Crinos form. This Gift lasts until canceled.

Mindspeak.Name: Mindspeak
Mindspeak.Text: The user communicates telepathically with 1 other member of her pack. The user and her packmate may join in pack attacks or defenses for the remainder of the turn. Draw 1 additional combat card if these characters enter combat. Discard this Gift after its effect takes place.

Mists of Vengeance.Name: Mists of Vengeance
Mists of Vengeance.Requires: Get of Fenris - Shadow Lords - Wendigo
Mists of Vengeance.Text: The Gift user may immediately enter combat with any Character(s) who has killed a packmate during the current turn. Mists of Vengeance will not interrupt a combat in progress. Discard this Gift after its effects take place.

Moon Bridge Assault.Name: Moon Bridge Assault
Moon Bridge Assault.Text: The character can 'sneak attack' any other character or ally in play, providing they and their target both have Caerns. This sneak attack can also cross the Gauntlet, allowing a physical character to attack an Umbral one. This attack can be played at any
Moon Bridge Assault.Errata: This Gift crosses the Gauntlet; and the user can interact with the target across the Gauntlet.

Moon Bridge Escape.Name: Moon Bridge Escape
Moon Bridge Escape.Text: The Gift user gives any 1 willing character or pack passage over a Moon Bridge. Characters are immediately removed from play until the next Redraw Phase. Discard this Gift after its effect takes place.
Moon Bridge Escape.Errata: Clarification: This immediately ends any combat the Character(s) was in. This is a Moon Bridge and effects that prevent use of Moon Bridges prevent this Gift from being used.

Mother's Touch.Name: Mother's Touch
Mother's Touch.Text: This Gift heals the lowest damage card from the target, up to a damage 4 card. This Gift will heal aggravated damage. Discard this Gift after its effect takes place.

Night terror.Name: Night terror
Night terror.Text: The Gift user may force any 1 pack to skip its next Redraw Phase. Discard this Gift once its effects have taken place.

Odor of Skunk.Name: Odor of Skunk
Odor of Skunk.Text: No creature except spirits and Wyrm creatures will initiate an attack on the Gift user. This Gift lasts until canceled.

Omens Foretold.Name: Omens Foretold
Omens Foretold.Text: Play Omens Foretold immediately after alphas are chosen. The target player must state whom, if anyone, he will attack during that Combat Phase as his alpha action. The player must attack their stated opponent. If their declared opponent dies, the alpha pa

Open Wounds.Name: Open Wounds
Open Wounds.Text: The Shadow Lords mystically opens the wounds of her target, causing them to fester and become infected. The Gift user selects any single damage card on the target; that damage card becomes Aggravated. Discard this Gift after its effects take place.

Opening the Silver Window.Name: Opening the Silver Window
Opening the Silver Window.Text: The Garou can create a lasting opening between the physical world and the Umbra. This opening allows characters to step sideways against a Gauntlet of 5. The portal lasts until the Gift is removed. Only characters who can step sideways can use the Silver
Opening the Silver Window.Errata: while this is in play, all characters that can step sideways can use the Silver Window instead of their caern. Silver Window is not a caern."

Parting the Velvet Curtain.Name: Parting the Velvet Curtain
Parting the Velvet Curtain.Text: The Garou can choose one non-Garou and bring him into the Umbra when stepping sideways. This may include individuals who cannot normally access the Umbra (like Kinfolk). The 'hitchhiker' must leave the Umbra when the Gift user does. This Gift is discarded
Parting the Velvet Curtain.Errata: This gift is permanent and attaches to the target. The target can still step sideways by other means.

Patagia.Name: Patagia
Patagia.Text: Forming his skin into baggy flaps, the Black Spiral Dancer gains limited flight abilities. For the current combat round the Gift user may only be hit by Combat Actions from weapon Equipment. Patagia may not be used by a character in frenzy. Discard this G
Patagia.Errata: You may play Patagia and then frenzy, but it will not work while frenzied. You will have to wait until the frenzy ends to use the effect. Patagia must be played before combat cards are selected."

Persuasion.Name: Persuasion
Persuasion.Text: The user of this Gift gains +1 Renown for moot voting. This Gift lasts until canceled.

Phantasm.Name: Phantasm
Phantasm.Text: Play when this character is attacked. The attacker cannot withdraw. The Gift user heals all damage at the end of each combat round. If the Gift user kills the attacker, instead remove the killing blow and end the combat. The attacker can take no actions e

Poison Tumors.Name: Poison Tumors
Poison Tumors.Text: The Gift user develops pus-filled tumors all over its body. The next opponent to damage the user takes two damage as they burst. Transfer this Gift to mark the damage. Cards which redirect damage to the Gift user will not trigger Poison Tumors.

Possession.Name: Possession
Possession.Text: Target a Victim of equal or lower Gnosis that is not a spirit. The Gift user is now considered to have that Victim's Rage and Health in its Breed form. Gnosis remains unchanged. The Gift user may not be attacked until the affected Victim is killed or remo

Power of the Ways.Name: Power of the Ways
Power of the Ways.Text: When in the Umbra the Philodox increases her Rage, Gnosis, and Health by 1. Discard this Gift when she returns to the physical world. A character cannot use more than one Power of the Ways at a time.

Psychic Blast.Name: Psychic Blast
Psychic Blast.Text: Target a creature with less Renown than the Gift user has Gnosis. The creature loses its printed special abilities except the ability to play Gifts. This does not affect keywords. Discard this Gift at the end of the next Regeneration phase.

Psychotic Hallucinations.Name: Psychotic Hallucinations
Psychotic Hallucinations.Text: The user can cause 1 target she is in combat with to wildly hallucinate. The victim's next Combat Action is a bluff regardless of their Rage. Discard this Gift after its effect takes place.

Purity of Spirit.Name: Purity of Spirit
Purity of Spirit.Text: The Ahroun protects himself from aggravated damage for one complete turn when in the Umbra. The Ahroun still takes any wounds, they simply are not aggravated injuries. This Gift is discarded after its effect takes place.
Purity of Spirit.Errata: May be played at any time, even if you are not in the Umbra. Once it enters the Umbra, no damage taken is aggravated."

Razor Claws.Name: Razor Claws
Razor Claws.Requires: Ahroun - Get of Fenris - Crinos Form
Razor Claws.Text: The character's next claw attack that connects does +2 damage. Place the Razor Claws card with the combat card for the purpose of recording damage.
Razor Claws.Errata: Clarification: RC adds damage to a combat action and is regenerated when that action is regenerated.

Redemption.Name: Redemption
Redemption.Text: The Gift user exorcises an evil spirit from a Bane Fetish. Select one Bane Fetish; if it has less Gnosis than the Gift user it is immediately discarded. Discard this Gift after its effects take place.
Redemption.Errata: This Gift can also destroy items that are both Fetishes and Bane Fetishes, even if it is being used as a fetish at the time."

Remove Gaia's Blessing.Name: Remove Gaia's Blessing
Remove Gaia's Blessing.Text: The user cancels any 1 Gift that requires Gnosis 7 or less. Remove the target Gift from play. Discard this Gift after its effect takes place.

Renew the Cycle.Name: Renew the Cycle
Renew the Cycle.Text: The Gift user invokes great power to recall a vampire to the earth. Select any one vampire in play. That vampire is immediately discarded. Vampire characters may not be affected by this Gift. No victory points are earned for destroying a vampire in this m

Resist Pain.Name: Resist Pain
Resist Pain.Requires: Children of Gaia - Get of Fenris - Philodox
Resist Pain.Text: Cards that would force the user to act at less than their full Rage do not reduce his Rage. All their other effects take place as normal. This Gift is permanent until cancelled.

Revilement.Name: Revilement
Revilement.Text: The patron totem of a pack turns his back on the pack, believing them to have strayed from his path. Select a Pack Totem in play; that Pack Totem is discarded immediately. Discard this Gift after its effects take place.

Rilkean Heart.Name: Rilkean Heart
Rilkean Heart.Text: The Fianna steels herself for the upcoming battle. For the duration of the combat, the character may not be forced to lose the ability to play Combat Actions. Rilkean Heart may not be used during a combat in which the user frenzies. Discard this Gift afte
Rilkean Heart.Errata: The Gift user cannot frenzy for the duration of the combat.

Roar of the Wyrm.Name: Roar of the Wyrm
Roar of the Wyrm.Text: 1 opponent facing the Gift user in combat cannot play a Combat Action in the upcoming round. Discard this Gift after its effect takes place.

Roll Over.Name: Roll Over
Roll Over.Text: The target of this Gift may not attack you or your pack until 1 of your pack members initiates an attack on the target. This Gift lasts until canceled by a member of the user's pack, or until the target is attacked.

Satyr's Wisdom.Name: Satyr's Wisdom
Satyr's Wisdom.Text: The Gift user plays a tune that drives others MAD! Target a character. It may not vote in Juntas this turn.�

In addition, treat the target as having.Name: In addition, treat the target as having Renown 1 when deciding the order of alpha actions. Discard this Gift at the end of the turn.

Savage Fury.Name: Savage Fury
Savage Fury.Text: You and your opponent both enter frenzy. Discard this Gift after its effect takes place.

Failed bluffs played by opponents, which.Name: Failed bluffs played by opponents, which are attacks, are not discarded but are placed as damage cards on the creature which played them.�

Opponents must play at least one Combat.Name: Opponents must play at least one Combat Action per round.�

Scent of Distinction.Name: Scent of Distinction
Scent of Distinction.Text: The Galliard can check any one character in play's Crinos statistics. This can be done to another player's werewolf before they have transformed. This Gift is discarded after its effect takes place.
Scent of Distinction.Errata: Clarication: This card is useless. Never look at it again.

Scent of Sweet Honey.Name: Scent of Sweet Honey
Scent of Sweet Honey.Text: Play this Gift at the beginning of the Equip/Ally Phase. The user plagues the target of this Gift with swarms of bees, clouds of flies and countless other vermin. The target of this Gift may not equip or recruit Allies this Equip/Ally Phase. Discard this
Scent of Sweet Honey.Errata: Play this at the end of the Regeneration phase. It will last the duration of the Resource phase.

Scream of Gaia.Name: Scream of Gaia
Scream of Gaia.Text: The Gift user screams at his opponents, knocking them back with the force of his shout. No damage is done, but all opponents in combat with the user who have less Gnosis than the user may not play Combat Actions during the next round of combat. Discard this Gift after its effect takes place.

Seizing the Edge.Name: Seizing the Edge
Seizing the Edge.Text: In order for a Moot this character calls to fail, a creature of equal or higher renown than the Gift-user must vote against it.  The total number of votes needed for it to fail is otherwise unchanged.

Sense Wyrm.Name: Sense Wyrm
Sense Wyrm.Text: Target a player; that player reveals the top five cards from his Sept deck. If any are Enemies, they are immediately placed in the Hunting Grounds. Reshuffle the player's Sept deck. Discard this Gift after use.

Serenity.Name: Serenity
Serenity.Text: This Gift cancels a frenzy. Discard this Gift after its effect takes place.

Shield of Gaia.Name: Shield of Gaia
Shield of Gaia.Text: The user of this Gift gains a vast attunement to Gaia's rhythms; the Weaver's laws have no effect on her. The user is not affected by any Combat Actions dealt by non-Fetish weapons. This Gift lasts until canceled.

Shriek.Name: Shriek
Shriek.Text: The Gift user emits a loud Shriek. Any opponents in combat who have less Gnosis than the Gift user act at 1 Rage for the next round of combat. Discard this Gift upon use.

Shroud.Name: Shroud
Shroud.Text: The character summons an all-encompassing cloud of darkness which enshrouds any 1 target. This immediately ends any combat involving the target. No other attacks may be initiated against the target. The Shroud dissipates during the next Regeneration Phase. Discard this Gift after its effect takes place.

Shroud of the Jungle.Name: Shroud of the Jungle
Shroud of the Jungle.Text: The Gift user can hide any 1 Battlefield for 1 full turn. The affected Battlefield cannot be engaged for any reason. This Gift will not affect a Battlefield that a character has already engaged.

Silver Claws.Name: Silver Claws
Silver Claws.Text: The user of this Gift gains claws of pure silver. The character's next attack that connects does aggravated damage. Place Silver Claws with the Combat Action for the purpose of recording damage. Silver Claws may not be used in conjunction with weapon attacks or Bite Combat Actions.

Song of Rage.Name: Song of Rage
Song of Rage.Text: The user sends any target with a lower Gnosis than her own into frenzy. Discard this Gift after its effect takes place.
Song of Rage.Errata: Clarification: This is a Full Frenzy (Revised 2014)

Spawning.Name: Spawning
Spawning.Text: This creates an exact duplicate of itself, including both Battle and Breed form. The duplicate does not have the Gift user's special ability and is a Spirit Ally. Any Gifts attached to the Gift user also affect the Spawn. A Character may have multiple cop

Spirit Drain.Name: Spirit Drain
Spirit Drain.Text: The user drains all energy from any 1 spirit in play. The user regains full health and the spirit is discarded and worth no victory points. The user's Gnosis must exceed the Gnosis of the spirit. Discard this Gift after its effect takes place.
Spirit Drain.Errata: Errata: If a spirit is Spirit Drained while in combat, it is not killed, it is discarded. No one gains Victory Points for killing it."

Spirit of the Fray.Name: Spirit of the Fray
Spirit of the Fray.Text: For the duration of the battle, the user of this Gift strikes first, playing and resolving his Combat Action before all others in the combat. Though the user's Combat Actions strike first, his opponents are not necessarily considered to be bluffing. Discard this Gift after its effect takes place.
Spirit of the Fray.Errata: Errata: The user gains the Fast Striking Keyword for the duration of the combat. (2014)

Spirit of the Tiger.Name: Spirit of the Tiger
Spirit of the Tiger.Text: The Gift user can draw 1 additional combat card for any Battlefield combat she is involved in. This Gift is permanent until canceled.

Splinter the Weakened Mind.Name: Splinter the Weakened Mind
Splinter the Weakened Mind.Text: Play on a character who has just declared an attack. That character is now attacking an eligible target of your choice. The Gift user's Gnosis must exceed his target's. A character cannot be made to attack an opponent he could not legitimately attack. Dis

Staredown.Name: Staredown
Staredown.Text: The character using this Gift intimidates any opponent he is facing, immediately ending combat. Discard this Gift after its effect takes place.

Stench of Death.Name: Stench of Death
Stench of Death.Text: Only spirits, Banes, and Metis can attack the user of this Gift. This Gift is permanent until canceled.

Sticky Paws.Name: Sticky Paws
Sticky Paws.Text: The Gift user may steal any 1 piece of Equipment from another character. The user must still meet the requirements of the stolen item before it may be used. If the original owner defeats the new owner in combat, she recovers her property. Discard this Gift after its effect takes place.

Strange Rain.Name: Strange Rain
Strange Rain.Text: The Uktena causes a rain of frogs, roaches, slugs, small squid, etc. Only characters with more Gnosis than the Gift user may be declared alpha for the next Combat Phase. Discard this Gift after its effects take place.
Strange Rain.Errata: Only Characters can be Alpha while this Gift is in effect.

Subjugation of Gaia.Name: Subjugation of Gaia
Subjugation of Gaia.Text: The Gift user can select any 1 Pack Totem, Lunar Phase, or piece of fetish Equipment and remove it from play. Discard this Gift after its effect takes place.
Subjugation of Gaia.Errata: This may also destroy Bane Fetishes.

Suffering Bastard.Name: Suffering Bastard
Suffering Bastard.Text: The target of this Gift must skip their next Regeneration Phase. Discard this Gift after its effect takes place.

Survivor.Name: Survivor
Survivor.Text: The Bone Gnawer using this Gift receives the aid of the Bear Totem to withstand the call of death. The character must suffer one additional damage card beyond those normally required to kill her before she will actually die. This Gift is permanent until canceled.

Swift Reconnaissance.Name: Swift Reconnaissance
Swift Reconnaissance.Text: The Gift user may see her opponents Combat Action before it is played and select her own Combat Action once her opponents has been seen. The Gift user must play a Combat Action during each round of combat. Discard this Gift after 3 rounds of combat have p

Take the True Form.Name: Take the True Form
Take the True Form.Text: The user forces any shapechanger to revert to his breed form. If this kills the character in combat, the victim's opponent gains victory points for the kill. Discard this Gift after its effect takes place.
Take the True Form.Errata: Clarification: Take the True Form is not an attack, and thus may not be redirected. Characters may counter Take the True Form by immediately playing an effect that send them to Crinos (including Frenzy where applicable) - this will prevent them from dying"

Tar Baby.Name: Tar Baby
Tar Baby.Text: Play at the start of combat or between rounds. Opponents that are frenzied or enter frenzy while in combat with the user of Tar Baby lose one Rage until end of combat. Each time an opponent plays an attack, they lose one Rage until the end of the combat.

Taste of Pain.Name: Taste of Pain
Taste of Pain.Text: Select 1 damage card that another character has received. That damage card now becomes aggravated and cannot be regenerated. Discard this Gift after its effect takes place.
Taste of Pain.Errata: CLARIFICATION: The target of Taste of Pain is a damage card, not a creature. updated 9/8/2015"

Tech Speak.Name: Tech Speak
Tech Speak.Text: The Glass Walkers convinces the spirits in her opponent's weapons that she is not the true target. The Glass Walkers may play this Gift on any opponent using non-Fetish weapons. For 1 round, after Combat Actions are exchanged, the opponent is damaged by h

The Badger's Heart.Name: The Badger's Heart
The Badger's Heart.Text: The victim of this Gift has trouble using its Rage effectively. Target a character. It now has its Breed form Rage in all forms. This Gift lasts until it is cancelled or when the target next enters a full frenzy.

Totem Form.Name: Totem Form
Totem Form.Text: The character assumes the form of a powerful Wyrm Totem, and gains 2 Rage, 2 Gnosis, and 2 Health. The character can use any Wyrm Gifts of any Gnosis. Discard this Gift during the next Redraw Phase.

Touch of the Eel.Name: Touch of the Eel
Touch of the Eel.Text: The user of this Gift becomes a conduit of electricity. The next opponent to successfully wound him in combat will take 1 damage. A character can only use 1 Touch of the Eel at a time. When hit, transfer this card as a damage card. Touch of the Eel will n

Toxic Claws.Name: Toxic Claws
Toxic Claws.Text: The user's claws become coated with a foul, deadly ichor. The character's next claw attack that damages an opponent does +2 damage. Place the Toxic Claws card with the combat card for the purpose of recording damage. Toxic Claws can not be used in Homid f
Toxic Claws.Errata: Adds damage to a combat action and is regenerated when that action is regenerated. Improved 2011

Trackless Waste.Name: Trackless Waste
Trackless Waste.Text: The Garou can interrupt any declared attack to cause the target of this Gift to become helplessly lost. Combat is aborted and attacker(s) cannot declare any other attacks for the rest of the turn. Discard this Gift after its effect takes place.

Tribal Wisdom.Name: Tribal Wisdom
Tribal Wisdom.Text: The Gift user may search through her sept deck and bring any 1 Battlefield into play immediately. Discard this Gift after its effects take place. Reshuffle the sept deck when used.

Discard Trick Shot from play while equip.Name: Discard Trick Shot from play while equipped with a Firearm to target a creature in combat. Target's controller discards a random combat card. If the target creature is frenzied, the controller must randomly discard half her combat hand (

True Fear.Name: True Fear
True Fear.Text: The user causes her opponent to be seized with paralyzing fear. For the next round of combat, the target may play no Combat Actions. Discard this Gift after its effect takes place.

Ultimate Argument of Logic.Name: Ultimate Argument of Logic
Ultimate Argument of Logic.Text: Play this Gift at the beginning of any Moot Phase. The Gift user is able to convince her target of even the most ridiculous concept. The target of this Gift must vote the same way as the Gift user does for the current Moot Phase. Discard this Gift after i

Voice of Reason.Name: Voice of Reason
Voice of Reason.Text: The Gift user gains 2 additional votes in all board meetings. This Gift is permanent until canceled. A character can not be affected by more than one Voice of Reason at a time.

Walking between Worlds.Name: Walking between Worlds
Walking between Worlds.Text: The Gift user can step sideways, into or out of the Umbra, regardless of the Gauntlet. Discard this Gift upon use.

Wasp Talons.Name: Wasp Talons
Wasp Talons.Text: The Black Fury using this Gift fires her claws from her hands into her target. Select 1 opponent in combat. The Wasp Talons strike home, doing 4 points of damage to that target. This Gift is played as a Combat Action, and counts as a firearm attack. Trans
Wasp Talons.Errata: See rules for Alternative Combat Actions (Revised 2014)

Wearing the Bear Shirt.Name: Wearing the Bear Shirt
Wearing the Bear Shirt.Text: A character affected by this Gift becomes bellicose and warlike. His frenzies may never be canceled. This Gift lasts until canceled.
Wearing the Bear Shirt.Errata: Both Limited and Full Frenzy are affected by this Gift.

Webbing.Name: Webbing
Webbing.Text: Play before combat starts or between rounds of combat. If target opponent's Rage exceeds the Gift user's, or target opponent is frenzied, combat ends and the target is removed from play until the beginning of the next Moot Phase.

Whelp Body.Name: Whelp Body
Whelp Body.Text: The target of this Gift acts at -3 Rage. The target's Rage may never drop below 1 as a result of this Gift. This Gift lasts until canceled.

Whispering Campaign.Name: Whispering Campaign
Whispering Campaign.Text: Sometimes a word in the right ear is all it takes.  Target a creature. That creature may not join pack actions and pack members may not join it in pack combat. This Gift is cancelled if the affected creature single-handedly kills an opponent of greater re

Wisdom of the Seer.Name: Wisdom of the Seer
Wisdom of the Seer.Text: The player may look at another player's sept hand once per turn until this Gift is canceled.

World of Human.Name: World of Human
World of Human.Text: The Garou can use this Gift to raise the Gauntlet of any caern by 1. A caern cannot have its Gauntlet raised by more than 4 in this fashion. This card remains in effect until the Gift is removed.

Wyrm Hide.Name: Wyrm Hide
Wyrm Hide.Text: The user's skin is coated with a thick black hide. The user gains 2 Health. A character cannot be affected by more than 2 Wyrm Hides at a time. This Gift is permanent until canceled.
Wyrm Hide.Errata: CLARIFICATION: the user may have two copies of Wyrm Hide attached. updated 9/9/2015

Armor of the Ancients.Name: Armor of the Ancients
Armor of the Ancients.Text: The Gift user develops huge chitinous plates all over its body.  It gains +3 Health.  However it moves slowly.  Any dodge it plays is considered a bluff.  This Gift is considered permanent until cancelled.

Crushing Jaws.Name: Crushing Jaws
Crushing Jaws.Text: As long as both are in this combat, the Gift user and target creature cannot step sideways, withdraw, escape or be removed from combat.  Discard this Gift after combat.

Feral Grin.Name: Feral Grin
Feral Grin.Text: Play on an opponent in combat with less Rage than the Gift user.  The target is removed from play for one round of combat.  Discard after use.

Laughter of the Soul.Name: Laughter of the Soul
Laughter of the Soul.Text: The target of this Gift may not take any actions.  Laughter of the Soul may not be played during combat.  Discard this Gift at the end of turn or when the target is attacked, whichever is sooner.

Sense of the Prey.Name: Sense of the Prey
Sense of the Prey.Text: Attach this Gift to its user.  When a combat that this creature participated in ends, it may immediately discard this Gift to immediately attack one of the other participants.

Sense Prey.Name: Sense Prey
Sense Prey.Text: Reveal the top 5 cards of target sept deck.  If any of the revealed cards are Victims, place them in the Hunting Grounds.  The Gift user gains Victory Points from killing Victims this turn.  Shuffle the sept deck after using this Gift.

Thirst for Vengeance.Name: Thirst for Vengeance
Thirst for Vengeance.Text: While the user is in combat with a Rival, it gains 2 Rage and its frenzies may not be prevented.

Perfect Cover.Name: Perfect Cover
Perfect Cover.Text: Target a Caern or Territory. It cannot be targetted by cards, attacked, or destroyed.  This Gift is permanent until cancelled or the Gift user is killed.

Primal Anger.Name: Primal Anger
Primal Anger.Text: The user gains strength from his pain.  The Gift user gains +3 Rage, but takes 1 damage. This damage can only be healed by cancelling this Gift.  This Gift is permanent until cancelled.

Alexandru ThunderRage.Name: Alexandru ThunderRage
Alexandru ThunderRage.Text: When Alpha Alexandru has +4 Renown during the Combat Phase (does not count towards victory points). Alexandru can select Alphas for other packs once per game per pack. Weakness: None.

Gunnar Draugrbane.Name: Gunnar Draugrbane
Gunnar Draugrbane.Text: Can change into Crinos form whenever and as often as you choose. +4 Rage, +5 Health. Weakness: Gunnar cannot use any Gifts.
Gunnar Draugrbane.Errata: Clarification: Gunnar can still use Gifts that were already attached to him like Might of Thor, but cannot play any new Gifts."

Jack Debiltongue.Name: Jack Debiltongue
Jack Debiltongue.Text: Can pack attack the highest-Renown target in play. Up to 10 Renown of pack can join him. Draw 1 additional card for every member of the pack. This attack may be taken as Jack's alpha action. Weakness: None.
Jack Debiltongue.Errata: Clarification: Jack may add up to 10 renown of packmates to the attack. Do NOT count Jack towards the total. Draw a card for each packmember that joins the combat this way. (2014)

Kelly Still Waters.Name: Kelly Still Waters
Kelly Still Waters.Text: Kelly can end any combat after the second round. This can be done once per turn. Weakness: Play combat cards at -2 Rage. Kelly cannot frenzy.

Klaital Stargazer.Name: Klaital Stargazer
Klaital Stargazer.Text: Can use ANY Gaia Gifts of ANY Gnosis. Once per turn he can cancel any one Action card played by a Garou.  Weakness: While imbued with Klaital Stargazer, the Garou cannot participate in any pack actions.
Klaital Stargazer.Errata: Original: Can Use ANY Gifts of ANY Gnosis. Once per turn he can make any 1 Garou forfeit any 1 action.

Leukippes.Name: Leukippes
Leukippes.Text: Play combat cards at +3 Rage against male characters and Wyrm creatures. Her pack's frenzies cannot be cancelled. +3 Health. Weakness: Will not take any actions if a male character is in her pack.
Leukippes.Errata: Clarification: The Rage Bonus applies only to creatures she is targetting with a combat action. If she is facing a male and a female character, she plays cards at +3 Rage only when targetting the male character. The bonus is not cumulative. If she's fight"

Lone Wolf Lupo.Name: Lone Wolf Lupo
Lone Wolf Lupo.Text: You may search through your deck as often as you wish and equip Lone Wolf with any non-fetish Equipment which then cannot be disarmed or destroyed. All Glass Walkers will vote as Lupo does in moots. Weakness: Lupus characters play combat cards at +2 Rage against Lone Wolf Lupo.
Lone Wolf Lupo.Errata: Clarification: If Lone Wolf trades his Equipment to another creature, it is no longer protected from being disarmed or destroyed.  Equipment that must be discarded to be used such as Flak Jacket or Nerve Agent still must be discarded by Lupo to be used. ("

MockMaw.Name: MockMaw
MockMaw.Text: Mockmaw allows you to add 1 to your maximum sept hand size for every kill he makes. Weakness: If the character channeling Mockmaw is ever killed, you must discard your entire sept hand. Your sept hand will also revert to a maximum hand size of 5.

Oisin Mac Gaelach.Name: Oisin Mac Gaelach
Oisin Mac Gaelach.Text: Any Faerie Kin in play become Oisin's allies for the remainder of the game. Oisin can recruit any number of Faerie Kin. Faerie Kin gain +3 Rage while Oisin is in play. Can use ANY Galliard Gift of Any Gnosis. Weakness: After 5 turns of being in play, Oisin and his host Garou are discarded along with any faeries in play.
Oisin Mac Gaelach.Errata: Errata: Oisin targets all Faeries, not just the Faerie Kin. He gives the +3 Rage to all Faeries, not just Faerie Kin. He may take control of Faeries that were controlled with a Kithain Freehold.  (Revised 2014)"

Old Red Eagle.Name: Old Red Eagle
Old Red Eagle.Text: Each pack member can regenerate an extra damage card during the Regeneration Phase. +4 Gnosis. Weakness: -3 Health (never less than 1).

Old Wolf of the Woods.Name: Old Wolf of the Woods
Old Wolf of the Woods.Text: Can Use ANY Lupus Gift of ANY Gnosis.  Play combat cards at +3 Rage when fighting Homids or Kinfolk. Weakness: Cannot use any Equipment.
Old Wolf of the Woods.Errata: Clarification: The Rage Bonus applies only to creatures she is targetting with a combat action. If she is facing a Homid and a Lupus character, she plays cards at +3 Rage only when targetting the Homid . The bonus is not cumulative. If she's fighting a a"

Shogeka Hunter Moon.Name: Shogeka Hunter Moon
Shogeka Hunter Moon.Text: Her frenzies cannot be cancelled. During moots, all Wendigo and Uktena in play will vote as she does. +3 Health. Weakness: Will never vote in favor of Tribal Alliances. You must select Shogeka as your alpha.

Shu Horus.Name: Shu Horus
Shu Horus.Text: Can automatically step into the Umbra. Once per game, Shu Horus can bring his entire pack into the Umbra for 1 full turn. +1 Health. Vampires attack Shu Horus at +3 Rage. Weakness: Vampire attacking Shu Horus automatically frenzy.
Shu Horus.Errata: Errata: Shu Horus can step into or OUT of the Umbra. This counts as stepping sideways and may be stopped by effects that prevent it.  He can allow packmates that can't step sideways to do so with this ability, but it would still be stopped by cards that s"

Yuri Tvarivich.Name: Yuri Tvarivich
Yuri Tvarivich.Text: +8 Renown for any moot votes. +1 Health. Weakness: None.

Battle Quest.Name: Battle Quest
Battle Quest.Text: Play at the end of any turn in which a single character is involved in (and survives) at least 3 different combats. The Battle quest is worth 3 victory points.

Bully's Quest.Name: Bully's Quest
Bully's Quest.Text: Play during the Regeneration Phase. If the character can kill 1 victim of Renown 3 or less without that opponent wounding the character, that kill is worth +2 victory points.

Cynic's Quest.Name: Cynic's Quest
Cynic's Quest.Text: Play this card whenever you are the attacker in a combat and you withdraw after suffering damage and doing none yourself. You gain 1 victory point.

Entrapment.Name: Entrapment
Entrapment.Text: Play this card on any character. That character may now attack any ally directly, as his alpha action. The ally may not refuse the challenge. If the character kills the ally, this card is worth 2 victory points.
Entrapment.Errata: Errata: That character may issue an unrefusable challenge to allies, as his alpha action. Clarification: When the user kills an Ally, this quest is put in its victory pile. (Revised 2014)"

Finding a Mate.Name: Finding a Mate
Finding a Mate.Text: During the Moot phase, this character selects a Victim under 6 Renown that is either an Animal or a Human. This Character may step in and replace the targeted Victim whenever it is attacked. If the target is still alive after 2 full turns, place this ques

Firebrand.Name: Firebrand
Firebrand.Text: Play this card during the Moot Phase before any Moots or Board Meetings are voted upon. If you can talk any member of another pack into voting as you do, regardless of whether the vote passes or fails, this card is worth 1 victory point.
Firebrand.Errata: Effects which force a Character to vote as you wish will satisfy the conditions for Firebrand.

Fool's Quest.Name: Fool's Quest
Fool's Quest.Text: Play when 1 of your characters of Renown 3 or less survives at least 3 rounds of combat with an opponent of Renown 8 or more. The Fool's quest is worth 2 victory points.

Forestry development.Name: Forestry development
Forestry development.Text: Select 1 Gaia caern. As an alpha action you may play the Forestry Development quest. The Gaia pack's alpha can defend. If the defending alpha declines or is killed, the selected caern is corrupted and removed from play. If the Gaia pack has no alpha, anot
Forestry development.Errata: Forestry Development attaches to the user. FD targets a Gaia pack and the creature using it may IMMEDIATELY attack a Caern that that pack controls as if they were a Territory. Additionally, as long as the Quest is attached, Bane Characters in the pack may"

Grudge Match.Name: Grudge Match
Grudge Match.Text: Play this quest during the Regeneration Phase of the game. You immediately select 1 opponent of equal or greater Renown. If your character can kill that opponent within 2 turns, you gain 2 additional victory points for the kill. If the opponent kills you,
Grudge Match.Errata: CLARIFICATION: Select a character or ally in another player's pack that is of equal or greater renown as the target of Grudge Match.  10/24/2015

In the Public Eye.Name: In the Public Eye
In the Public Eye.Text: Dealing with the human world can be tough. Play when you select a Kinfolk or Pentex Executive as Alpha. That Alpha may not be attacked by any Garou or Fera this turn. You may not pass your Alpha action. If your Alpha is alive at the end of the turn, place

Jackal's Quest.Name: Jackal's Quest
Jackal's Quest.Text: A character undergoing this quest must convince a character from another pack to give him a piece of Equipment. Play this card immediately after the Equipment has been traded. This card is worth 2 victory points.
Jackal's Quest.Errata: Anytime you acquire another pack's Equipment through any means, you may play this quest. Even if you cannot use the Equipment, you still may play this quest. This quest may only be played if the creature was part of another pack at the time."

Legendary Leadership.Name: Legendary Leadership
Legendary Leadership.Text: Play immediately after a successful moot vote. If a moot you played passed, your pack gains a number of victory points equal to that moot's Renown requirement.

Martyr's Quest.Name: Martyr's Quest
Martyr's Quest.Text: Play when 1 of your characters dies while killing an opponent of greater Renown. You gain 1 victory point.

Quest For Dominance.Name: Quest For Dominance
Quest For Dominance.Text: Play this card at the beginning of the Combat Phase before alphas are chosen. If you get to take your alpha action first in the Combat Phase, this card is worth 1 victory point. Place it in your victory pile.

Quest of Spirit.Name: Quest of Spirit
Quest of Spirit.Text: A character must single-handedly bind a spirit whose Renown is at least twice her own. If successful, the spirit is worth its full Renown in victory points.

Quest of Valor.Name: Quest of Valor
Quest of Valor.Text: A character undergoing this quest cannot refuse any challenges for three turns. If the character survives, this card is worth 1 victory point per player in the game.
Quest of Valor.Errata: Cards that prevent you from being challenged will not prevent a creature on quest of Valor from being challenged.

Rank Challenge.Name: Rank Challenge
Rank Challenge.Text: This quest cannot be used in Crinos. This Garou or Fera immediately attacks any breed-form non-Metis Garou or Fera of higher renown. No pack actions may be used. Combat ends when either character shifts to Crinos and 'loses', unless either character is fr

Reclaiming the Stolen.Name: Reclaiming the Stolen
Reclaiming the Stolen.Text: Play this card on a character in your pack who has had an item stolen from her. If that character can defeat the new owner in combat or otherwise reclaim her stolen property, this card is worth 2 victory points.

Rescue Mission.Name: Rescue Mission
Rescue Mission.Text: Choose 1 dead Gaia character in a Wyrm pack's victory pile. As an alpha action you may play the Rescue Mission. The Wyrm pack's alpha has the option of defending. If the Wyrm alpha declines or is killed the selected Gaia character is removed from the vict

Rival's Quest.Name: Rival's Quest
Rival's Quest.Text: Play this quest after defeating a character with whom your character has a Rivalry. That kill is worth an additional 2 victory points.

Scourging the Wyrm.Name: Scourging the Wyrm
Scourging the Wyrm.Text: Select 1 character and 1 enemy in the Hunting Grounds. If that character can single-handedly slay the enemy within one turn, that enemy is worth +1 victory points.

Serpent's Quest.Name: Serpent's Quest
Serpent's Quest.Text: Play when an opponent attacks a member of your pack but withdraws after the first round of combat. You gain 2 victory points.
Serpent's Quest.Errata: This may only be played after the end of the first round of combat.

Slash and Burn.Name: Slash and Burn
Slash and Burn.Text: Play this card when you declare an attack against a pack's Territory. If the Territory, is destroyed, you gain 2 victory points in addition to any kills made during the combat.

Talebearer's Quest.Name: Talebearer's Quest
Talebearer's Quest.Text: Play when a Character of Renown 4 or less that is a Bastet, Corax, or Galliard participates in the same pack action as a creature of Renown 8 or higher and survives. Place this quest in your Victory Pile where is worth 3 Victory Point.

Trophy.Name: Trophy
Trophy.Text: Play this quest on any creature. When the user kills a creature in combat, place Trophy in your victory pile where it is worth two VP. The user may steal a piece of Equipment from that creature and equip it if the card's requirements are met.

Umbral Quest.Name: Umbral Quest
Umbral Quest.Text: Play this card on a character in your pack at the beginning of the Moot Phase. The character will immediately enter the Umbra and the Hunting Grounds for 2 turns. If she returns without having taken any damage, this card is worth 3 victory points. Characters in the Umbra may not vote in moots.
Umbral Quest.Errata: Clarification: Umbral Quest can only be used by creatures that can Step sideways. If the creature is removed from the Umbra, the Hunting Grounds, or play before this Quest is over, the Quest is discarded (unsuccessful) and the creature returns to the phys"

Weaver's Quest.Name: Weaver's Quest
Weaver's Quest.Text: Play this quest when 1 of your characters kills another character using a non-Fetish weapon Equipment. This quest is worth 1 victory point.

Mnesis Dreams.Name: Mnesis Dreams
Mnesis Dreams.Text: Play on a Mokole Character during the Umbra Phase.  If it takes no damage for 2 full turns, shuffle a card from your sept discard into your sept deck and place this Quest in your Victory Pile where it is worth 2 Victory Points.

Death of Black Tooth.Name: Death of Black Tooth
Death of Black Tooth.Text: Play this card on any Ajaba, Bastet or Garou Character. That Character may attack the highest renown Bastet in play directly, as his Alpha action. If the character kills the Bastet, put this card in your Victory Pile where it is worth 3 victory points.

Scavenger's Quest.Name: Scavenger's Quest
Scavenger's Quest.Text: Play on a Bone Gnawer, Ajaba, or Corax when combat between another pack's Alpha and a prey creature ends.  This Character may immediately attack the Prey.  It may not use pack actions.  If the prey is killed, it is worth full VP regardless of allegiance a

Allies' Gateway.Name: Allies' Gateway
Allies' Gateway.Text: Select any caern in play. If the owner of the caern consents, your pack and his pack can benefit from both Caerns' advantages until the next Redraw Phase.

Gathering for the Departed.Name: Gathering for the Departed
Gathering for the Departed.Text: This Rite is enacted to honor the recently deceased. It must be called immediately after the death of any character from your pack. The departed character's heroics are sung as her packmates howl her praise. Your pack gains 1 victory point.
Gathering for the Departed.Errata: Errata: play at the end of the combat.

Invocation of Hakaken.Name: Invocation of Hakaken
Invocation of Hakaken.Text: Once a mighty Shadow Lords, Hakaken was seduced by his own pride into serving the Wyrm. Play this Rite on any character using a Shadow Lords Gift. That character must be selected as alpha until the game is over or the character is killed. A pack may only
Invocation of Hakaken.Errata: See Faq entry on can't versus must effects.

Rite of Binding.Name: Rite of Binding
Rite of Binding.Text: Play this Rite on any spirit ally your character has just defeated. The spirit ally is not dead, but instead has been bound and is now an ally of your pack. This spirit ally is worth half of its Renown in victory points.

Rite of Chiminage.Name: Rite of Chiminage
Rite of Chiminage.Text: The Garou using the Rite of Chiminage selects 1 pack with a Caern. He may use that Caern as if his pack controls it. A Gaia Garou may only use a Gaia Caern and a Wyrm Garou may only use a Wyrm Caern. This Rite lasts until it is voted out with a Moot.
Rite of Chiminage.Errata: You can't use this to gain access to a caern controlled by the wrong allegiance, even if it the caern can be used by Wyrm or Gaia.  This rite lasts until it is voted out with a Moot (if targeting a Gaia Caern) or a Board Meeting (if targeting a Wyrm Caern"

Rite of Claiming.Name: Rite of Claiming
Rite of Claiming.Text: When a character enacts this Rite you may choose any caern in play and claim it as your own. The owner of that caern selects one of his Garou to defend his claim. The characters immediately fight for ownership of the caern. If your character can kill the
Rite of Claiming.Errata: Use the rules for attacking territories but only a Garou may act as the initial defender. The Garou doesn't need to be alpha. Other creatures may be drawn in via pack action.

Rite of Glory.Name: Rite of Glory
Rite of Glory.Text: Play this card on 1 of your characters before alphas are chosen. This character immediately enters the Hunting Grounds. If the character survives the current Combat Phase without leaving the Hunting Grounds, she returns and gains 1 Rage and 1 Renown.

Rite of Investiture.Name: Rite of Investiture
Rite of Investiture.Text: Play at the end of a Moot Phase in which 1 of your characters successfully called a moot. You may select any 1 ally and make him a full member of your pack. As such, that ally may now take any action, including voting in moots, that any other pack member may take.
Rite of Investiture.Errata: Errata: This can only be used on Allies you permanently control. Allies that are only temporarily part of your pack will not remain with your pack if targetted with RoI. Once Investitured, the Ally changes card type to become a Character. RoI has no Renow"

Rite of Realm Binding.Name: Rite of Realm Binding
Rite of Realm Binding.Text: This Rite binds its chosen target in the world in which she happens to be. The victim is trapped either in the physical world or in the Umbra and cannot breach the Gauntlet without the assistance of a Rite or action card.

Rite of Renunciation.Name: Rite of Renunciation
Rite of Renunciation.Text: This Rite is performed when a Gaia character wishes to change her auspice. The Rite user selects a new auspice for herself. After changing her auspice, the user of this Rite may ONLY use Gifts of that auspice, and may not use Gifts based on any other requ
Rite of Renunciation.Errata: This will not change a Wyrm creature's Aspect or Caste. Equipment that allows you to play Gifts of X group will still allow you to do so.

Rite of Return.Name: Rite of Return
Rite of Return.Text: This Rite allows your character to immediately return to the physical world in her breed form, regardless of the Gauntlet or closed realm.

Rite of Scarification.Name: Rite of Scarification
Rite of Scarification.Text: When using this Rite the character ritually scars himself with spiritual glyphs and brands. The user's skin actually becomes a Fetish in and of itself, giving the character +2 Health. A character may only benefit from 1 Rite of Scarification at any time.

Rite of Summoning.Name: Rite of Summoning
Rite of Summoning.Text: You may force 1 alpha to declare his attack against the character invoking this Rite.
Rite of Summoning.Errata: This forces the next Alpha to attack you, even if they wished to pass and do nothing. Rites do not cross the Gauntlet, so cannot force an alpha on the other side of the Gauntlet to attack the user if he is not a valid target. It will then affect the next"

Rite of Talisman Dedication.Name: Rite of Talisman Dedication
Rite of Talisman Dedication.Text: The character invoking this Rite binds his possessions to him spiritually. None of this character's Equipment may be stolen from him, nor may he trade it to another character. The Equipment may still be destroyed by effects which will do so.

Rite of the Black Spiral.Name: Rite of the Black Spiral
Rite of the Black Spiral.Text: Play on any Gaia pack Garou this character has just killed. The target does not die, but instead descends into the maddening Black Spiral. The killing damage card is discarded, and the defeated character becomes an ally of the Rite user's pack. The charac
Rite of the Black Spiral.Errata: Rite of the Black Spiral (RotBS) maybe played during combat. RotBS prevents a creature from dying, so effects dependent on the Character being dead will not work (such as Caern of the Snow Leopard or Gathering for the Departed) If facing a frenzied Charac"

Rite of the Pentarch.Name: Rite of the Pentarch
Rite of the Pentarch.Text: Play when you kill a victim in the Hunting Grounds. Instead of gaining victory points, you can add that victim's Renown to any pack member's Gnosis. The Gnosis increase will last for 1 turn only, then the victim is discarded.

Rite of Wisdom.Name: Rite of Wisdom
Rite of Wisdom.Text: Play this card on any 1 of your characters at the beginning of the current Moot Phase, before characters vote on any moots. At least 1 issue must be voted on. This character must always vote first. If the character is on the winning side of all votes, she gains 1 Gnosis and 1 Renown.
Rite of Wisdom.Errata: Clarification: Play this card on any 1 of your Characters at the beginning of the current Moot Phase, after at least one Moot is called. This Character must always vote first. If the character is on the winning side of all Moots she can vote on, she gains"

Rite of Wounding.Name: Rite of Wounding
Rite of Wounding.Text: Play this card after a character declines a challenge. That character cannot regenerate until she engages in battle.
Rite of Wounding.Errata: Clarification: Engages in battle means you were involved in a combat, even if it was cancelled or you fled before first round. (2014)"

Satire Song.Name: Satire Song
Satire Song.Text: The Galliard invoking this Rite must have a higher Renown than his chosen victim. The Galliard performs a song of shame, reducing the target's Renown by 1. Only 1 Satire Song can be placed on a character.

Shattering the Binding Fetters.Name: Shattering the Binding Fetters
Shattering the Binding Fetters.Text: By enacting this Rite the Uktena severs the ties that bind a spirit into servitude. Any single spirit Ally may be targeted by this Rite and is immediately sent into the Hunting Grounds. If the Ally was part of a Gaia pack, it becomes an Enemy; if the Ally

Stone of Scorn.Name: Stone of Scorn
Stone of Scorn.Text: Play this card immediately on any character who declines a challenge. That character loses all voting rights until she engages in battle.
Stone of Scorn.Errata: Clarification: Engages in battle means you were involved in a combat, even if it was cancelled or you fled before first round. (2014)"

The Vigil Forsaken.Name: The Vigil Forsaken
The Vigil Forsaken.Text: The character who enacts the Vigil breaks the rituals of another. The Vigil Forsaken cancels any single Rite in play. A pack may only play 1 Vigil Forsaken per game. Discard this Rite after its effects take place.
The Vigil Forsaken.Errata: This can be played to target a Rite at any time, not just when it has first been played."

Victory Party.Name: Victory Party
Victory Party.Requires: Ahroun - Get of Fenris - Crinos form
Victory Party.Text: Play this Rite immediately after a pack defeats an enemy in the Hunting Grounds of Renown 8 or more. This card is worth +2 victory points.
Victory Party.Errata: Errata: play at the end of the combat. (Revised 2014)

Blood Omen.Name: Blood Omen
Blood Omen.Text: Play after this character killed a Victim. You may look at the top seven cards of target player's sept deck and choose two to discard. Put the remaining cards back on top of the deck in any order. Only one Blood Omen may be played for each Victim killed.

Baptism of Fire.Name: Baptism of Fire
Baptism of Fire.Text: Restricted. Helpful guardian spirits guide you to a shapeshifter cub. When played, search your hand or deck for a Garou or Fera that is of Renown 6 or less. It joins your pack and is now a Character.

Rite of the Opened Caern.Name: Rite of the Opened Caern
Rite of the Opened Caern.Text: Your pack must have a Caern in order to use this Rite. You call upon the Caern's totem to bless your pack. All your Characters and Spirit Allies are +2 Gnosis until the end of the turn. Draw a Sept card during the next Umbra phase.

Burn the Library.Name: Burn the Library
Burn the Library.Text: Target a pack's sept deck.  Discard the top 3 cards.

Rite of the Stone.Name: Rite of the Stone
Rite of the Stone.Text: This ritual binds the power of the Sun into a mystical stone which is then swallowed.  The user gains +1 Rage and +1 Gnosis.  Permanent until cancelled.

Acting Chairman.Name: Acting Chairman
Acting Chairman.Text: If you win this Junta, attach it to the Character that called it: for the rest of the game that Character can call and vote in all Board Meetings regardless of restrictions, even if there are no other Wyrm packs in the game.

Aggressive Agenda.Name: Aggressive Agenda
Aggressive Agenda.Text: If this Junta passes, Wyrm alphas gain Rivalry: Gaia until the end of turn and may not voluntarily pass as their Alpha action. If there are no Gaia alphas in the physical world at the end of the current turn put this card in your Victory Pile where it is

Balance of Gaia.Name: Balance of Gaia
Balance of Gaia.Text: The Philodox calling this vote selects one kill in any Gaia pack's victory pile. If Balance of Gaia passes, that kill is removed from the victory pile and discarded, and the affected pack must attack only Wyrm creatures for the upcoming Combat Phase. If n
Balance of Gaia.Errata: Errata: This may target ANY card in a Gaia pack's Victory pile, not just a kill (errata, 8.14)"

Banishment by the Council.Name: Banishment by the Council
Banishment by the Council.Text: Select an enemy in the Hunting Grounds. On a successful vote that enemy is immediately discarded and worth no victory points. Return the enemy to its owner's discard pile.

Bestowing the Boon.Name: Bestowing the Boon
Bestowing the Boon.Text: Select one Gaia character. If Bestowing the Boon passes, that character may take any single piece of Equipment from any Gaia character in play. The character receiving the Boon must still be able to meet the Equipment's requirements before he may use it.

Beta Status.Name: Beta Status
Beta Status.Text: Select one pack. If Beta Status passes, the affected pack may not gain the benefits of Territories. A pack affected by Beta Status may remove this stigma by defeating any opponent of Renown 5 or higher.

Betrayal of Principles.Name: Betrayal of Principles
Betrayal of Principles.Text: Target a pack with no Victory Points from Wyrm creatures and more VP from Gaia creatures than any other source. If this moot passes, the highest renown character in the targeted pack is discarded. A pack may only be affected by one Betrayal of Principles

Board of Directors.Name: Board of Directors
Board of Directors.Text: Select 1 Wyrm character of Renown 7 or greater. On a successful vote that character becomes a member of Pentex's Board of Directors. This character gains 1 Renown.

Business Merger.Name: Business Merger
Business Merger.Text: Select 2 Wyrm Packs.  On a successful vote these 2 packs have a temporary alliance.  These packs will never willingly attack one another while this truce is in effect.  This Merger can be voted out during any Moot Phase.

Caern Building.Name: Caern Building
Caern Building.Text: This Rite takes the form of a moot, as caern building requires cooperation. Select a player to own the caern. If the vote passes, the player gains 4 victory points. For the remainder of the game, all enemies in the Hunting Grounds will gain 2 Rage when fighting members of the controlling player's pack. The player keeps these modifiers as long as the caern is in play.
Caern Building.Errata: Clarification: Multiple copies of this Caern can be in play, but each pack may only have one. It creates a generic Gauntlet 5 Caern."

Calling a Champion.Name: Calling a Champion
Calling a Champion.Text: Select any character. This character must act as alpha for his pack if this vote succeeds. Only Ahroun may vote during this moot.

Close the Bawn.Name: Close the Bawn
Close the Bawn.Text: A successful vote banishes all spirit allies. Discard any spirit allies in play. Only Homids can vote on this moot.

Cooperation.Name: Cooperation
Cooperation.Text: If this Moot passes, it becomes an Event and remains in play. The sept draw of Gaia packs is increased by 1.�

Discard this card if a member of a Gaia.Name: Discard this card if a member of a Gaia pack kills a member of another Gaia pack.

Corporate Acquisition.Name: Corporate Acquisition
Corporate Acquisition.Text: On a successful vote, control of 1 Wyrm caern is transferred to another Wyrm pack.  Select both the caern and the pack before any votes are placed.

Corporate Downsizing.Name: Corporate Downsizing
Corporate Downsizing.Text: If passed, each other pack must discard an Ally from play. If they are unable to discard an Ally, they may not play Allies, Equipment Territories, or Caerns next turn.

Crusade.Name: Crusade
Crusade.Text: Select a type of Wyrm creature (ex: Fomori). If this moot passes, for the rest of the game Alphas have Rivalry to Wyrm creatures of that type. Gaia creatures act at +1 Rage while attacking the named creature type.
Crusade.Errata: Improved 2011.  Creature types include Garou, Bastet, Vampire, Bane etc."

Culling the Weak.Name: Culling the Weak
Culling the Weak.Text: Target any Wyrm creature. Wounded creatures cannot vote in this Junta. If you win the vote, the target takes 2 Aggravated damage. If this damage kills it and it is not a part of your pack, discard the creature and place this card in your Victory Pile wher

Diminished Worth.Name: Diminished Worth
Diminished Worth.Text: Target a pack. If you win the vote, all members of target pack permanently lose 1 Renown and are worth 1 less Victory Point. Fomori in the targeted pack are instead worth 2 less renown and worth 2 less Victory Points. Creatures can't be reduced to less th

Discredited Lineage.Name: Discredited Lineage
Discredited Lineage.Text: Select a Garou channeling a Past Life. On a successful vote, that Garou must discard the Past Life. Only characters of the same tribe as the accused Garou may vote.

Grand Restructuring.Name: Grand Restructuring
Grand Restructuring.Text: In times like these, radical changes are sometimes the best course of action. If Grand Restructuring passes, all Gaia characters must subtract their Renown from 10. This becomes their new Renown. Grand Restructuring lasts for 1 full turn, and will not aff

Impergium.Name: Impergium
Impergium.Text: If this vote is successful, discard all human Kinfolk allies in play. Only Lupus can vote on this moot.

Jackal's Curse.Name: Jackal's Curse
Jackal's Curse.Text: The Ragabash calling this vote selects any 1 kill from a pack's victory pile. On a successful vote, that kill is discarded and no longer worth any victory points. Only Ragabash may vote on this issue. A pack may only have 1 Jackal's Curse played against it per game.
Jackal's Curse.Errata: Errata: Jackal's Curse can target any card in the Victory Pile, not just kills."

Justice Under Gaia.Name: Justice Under Gaia
Justice Under Gaia.Text: On a successful vote, no Equipment can be used during the next Combat Phase.

Opening of the Moon Bridges.Name: Opening of the Moon Bridges
Opening of the Moon Bridges.Text: On a successful vote, Moon Bridges are opened between all Caerns in play. Now alphas can directly attack any non-alpha character or ally in play (providing the alpha and their target both belong to a pack that has a caern). The target of this attack no lo

Pack Reprimand.Name: Pack Reprimand
Pack Reprimand.Text: Choose a pack with a caern. On a successful vote that pack is punished for poorly managing their caern. That caern is discarded.

Pentex Recruitment.Name: Pentex Recruitment
Pentex Recruitment.Text: You may call this Board Meeting even if there are no other Wyrm packs in the game. Target a Wyrm creature. If this passes, the target gains the keyword Pentex and you may search your deck for a card requiring Pentex and immediately play it.

Promotion.Name: Promotion
Promotion.Text: Target a Character of Renown 5 or less. If this Junta passes, attach this to the targeted character and it acts at +4 Renown during the Moot phase. Pentex Characters have +2 Renown for voting on this Junta. A Character can only benefit from one Promotion.

Public Ridicule.Name: Public Ridicule
Public Ridicule.Text: Target a Wyrm creature. All its accomplishments are mocked, and it loses its confidence. If this Junta passes, attach this to the target and it cannot be Alpha for the rest of the game unless it is the only possible choice.�

Punitive Damages.Name: Punitive Damages
Punitive Damages.Text: Select 1 piece of Equipment held by a Wyrm character or ally.  On a successful vote, that piece of Equipment is immediately transferred to the character calling the board meeting.

Rampage.Name: Rampage
Rampage.Text: If Rampage passes, Wyrm characters must attack the Territories of Gaia packs, if there are any, during the next Combat Phase. If there are no Territories left in play, Wyrm characters may declare their attacks as normal.
Rampage.Errata: Only applies to Gaia controlled territories.

Reinvesting Profits.Name: Reinvesting Profits
Reinvesting Profits.Text: Select 1 card from a Wyrm pack's victory pile. If the board meeting passes, the selected card is discarded. However, that pack gets to draw an additional number of sept cards equal to the discarded number of victory points. These additional sept cards are

Remove Funding.Name: Remove Funding
Remove Funding.Text: Choose an Ally in play. If this Junta passes that Ally is discarded, as are all Allies with the same name that are Allies of Wyrm packs.

Business Caste creatures can instead pla.Name: Business Caste creatures can instead play Remove Funding as an Action that causes a target pack to discard one of the

Research and Development.Name: Research and Development
Research and Development.Text: If this Boardmeeting passes, all Wyrm players immediately draw three sept cards. They discard their whole Sept hand at the end of the turn.

Research and Development (2).Name: Research and Development (2)
Research and Development (2).Text: All Wyrm players draw three additional sept cards into their hand. They discard their whole sept hand at the end of the turn.

Resource Appropriation.Name: Resource Appropriation
Resource Appropriation.Text: The character calling for Resource Appropriation selects any single Territory, in play. If this vote passes, the vote-caller's pack acquires the Territory, even if they cannot meet that Territory's requirements.

Rite of Passage.Name: Rite of Passage
Rite of Passage.Text: For the next Combat Phase, only Garou of 1 or 2 Renown may be selected as pack alphas. If a pack does not have a character of that Renown level, then it has no alpha this turn.
Rite of Passage.Errata: Clarification: If your pack does not have a GAROU of Renown 1 or a GAROU of Renown 2, you have no Alpha. (2014)"

Ritual Challenge.Name: Ritual Challenge
Ritual Challenge.Text: Name 2 characters. On a successful vote, these 2 characters immediately enter combat for 3 rounds. Combat proceeds as normal, except that pack tactics and actions may not be used.
Ritual Challenge.Errata: Errata: BOTH creatures are considered attacker and defender. no additional creatures may be added to the combat and no one may replace anyone in the combat. Combat ends after three rounds. (Revised 2014)

Ritual Of the Dark Spiral.Name: Ritual Of the Dark Spiral
Ritual Of the Dark Spiral.Text: Select 1 non-Black Spiral Wyrm character. On a successful vote that character must walk the Black Spiral. Decide the following randomly (equal chance of each): A - The character is driven mad and destroyed. B - The character gains strength from his madnes

Shining Example.Name: Shining Example
Shining Example.Text: Select a Garou when calling this moot. If passed, the selected Garou is now considered Loyal to his tribe and may double his renown when voting on moots called by members of that tribe. He may not double his Renown on Moots called by his own pack. Only Ga

Silver Record.Name: Silver Record
Silver Record.Text: Select 1 pack in play. A successful vote includes 1 of this pack's exploits in the legendary Silver Record. If the vote passes, this card is worth 2 victory points to the chosen pack. A pack may not benefit from more than 1 Silver Record per turn.
Silver Record.Errata: Clarification: Place this Moot in your Victory Pile if it passes. (2014)

Skindancer.Name: Skindancer
Skindancer.Text: Choose 1 Garou. Successful vote means that the Garou is revealed as being a Skindancer (thus of the Wyrm and considered an enemy card) and is removed from his pack and placed in the Hunting Grounds. If the vote fails, the character calling the vote loses her voting ability for the remainder of the game.

Spirit Pacts.Name: Spirit Pacts
Spirit Pacts.Text: If this Junta passes, for the rest of the game all Spirits bound by Gaia packs are worth their full printed renown in Victory Points, and Gaia packs can bind Spirits even while not in the Umbra.�

Theurges can instead play Spirit Pacts a.Name: Theurges can instead play Spirit Pacts as a Quest that let

Survival of the Fittest.Name: Survival of the Fittest
Survival of the Fittest.Text: Select 1 Wyrm character who has taken at least 1/2 Health in damage.  On a successful vote, combat immediately begins between that character and the character calling this board meeting.  Combat lasts for 4 rounds.

The Litany's Guidance.Name: The Litany's Guidance
The Litany's Guidance.Text: The character who calls this vote declares war on the Wyrm and its followers. If The Litany's Guidance passes, for the remainder of the game Gaia packs will not gain any victory points for killing Gaia pack characters or allies.

The Stolen Wolf.Name: The Stolen Wolf
The Stolen Wolf.Text: Select a Garou. Only members of that Garou's auspice may vote. If passed, the victim reverts to his breed form. He cannot change form until he frenzies. If the vote fails, the victim frenzies and immediately attacks the character who called the vote. Each player draws combat cards and combat proceeds as normal.

Tribal Alliance.Name: Tribal Alliance
Tribal Alliance.Text: Name 2 tribes. Upon a successful vote, these 2 tribes will form a temporary alliance. This alliance lasts until it is voted out during a Moot Phase. While the alliance lasts, members of these 2 tribes will not attack each other. No card is necessary to call a vote to end a Tribal Alliance.
Tribal Alliance.Errata: Errata: This creates Loyalty between members of the named tribes. All X will have Loyalty towards Y, all Ys will have Loyalty to X. You must name two different tribes. Clarification: You may name Garou or Bastet tribes or one of each. (Revised 2014)"

Tribal War.Name: Tribal War
Tribal War.Text: Choose 2 tribes. Characters from these tribes must attack each other before they attack any other opponents in the game. If members of both tribes are in 1 pack, then 1 must be discarded (no victory points will be gained). The Tribal War lasts until a vote is called and passed to end it. No card is necessary to call a vote to end a Tribal War.
Tribal War.Errata: Errata: This creates a Rivalry between members of the named tribes. All X will have Rivalry towards Y, all Ys will have Rivalry to X. You must name two different tribes. You must name two different tribes. Clarification: You may name Garou or Bastet tribe"

War Council.Name: War Council
War Council.Text: Select 1 Gaia Garou. This character is nominated for a prestigious position on the Amazon War Council. This character gains 1 Renown.

Winter Wolf.Name: Winter Wolf
Winter Wolf.Text: A successful vote removes any 1 Garou of Renown 6 or higher from play. This Garou is placed in its owner's victory pile and is worth its Renown in victory points.
Winter Wolf.Errata: Errata: Target a Garou Character of Renown 6 or higher. If this vote passes, remove it from the game and put it into its original owner's Victory Pile. Winter Wolf cannot target prey. (Revised 2014)"

Working the System.Name: Working the System
Working the System.Text: Select a pack in play. If this Junta passes, the pack has taken credit for someone else's work. Put this card in the selected pack's Victory Pile where it is worth 2 Victory Points. A pack cannot call more than one Working the System per turn.

Industrial Espionage.Name: Industrial Espionage
Industrial Espionage.Text: Target a card in any discard pile and a player.  If this Junta passes, that player may play that card once next turn as if it were in her hand.

Opposing Goals.Name: Opposing Goals
Opposing Goals.Text: If this Junta passes, for the rest of the game your alpha may swap alpha actions with another Wyrm Alpha.  The swap must be declared before either creature takes their alpha action.  If this vote fails, Wyrm pack members may only attack Wyrm creatures and

Prove your Strength!.Name: Prove your Strength!
Prove your Strength!.Text: If this Junta passes, members of the targeted pack may not participate in pack actions or step in for other creatures. Damage played on pack members may not be redirected. These effects last until the end of the turn. Only Beast-of-War Characters can vote

Battle of Screaming Mud.Name: Battle of Screaming Mud
Battle of Screaming Mud.Text: Attacker: 10 Renown Black Fury alpha. Draw 4 additional combat cards. Attackers are at 1/2 Rage (round up). Attackers cannot withdraw, however Umbral Escapes cannot be prevented. Only 1 Battle of Screaming Mud may be played per game. Defender: 20 Renown B

Battle of Vista Cataract.Name: Battle of Vista Cataract
Battle of Vista Cataract.Text: Attacker: 15 Renown Wyrm Pack. Draw 1 additional combat card. Attackers may not yield this Battlefield. Defender: 15 Renown Gaia Pack. Draw 1 additional combat card. No Combat Actions of Rage 7 or higher may be played.

Border Territory Skirmish.Name: Border Territory Skirmish
