Border Territory Skirmish.Text: Attacker: 8 Renown Gaia or Wyrm Pack. Draw no additional combat cards. Defender: 8 Renown of the opposite allegiance. Draw no additional combat cards.

The victim of this card may take no actions (other than regenerate), including combat actions, until this wound is healed. Mangle can not be bluffed.

Mantis Form.Name: Mantis Form
Mantis Form.Text: Kailindo. If the character using Mantis Form is not in Crinos form when this Combat Action is played, he enters Crinos form this combat round. Use the character's Crinos form Rage to determine whether or not Mantis Form is a bluff.
Mantis Form.Errata: Can be played by creatures that can't change to Crinos form. They simply don't shapeshift.

Massive Wound.Name: Massive Wound
Massive Wound.Text: Usually only one combatant walks away from a Massive Wound - the one who dealt it.

Nerve Cluster.Name: Nerve Cluster
Nerve Cluster.Text: Kailindo. A vital nerve is struck. For the rest of the combat, the victim of a Nerve Cluster is considered to have a Rage of 1 when playing Combat Actions.

No Escape.Name: No Escape
No Escape.Text: Play when attacker announces that he will not continue combat. Your defending Garou has trapped him, and combat will continue until you choose to end it.
No Escape.Errata: Clarification: Any time the target's pack can withdraw this combat, you decide if they do or not."

Off-balanced Attack.Name: Off-balanced Attack
Off-balanced Attack.Text: The character playing this card plays Combat Actions at -1 Rage for the next round of combat.

Organ Puncture.Name: Organ Puncture
Organ Puncture.Text: A character damaged by the Organ Puncture cannot initiate combat until this wound is healed. He can, however, defend if attacked.

Overbear.Name: Overbear
Overbear.Text: Requires: no weapon. You pin down an opponent so you and your pack can savage him.  The creature damaged by Overbear may not block or dodge any attacks the next round of combat.

Overextended Attack.Name: Overextended Attack
Overextended Attack.Text: Your character may not play a Combat Action next round. If your character is the attacker, she may not withdraw before the next round.

Pack Defense.Name: Pack Defense
Pack Defense.Text: Play before one of your defending characters begins combat. You may pull members from your pack into the combat, up to 15 Renown worth of characters (including the original character). You may draw 1 additional combat card per additional pack member.
Pack Defense.Errata: Creates a pack action. Creates a pack defense. Clarification: Played in the pre-combat step. (Revised 2014)

Painful Slash.Name: Painful Slash
Painful Slash.Text: You slash open your opponent's throat, causing massive blood loss. �A creature damaged by this card may not play more than one combat action per round until this wound is healed.

Passive Aggression.Name: Passive Aggression
Passive Aggression.Text: Kailindo. If your opponent's Combat Action is Rage 5 or lower during the round you play Passive Aggression, he is affected by his own Combat Action.

Planned Strike.Name: Planned Strike
Planned Strike.Text: The damage from this card cannot be redirected to a different target by any means.

Combat Restricted. You take a moment to.Name: Combat Restricted. You take a moment to load in another clip. Draw 3 combat cards.

Reckless Swing.Name: Reckless Swing
Reckless Swing.Text: If this attack is dodged your character cannot play a combat action during the next round of combat.

Redirected Attack.Name: Redirected Attack
Redirected Attack.Text: * If your Gnosis is greater than your opponent's, he is affected by his own combat action. This combat action is only playable in the Umbra.

Reinforcements.Name: Reinforcements
Reinforcements.Text: Any time after the third round of combat you may add up to 10 Renown worth of characters to the combat. This becomes a pack action. You may use Reinforcements to add characters to a Battlefield combat. Draw 2 additional combat cards.
Reinforcements.Errata: Can be used in normal combat, not just Battlefields. You may opt not to bring in additional creatures and just draw 2 cards.  If you draw in additional creatures, this creates a pack action and may be either a pack attack or pack defense.  If you just dra"

Rend and Tear.Name: Rend and Tear
Rend and Tear.Text: Your character gives his victim a heapin' helpin' of visceral agony.

Rent Asunder.Name: Rent Asunder
Rent Asunder.Text: You tear some fool in half. Only usable in Crinos form. This card cannot be bluffed.

Ribs Crushed.Name: Ribs Crushed
Ribs Crushed.Text: A character damaged by Ribs Crushed cannot bluff until this wound is healed.

Riposte.Name: Riposte
Riposte.Text: The character playing this Combat Action must be equipped with a Klaive or Grand Klaive. The character parries (dodges) any single attack dealt during this round of combat, and gives a quick follow-up strike.

Run Like Hell.Name: Run Like Hell
Run Like Hell.Text: When this card is played on a character, she immediately flees, exiting combat. A character cannot participate in moot voting until she has engaged in combat with the foe or foes she ran from. This effect takes place after your opponent's Combat Action is resolved.

Sacrifice.Name: Sacrifice
Sacrifice.Text: The creature playing Sacrifice may not play Combat Actions next round.

Sap Spirit.Name: Sap Spirit
Sap Spirit.Text: Sap Spirit cannot be Blocked. This combat action is only playable in the Umbra.

Savage Beatdown.Name: Savage Beatdown
Savage Beatdown.Text: If Savage Beatdown damages a frenzied creature, its controlling player must randomly discard half their combat hand (round up).

If played by a frenzied creature, discar.Name: If played by a frenzied creature, discard a random combat card from your hand at the end of the Bluff step.

Shieldmate.Name: Shieldmate
Shieldmate.Text: Play this card when 1 of your characters is attacked. 1 packmate joins your character in pack defense as a shieldmate. Draw 1 additional combat card. You may choose whether your opponent's Combat Actions are played on the defender or the shieldmate.
Shieldmate.Errata: Creates a pack action.  Creates a pack defense. Clarification: Whenever the attacker assigns a Combat Action or face-down combat card to the defender or shieldmate, you can choose either as the target. You can choose a different target each time. (Revised"

Solid Blow.Name: Solid Blow
Solid Blow.Text: A well-placed blow to the opponent's chin.

Stinging Wound.Name: Stinging Wound
Stinging Wound.Text: If your opponent is damaged by this attack, he may play Combat Actions at +1 Rage for the next round.

Stunning Strike.Name: Stunning Strike
Stunning Strike.Text: Requires: not frenzied. Fast Striking. If this attack damages a Frenzied creature, its frenzy ends at the end of the current combat round, and the character may not frenzy again during this combat.

Submission Hold.Name: Submission Hold
Submission Hold.Text: Requires: not frenzied. If Submission Hold damages a non-frenzied creature, that creature is removed from combat at the end of this round. If Submission Hold damages a frenzied creature, that creature cannot dodge next round.

You unexpectedly lash out at your oppone.Name: You unexpectedly lash out at your opponent, catching him by surprise. This attack can not be Dodged. Sucker Punch does +2 damage if it is a bluff.

Superior tactics.Name: Superior tactics
Superior tactics.Text: You may choose 2 of your characters involved in a Battlefield conflict. Your opponent can only play Combat Actions against these two characters. If either one of these characters dies, your opponent can play Combat Actions against any of your characters i

Surprise Ally.Name: Surprise Ally
Surprise Ally.Text: Play before the first round of a combat (including right after a challenge has been accepted) or between rounds of a combat. You may draw in one additional pack member for a pack attack. Draw 1 additional combat card. Surprise Ally can only be used during combats in which your character is the attacker.
Surprise Ally.Errata: Creates a pack action.  Creates a pack attack.

Surprise Attack.Name: Surprise Attack
Surprise Attack.Text: If this card damages an opponent during the first round of combat, the victim will inflict no damage this round.
Surprise Attack.Errata: Clarification: If 2 Surprise attacks resolve against each other, neither creature will do damage. Surprise Attack cannot cancel damage from a Fast Striking effect unless Surprise Attack was also Fast Striking. Errata: Surprise Attack only prevents damage"

Swipe.Name: Swipe
Swipe.Text: A tough, scraping blow that rarely fails to draw blood.

Tag Teaming.Name: Tag Teaming
Tag Teaming.Text: Tag Teaming deals +2 damage if another Tag Teaming has been played by any of your pack members in this combat. If two Tag Teamings are played by two of your pack members simultaneously, both receive the bonus.

Taking the Death Blow.Name: Taking the Death Blow
Taking the Death Blow.Text: Play this card when 1 of your characters receives a mortal wound. Select another member of your pack to take the wound instead. Combat may continue with the new pack member in place of the old. The new pack member is considered to be alpha for the remainder of the Combat Phase.
Taking the Death Blow.Errata: Errata: Taking the Death Blow can only be used if both creatures could be in a pack action. This effect will not cross the Gauntlet. The original target of the attack escapes combat if able. If not, it creates a pack action. The creature using Taking the"

Telling Blow.Name: Telling Blow
Telling Blow.Text: If the damage from this card immediately kills your opponent, place Telling Blow in your victory pile for an additional 3 victory points.
Telling Blow.Errata: Clarification: This must be the killing blow to take effect. If a Prey Creature kills someone with a Telling Blow, TB has no special effect."

Umbral Escape.Name: Umbral Escape
Umbral Escape.Text: Fast Striking. This creature steps sideways into the Umbra, even if it a creature type that could not normally step sideways. It returns from the Umbra at the start of the next turn. If you play Umbral Escape for a member of your pack, remove Umbral Escap
Umbral Escape.Errata: Errata: Spirits can Umbral Escape: they then exist only in the Umbra. You must return to the real world after using Umbral Escape, even if you have a caern. Clarification: If an opponent Umbral Escapes and you wish to follow, play Umbral Escape as your Co"

Umbral Flurry.Name: Umbral Flurry
Umbral Flurry.Text: All things possess a living energy. A true warrior learns to harness his - and his opponent's. In the Umbra, spirit is energy. This combat action is only playable in the Umbra.

Vital Blow.Name: Vital Blow
Vital Blow.Text: You strike your opponent in the, er, um.. sensitive area. For the next round of combat, your opponent is considered to have a Rage of 1 for the purposes of playing Combat Actions.

Full Auto.Name: Full Auto
Full Auto.Text: Creatures attempting to dodge just throw themselves in the way of this blistering attack.  If played with a Firearm, this attack cannot be dodged and does 2 damage if the target played a dodge this round.

Head Butt.Name: Head Butt
Head Butt.Text: If any damage from this card is blocked, this card becomes a damage card on the creature that played it, unless it is a Mokole.

Pistol Whip.Name: Pistol Whip
Pistol Whip.Text: Requires: Firearm. Fast Striking. You surprise your opponent with a smack upside the head.  If this card damages its target, Fast Striking Combat Actions played by the target this round do no damage.

Searching for Weakness.Name: Searching for Weakness
Searching for Weakness.Text: You take a moment to consider your opponent's battle plans before attacking.  The creature playing Searching for Weakness acts at +2 Rage for the duration of combat, but cannot frenzy during this combat.

Tail Lash.Name: Tail Lash
Tail Lash.Text: Tail Lash does +4 damage if played by a Rokea or Mokole not in Homid form.  It may not be used with a weapon.

Duck and Cover.Name: Duck and Cover
Duck and Cover.Text: Instinctive. Dodge one attack.  If a creature is prevented from playing a combat action, she may play Duck and Cover in the Reveal Step. Treat Duck and Cover as if it were played normally.

Whiplash.Name: Whiplash
Whiplash.Text: If Whiplash does damage, the creature that played it and the creature damaged by Whiplash may not play Gifts for the rest of this combat.

Possessed Dik-Dik.Name: Possessed Dik-Dik
Possessed Dik-Dik.Text: Animal. Fomori. Combat actions played by the Possessed Dik-Dik have Fast Striking.

Bitter Hatar.Name: Bitter Hatar
Bitter Hatar.Text: Ananasi. This lone werespider is more aligned to the Wyrm than its kind would admit. You may play this creature from your hand as an Ally requiring Ananasi.

Big Game Hunter.Name: Big Game Hunter
Big Game Hunter.Text: Human. The Big Game Hunter can only play one Combat Action of Rage 6 or more per combat. He is considered armed with a firearm and acts at Rage 3 if disarmed.

Ootani Oil Bane.Name: Ootani Oil Bane
Ootani Oil Bane.Text: Bane Spirit. Attackers may not withdraw from combat with an Ootani until they have no more combat cards in hand. Weapons cannot be used in combat with an Ootani.

Vhujunka.Name: Vhujunka
Vhujunka.Text: Monster. While your pack is fighting a Vhujunka, discard a random combat a card from your hand at the beginning of each combat round. If the Vhujunka is an Ally, this ability affects its own pack instead.

Illganwasi baKalahari.Name: Illganwasi baKalahari
Illganwasi baKalahari.Text: Spirit. This spirit swarm engulfs opponents, attacking from all sides.  Pack actions may not be used when facing Illganwasi baKalahari. It may never lose the ability to play a Combat Action. It may use Wendigo Gifts.

Famine Gaffling.Name: Famine Gaffling
Famine Gaffling.Text: Spirit. If Famine Gaffling survives a combat against a player's packmembers, the player's sept hand size is reduced by 1 next turn.

Elethoi.Name: Elethoi
Elethoi.Text: Spirit. Elethoi are spirits that live in the eye of storms and can only be affected by Gifts and Umbral attacks. This bane can use Umbra-only Combat Actions against opponents not in the Umbra. It cannot be bound or controlled.

First Team #36.Name: First Team #36
First Team #36.Text: Fomori. Pentex. While in combat with First Team #36, Gaia Characters act at -3 Gnosis and cannot use Crinos-only Combat Actions. The First Team is armed with a Firearm and cannot be disarmed.

Endron Security Team.Name: Endron Security Team
Endron Security Team.Text: Human. Pentex. Unique. No other Pentex Enemy may be attacked while a Security Team is in the Hunting Grounds. The Security Team is armed with a 9mm Pistol. If disarmed it acts at Rage 3.

Lord Choke, Master of Smog
Smrtihara, Eater of Memory\tAhadi-Sahel\.Text: Each time your pack wins a Board Meeting it called, you may place it face down in your Victory Pile where it is worth 1 VP.

Apophis Pipeline Protesters.Name: Apophis Pipeline Protesters
Apophis Pipeline Protesters.Text: Human. While the Protesters are in the Hunting Ground, prey cannot attack anyone.

A Satisfied Ego.Name: A Satisfied Ego
A Satisfied Ego.Text: Play on a Garou or Fera. When you win a Junta this Character called, place this card in your Victory Pile where it is worth 2 VP.

Culling the Herd.Name: Culling the Herd
Culling the Herd.Text: This Character supports starting a new Impergium to wipe out the humans. Play after a Red Talon or Ratkin in your pack kills a Human: put this card in your Victory Pile where it is worth 2 VP. Discard it from your Victory Pile, except during combat, to di

Deny the Hungry.Name: Deny the Hungry
Deny the Hungry.Text: Cannot be used during combat. Destroy target Territory and its controller must discard 2 random cards from his combat hand. Discard this Gift after use.

Fog.Name: Fog
Fog.Text: Opponents cannot use Combat Events while attacking your non-alpha pack members. Discard Fog to cancel a Combat Event played by a creature attacking either a Victim or a member of your pack.

Devour the Dead.Name: Devour the Dead
Devour the Dead.Text: Fomori may use this Rite as if they were Cultists. In a turn in which an opponent killed one of your pack members, you may target them with this Rite. Place this Rite in the target's Victory Pile where it is worth -3 VP.

Scavenger.Name: Scavenger
Scavenger.Text: Spirit. Once per Moot Phase, you may place an Ally from your discard pile under the Scavenger. In any other phase, exchange a card in your hand for a card under Scavenger. Discard all cards under Scavenger if it is killed.

Lord Ebola.Name: Lord Ebola
Lord Ebola.Text: Lord Ebola is a carrier for some of the most virulent diseases in Africa. His opponents in combat act at -1 Rage for each damage card on them.

Aggressive Bite.Name: Aggressive Bite
Aggressive Bite.Text: Requires: not in Homid form. If the user is not frenzied, a creature wounded by this card cannot escape, step sideways or be removed from combat until after the next round of combat.

Dust Storm.Name: Dust Storm
Dust Storm.Text: Requires: Fera. The Fera summon up the punishing winds of the Kamsin to disorient their foes and protect what is rightfully theirs. Play in the Combat Phase after Alphas are announced but before the first alpha action is taken. Target player discards his

Mangle.Name: Mangle
Mangle.Text: Crinos form only. Cannot be bluffed. Slow Striking. The creature wounded by Mangle cannot take any actions (including Combat Actions) until the end of the next combat round.
Mangle.Errata: Original: Only usable in Crinos form. The victim of this card may take no actions (other than regenerate), including combat actions, until this wound is healed. Mangle can not be bluffed."

Furmling.Name: Furmling
Furmling.Text: An elemental spirit of balefire infests the area. All damage from a Furmling is aggravated.
Furmling.Errata: Original: An elemental of balefire infests the area.  All damage from a Furmling is aggravated.

Dreamspeaker Mage.Name: Dreamspeaker Mage
Dreamspeaker Mage.Text: Restricted. Once per turn the Dreamspeaker can step sideways and/or can cancel any single Gift. If in combat, she may not step sideways until one round of combat has passed. The Mage can use Auspice Gifts.
Dreamspeaker Mage.Errata: Original: Once per turn the Dreamspeaker can freely enter or leave the Umbra during any phase of the game. Once per turn, the Dreamspeaker can cancel any one Gift. The Dreamspeaker can use any Auspice Gifts."

Animal Attraction.Name: Animal Attraction
Animal Attraction.Text: The Character may make himself irresistible to others.  Target non-Spirit Ally becomes a member of your pack.  This cannot be played in combat or during the combat phase.

Ghouled Bubasti Kinfolk.Name: Ghouled Bubasti Kinfolk
Ghouled Bubasti Kinfolk.Text: (Regenerates.) Unique.  Animal.  Kinfolk.  The Followers of Set enslaved all the Bubasti's feline kin millenia ago.  Ghouled Bubasti Kinfolk can play two Combat Actions a round if both are Rage 2 or less.  It may use Bubasti and Feline Gifts. Loyalty: Vam

Mosquito Swarm.Name: Mosquito Swarm
Mosquito Swarm.Text: Spirit.  Animal. When you play Mosquito Swarm you may search your deck for all copies of Mosquito Swarm and play them.  Whenever this Ally enters combat it may automatically pack attack and defend with other Mosquito Swarms in your pack.  Draw a combat ca

Caern of the Bloodied Rock.Name: Caern of the Bloodied Rock
Caern of the Bloodied Rock.Text: The inhabitants of this caern in the Kalahari desert carefully stockpile supplies for when times are bad.   This pack may not be forced to skip its Redraw phase nor be prevented from drawing cards.

Sept of the Last Stone.Name: Sept of the Last Stone
Sept of the Last Stone.Text: This Caern of Strength sits at the mouth of the Nile where members of many tribes mingle together.  Your pack members gain 1 Rage for every tribe in your pack, to a maximum of +3.

Den Realm.Name: Den Realm
Den Realm.Text: Den Realms are guarded by lone Bastet, making them fragile.  Any creature in your pack (including allies) may step sideways using the Den Realm, even if it cannot normally step sideways. The Den Realm is both a Caern and a Territory.

Lord Hakimu.Name: Lord Hakimu
Lord Hakimu.Text: The King of the Maiy\�o Simba is a master of spiritual warfare. Once per turn, Hakimu can grant any creature the ability to play Umbra only combat actions in the real world until end of turn.

Mapute.Name: Mapute
Mapute.Text: Mapute petitioned his tribe to form a war party - a Tekla - against those who killed his parents. Bastet in his pack have Loyalty: Bagheera and Rivalry: Garou

Heckles-the-Wyrm.Name: Heckles-the-Wyrm
Heckles-the-Wyrm.Keywords: Garou - Get of Fenris - Ragabash - Metis - Male - Gaia
Heckles-the-Wyrm.Text: If the top card of your combat discard pile is a Combat Action of Rage 1 or less, Heckles may play it as if it was in your hand.

Enbata Lord-of-Thorny-Skies.Name: Enbata Lord-of-Thorny-Skies
Enbata Lord-of-Thorny-Skies.Text: Iksakku.  Embata is convinced the Simba will repeat Black Tooth�s reign of terror and betray the Ahadi.  He must vote against Juntas called by Simba.  Rivalry: Simba.

Firyal the Whip.Name: Firyal the Whip
Firyal the Whip.Text: Between combat rounds, if Firyal does not have a weapon and she played an attack of Rage 2 or less in the previous round, draw a combat card.

Kiva.Name: Kiva
Kiva.Text: As one of the architects of the Ahadi, Kiva is accorded great respect by Fera and Garou alike.  Once per turn, she may interupt a Gaia Alpha�s action and allow another Gaia Alpha to take its action first.

Treerose.Name: Treerose
Treerose.Text: If another Bastet is your Alpha, target a player.  The target must state whom his Alpha will attack during that Combat Phase;  the Alpha must attack his stated target if possible.

Shreds-the-Surface.Name: Shreds-the-Surface
Shreds-the-Surface.Text: If Shreds is Alpha and there is a Pentex Enemy in the Hunting Grounds at the end of turn, he may take a second Alpha action. He can use Red Talon Gifts. Rivalry: Pentex.

Spotless.Name: Spotless
Spotless.Text: At the end of any combat he is in, Spotless regenerates (as if it was the regeneration phase). He cannot declare an attack or join pack actions while wounded.

Tear.Name: Tear
Tear.Text: Tear lost her cub early and now acts as foster mother to orphaned Bagheera. Loyalty: Bagheera

Thunder's Sovereign.Name: Thunder's Sovereign
Thunder's Sovereign.Text: A member of the Amadu'o Simba, once per turn he may call a Renown 8 moot to give all Bastet characters +2 Gnosis and +2 Health. He can/'t vote in other Junta until this has passed.

Zari.Name: Zari
Zari.Text: Quests played on Zari are worth +1 VP when placed in your Victory Pile.  Loyalty: Bastet.

Tongue-Biter.Name: Tongue-Biter
Tongue-Biter.Keywords: Bastet - Hellcat - Metis - Eater-of-Souls - Female - Wyrm
Tongue-Biter.Text: Once per turn, Tongue-Biter can cause a Bastet to lose 2 Renown for voting purposes.

Blood-of-Witches.Name: Blood-of-Witches
Blood-of-Witches.Keywords: Bastet - Hellcat - Metis - Defiler - Female - Wyrm
Blood-of-Witches.Text: Blood-of-Witches may recruit allies with Uktena prerequisites. Rivalry: Black Spiral Dancers.

Maze-Eater.Name: Maze-Eater
Maze-Eater.Keywords: Bastet - Hellcat - Metis - Beast-of-War - Male - Wyrm
Maze-Eater.Text: Maze-eater is wildly unstable. Whenever an effect would change his Rage, it instead changes his Gnosis and vice versa.

Thornclaw.Name: Thornclaw
Thornclaw.Keywords: Bastet - Hellcat - Feline - Beast-of-War - Female - Wyrm
Thornclaw.Text: Thornclaw�s attacks has Wyrm tainted claws. Wounded opponents in combat with him act at -2 Gnosis until the end of combat.  Thornclaw cannot use weapons.

Rene the Demonkeeper.Name: Rene the Demonkeeper
Rene the Demonkeeper.Keywords: Bastet - Hellcat - Homid - Eater-of-Souls - Male - Wyrm
Rene the Demonkeeper.Text: Rene may automatically pack attack with any Spirit Allies in his pack. Draw a combat card when Rene joins the combat.

Burning Water.Name: Burning Water
Burning Water.Keywords: Bastet - Hellcat - Homid - Beast-of-War - Male - Wyrm
Burning Water.Text: Burning Water does aggravated damage against Mokole and Rokea. Rivalry: Mokole.

Calamity.Name: Calamity
Calamity.Keywords: Bastet - Hellcat - Metis - Eater-of-Souls - Female - Wyrm
Calamity.Text: Calamity sows misfortune wherever he goes. When Calamity is Alpha, he may choose an alpha to take its Alpha action last.

Scatterer-of-Bones.Name: Scatterer-of-Bones
Scatterer-of-Bones.Keywords: Bastet - Hellcat - Homid - Defiler - Female - Wyrm
Scatterer-of-Bones.Text: Scatterer-of-Bones has a taste for human flesh. She will never withdraw from combat against a creature in Homid form.

The Darkness.Name: The Darkness
The Darkness.Keywords: Bastet - Hellcat - Feline - Beast-of-War - Male - Wyrm
The Darkness.Text: The Darkness believes he is a great Simba hero reborn.  He may use any card requiring Simba.  All Simba gain Rivalry: The Darkness.

CHARGE!!!.Name: CHARGE!!!
CHARGE!!!.Text: When this card is revealed, the user may change to Crinos or Breed form.  The user's Combat Actions may not be blocked next round.

Lobotomy.Name: Lobotomy
Lobotomy.Text: While a creature is wounded by Lobotomy, it has a Gnosis of zero.

Spite.Name: Spite
Spite.Text: When Spite is revealed, its damage becomes that of a Combat Action played by a Rival this round if possible. If Spite's target is a Rival and it is dodged, block or cancelled, you may return this card to your combat hand during the Withdrawal step.

Bitter Blow.Name: Bitter Blow
Bitter Blow.Text: If the target of this attack is a rival, this attack cannot be dodged.

Harry.Name: Harry
Harry.Text: You may have any number of this card in your combat deck.  If this Combat Action is dodged, blocked or cancelled and it was played in Animal form, you may return this card to your combat hand during the Withdrawal step.

Vicious Assault.Name: Vicious Assault
Vicious Assault.Text: If Vicious Assault is not blocked and is played by a creature with 8 or more Rage, it does 3 extra damage.

No Fooling.Name: No Fooling
No Fooling.Text: Play to cancel an Action card played this combat (this will not cancel Combat Actions) OR Play between rounds of combat to draw 2 combat cards.

The Whole Nine Yards.Name: The Whole Nine Yards
The Whole Nine Yards.Text: Play before the first round of combat. This Character enters full frenzy. This frenzy cannot be prevented. When played, all other pack members are immediately removed from play until the end of combat.  Remove The Whole Nine Yards from the game after it i

Cornered Rat.Name: Cornered Rat
Cornered Rat.Text: Limited Frenzy.  Cannot be prevented.  This packmember enters a limited frenzy (even if creatures of its type cannot frenzy).  Draw 3 combat cards.  While frenzied, whenever this creature deals 3 or more damage to the same opponent in any round it escapes

Spring the Trap.Name: Spring the Trap
Spring the Trap.Text: Play between rounds of combat.  A pack member in Animal form may join the combat.  If it does, this creates a pack action.  Draw a combat card.  In the next combat round, Combat Actions played by the creature that joined the combat are Fast Striking.

Cub's Cry.Name: Cub's Cry
Cub's Cry.Text: Play between rounds of combat. Each pack member loyal to this creature joins the combat.  If any do, this creates a pack action.  Draw 2 combat cards for each creature that joined combat in this way.

Soul Drinker.Name: Soul Drinker
Soul Drinker.Text: Spirit. While a Soul Drinker in the Hunting Grounds:  Gaia players may not play a Gift or Rite if a copy of that card is in their Sept discard pile; and Wyrm alphas act at +2 renown when determining order of Alpha actions.

Stoneheaded Mace.Name: Stoneheaded Mace
Stoneheaded Mace.Text: Weapon.  The user plays Combat Actions at +2 Rage in Homid form and +3 rage in Crinos form.

The Breaking of the Storm.Name: The Breaking of the Storm
The Breaking of the Storm.Text: Restricted.  May not be played during a combat. A horrific storm tears through the Umbra  and real world at the death of the corrupt Bastet, Black Tooth.  Shuffle all Allies and Prey with less than 7 Renown into their owners� sept decks.

Thrall of the Wyrm.Name: Thrall of the Wyrm
Thrall of the Wyrm.Text: Restricted. Play when a creature frenzies. Each time it reveals a Combat Action that is not a bluff, decide randomly if becomes a bluff.  Cards which may not be bluffed are discarded instead.

Lion.Name: Lion
Lion.Text: When this creature steps in for prey, this Character acts at +2 Rage and opponents cannot use Weapons or Armor. This Character can change into Crinos form at any time.

Knight Entropy.Name: Knight Entropy
Knight Entropy.Text: Knight Entropy's pack earns recognition for each Territory or Caern they destroy. Put each such destroyed card face down in your pack's Victory Pile where it is worth 1 Victory Point. Your pack members may attack Caerns as if they were Territories.

Obedience.Name: Obedience
Obedience.Text: Play at the start of combat on target creature. Before each round of combat, name a Combat Action. The target must play that card if possible this round.  If nobody can play a card of that name for the target then it may  play Combat Actions as normal. Di

Rallying Challenge.Name: Rallying Challenge
Rallying Challenge.Text: Play at the start of combat.  All members of your pack without armor block 1 damage from all attacks against them each combat round. Discard this Gift at the end of combat.

Armor of Kings.Name: Armor of Kings
Armor of Kings.Text: Play at the start of combat if the user is not equipped with armor. The Gift user shrugs off all but the mightest blows as if they were bites by flies. For the duration of this combat, the user blocks 2 damage from all attacks targeting him. Discard this

Clawstorm.Name: Clawstorm
Clawstorm.Text: You may not use this Gift with a Firearm. Play between rounds of combat. Draw 2 combat cards. You may play up to 3 combat actions next round. You may not bluff for the rest of the combat.  Discard this Gift after use.

Ojas Surge.Name: Ojas Surge
Ojas Surge.Text: The user can channel his energies. When he calls a Junta or is declared Alpha, he has +2 Rage and +2 Renown until the end of phase. This Gift is permanent until cancelled.

Submit.Name: Submit
Submit.Text: Cancel a Gift played by a target of lower renown. The Gift user is considered to have -2 renown for purposes of affecting non-Bastet.

Impala's Flight.Name: Impala's Flight
Impala's Flight.Text: All Combat Actions played by opponents lose the Fast Striking keyword.  The Gift user may dodge undodgeable combat actions. Draw a Sept card if you play this on a Swara. This Gift is permanent until cancelled.

Rising Sun.Name: Rising Sun
Rising Sun.Text: Play at the start of any combat.  The Gift user surrounds himself with a blazing aura of light.  Once this combat you may cancel a Wyrm Gift.  Wyrm creatures must play their lowest Rage, lowest damage combat card each round. Discard this Gift at the end o

Faerie Lights.Name: Faerie Lights
Faerie Lights.Text: Play on the user at the beginning of combat. Once per combat, after bluffs are determined, you may cancel a combat action of damage 2 or less. Discard this Gift at the end of combat.

Festival of Dreams.Name: Festival of Dreams
Festival of Dreams.Text: Only Bastet may vote on this Moot. If this Moot passes, each player with a Bastet in their pack may draw 2 sept cards during their next Resource phase. The player(s) who control the highest Gnosis Bastet character(s) draw a Sept card when this Moot passes

Call the Four Winds.Name: Call the Four Winds
Call the Four Winds.Text: For each Bastet, Gurahl and Theurge in your pack you may target one non-Totem Event in play:  those Events are discarded.

Rite of Fear.Name: Rite of Fear
Rite of Fear.Text: Until the end of turn, creatures act at -2 Rage and may not use Firearms while attacking your pack. This Rite may not reduce an attacker�s Rage below one.

Foreign Aid Workers.Name: Foreign Aid Workers
Foreign Aid Workers.Text: Human.  Human Victims regenerate at the beginning of the Regeneration and Umbra phases.

Elwood 'Slicer' Nedervitch (Crinos).Name: Elwood 'Slicer' Nedervitch (Crinos)
Elwood 'Slicer' Nedervitch (Crinos).Keywords: Pentex - Iliad Fomori - Homid - Beast-of-War - Wyrm - Male
Elwood 'Slicer' Nedervitch (Crinos).Text: First Team 28. Elwood may automatically pack attack with First Team 28.  He may flip to Battle form when he does so.

Carlotta Stearns.Name: Carlotta Stearns
Carlotta Stearns.Keywords: Pentex - Odyssey Fomori - Homid - Eater-of-Souls - Wyrm - Female
Carlotta Stearns.Text: First Team 28.  Opponents facing Carlotta in combat may not use Firearms.  She may automatically pack defend with First Team 28.  Draw a sept card when she pack defends. Loyalty: Pentex.

Charlene Brell.Name: Charlene Brell
Charlene Brell.Keywords: Pentex - Iliad Fomori - Homid - Beast-of-War - Wyrm - Female
Charlene Brell.Text: First Team 28. She may automatically pack attack and defend with First Team 28.  When she enters combat, draw a combat card for each of your First Team 28 Characters with a firearm in combat.

Leergo Cat Swallower.Name: Leergo Cat Swallower
Leergo Cat Swallower.Keywords: Garou - Black Spiral Dancer - Galliard - Homid - Eater-of-Souls - Wyrm - Male
Leergo Cat Swallower.Text: Pentex. First Team 28.  Leergo gains 1 Rage while fighting Gaia characters. He may automatically pack attack and defend with First Team 28.  Rivalry: Garou.

Melvin Spivey.Name: Melvin Spivey
Melvin Spivey.Keywords: Pentex Executive - Eater-of-Souls - Homid - Wyrm - Male
Melvin Spivey.Text: First Team 28.  Melvin may automatically pack attack and defend with First Team 28.  Draw 3 combat cards when he enters combat this way. Melvin may equip and use two weapons at the same time.

Desert Klaive.Name: Desert Klaive
Desert Klaive.Text: Weapon. Klaive. This weapon does aggravated damage, but causes the bearer to act at -1 Gnosis. It's bearer draws an extra combat card whenever it enters combat.

The Huntress.Name: The Huntress
The Huntress.Keywords: Bastet - Simba - Feline - Female - Gaia - Wyrm
The Huntress.Text: Black Tooth's Pride, The Endless Storm, abandoned the Huntress to die. Now she watches the Ahadi and decides which side to fight for. She can use cards with Ahroun prerequisites.

Kisasi.Name: Kisasi
Kisasi.Text: If you start with Kisasi in play, Kisasi and non-Ajaba Characters in her pack gain Loyalty to each other for the rest of the game.

Ahmed Night Sands.Name: Ahmed Night Sands
Ahmed Night Sands.Keywords: Garou - Children of Gaia - Ahroun - Homid - Gaia - Male
Ahmed Night Sands.Text: Iksakku. Ahmed is a master strategist. If none of Ahmed's pack are frenzied, his pack may look at the combat actions of frenzied opponents before selecting their own.

Sept of the Etesian Wind.Name: Sept of the Etesian Wind
Sept of the Etesian Wind.Text: Once per turn, except during combat, you may choose for your pack to be able to step sideways as if it was the Umbra phase.

The Descending Aerie.Name: The Descending Aerie
The Descending Aerie.Text: The winds that howl through this Walid Set temple sing the praises of the dark god Set.  Creatures attacking the pack automatically enter Full Frenzy but draw no combat cards for the frenzy.  Your Walid Set Characters may step sideways. This Caern may be

Covert Operative.Name: Covert Operative
Covert Operative.Text: Cannot be played during combat. Target Ally in your pack permanently joins another pack of your choice.  While that Ally remains in that pack you may look at their sept and combat hand once each per turn.  Put Covert Operative in your Victory Pile where i

Expulsion.Name: Expulsion
Expulsion.Text: Choose a Character of lower renown. If the vote passes, the target enters the Hunting Grounds and becomes a Victim. Only Homids may vote in this Junta.

For the Common Good.Name: For the Common Good
For the Common Good.Text: Target an Ally of Renown 6 or less. If you win the vote, place the Ally in its controller's Victory Pile where it is worth its renown in Victory Points.  Only Cultists can call and vote on this Junta.

Lung Burst.Name: Lung Burst
Lung Burst.Text: A creature damaged by this attack can barely breath and has trouble moving.  All Combat Actions it plays until the end of this combat gain Slow Striking.

Babel's Curse.Name: Babel's Curse
Babel's Curse.Text: The Gift user turns everyone's speech to gibberish so they can't communicate with their packmates.  Creatures with less Gnosis than the user cannot join pack actions.  Discard this Gift the end of turn.

Backbite.Name: Backbite
Backbite.Text: Play this Gift at the end of the Reveal step of combat.  This Gift becomes an additional 0 Rage, 1 Damage Combat Action:  use this card to mark the damage. The Gift user dodges all attacks in the next round of combat. You may play Backbite even if you pre

Touch of Death.Name: Touch of Death
Touch of Death.Text: Cannot be played during combat. Target another player's character and randomly reveal a card from that player's combat hand. If the revealed card was a Combat Action, place it on the target. You score Renown if this kills the target. If the revealed card

Skin of the Adder.Name: Skin of the Adder
Skin of the Adder.Text: The user gains +2 Health.  Combat Actions that inflict aggravated damage on the user do normal damage instead.  Permanent until cancelled.

Majesty.Name: Majesty
Majesty.Text: Play during combat. The user cows his opponents. Target character flips from breed form to Crinos form, or vice versa, unless doing so would kill her. Any time the target's pack can withdraw this combat, you decide if they do or not. If played by prey, th

Tongue of the Serpent.Name: Tongue of the Serpent
Tongue of the Serpent.Text: Play as an alternative Combat Action with Fast Striking that deals 2 aggravated damage. Use this card to mark the damage.  The user may heal her lowest damage card of damage 4 or less during the Withdraw step.

Castigate.Name: Castigate
Castigate.Text: Target a pack. The gift user tells to the spirits all the misdeeds of the pack.  Target pack may not play Totems or recruit Spirit Allies for one full turn and you may choose one of their Pack Totems or Personal Totems which they must discard.  Discard th

Apophis Pipeline.Name: Apophis Pipeline
Apophis Pipeline.Text: This place has strange effects. You may play Gifts face down under this card at any time. Fomori in your pack may use these Gifts as if they were in your hand (they must still meet the requirements). If this Territory is destroyed, discard all the face do

Temple of Set.Name: Temple of Set
Temple of Set.Text: Your Allies act at +1 Rage, Gnosis and Health and may may play Gifts that require Cults or Walid Set.

Brooding Mother.Name: Brooding Mother
Brooding Mother.Text: Mokole. This Mokole mother hides herself deep in the Congo. Creatures attacking the Brooding Mother act at -1 Rage and may not Frenzy.

Riot.Name: Riot
Riot.Text: The Gift user incites people to destroy anything they can get their hands on. Target pack must do two of the following if possible: # discard a Territory she controls; # discard a piece of Equipment she controls; # discard the top three cards of her sept
Riot.Errata: Pick two different options. you may not do the same item twice. If you can't do two different things, than you only do one, total. 12/30/2015"

Hippopotamus Ghoul.Name: Hippopotamus Ghoul
Hippopotamus Ghoul.Text: Animal.  Crinos form.  If this Ally is killed, your sept hand size is reduced by 2 cards next turn.

Abnatha, the Laughing One
Satsobek, Servitor to Shenebti\tAhadi-Co.Keywords: Garou - Silent Strider - Theurge - Homid - Gaia - Wyrm - Male  - Eater-of-Souls - Pentex Executive
Satsobek, Servitor to Shenebti\tAhadi-Co.Text: Abnatha has eaten too many of the wrong brains. He may play any card requiring Eater-of-Souls or Pentex Executives. He may not vote on Junta or recruit Allies.

Corrupt Official.Name: Corrupt Official
Corrupt Official.Text: Human. Once per turn, the Corrupt Official can recruit an Ally, play a Territory, or call a Board Meeting which one of your starting pack could have done. Corrupt Official can vote in one Board Meeting per turn.

Odyssey Brain Serum.Name: Odyssey Brain Serum
Odyssey Brain Serum.Text: When you wound a member of another pack, you may attach this to the damage card which become aggravated. While the Serum is attached, you choose how the creature votes in all Junta it can vote in and you may call Junta even if there is only one pack of yo

Sniper Fire.Name: Sniper Fire
Sniper Fire.Text: Requires packmate with Firearm. Play between rounds of combat.  A packmember armed with a Firearm may join the combat.  If they do, this creates a pack action.  While opponents have another valid target, this packmember can ONLY be targeted by Firearms an

Relshab the Faceless Eater.Name: Relshab the Faceless Eater
Relshab the Faceless Eater.Text: Once per turn, not during combat, you may remove from the game the top three cards of your combat deck to destroy one Ally or Prey creature (it is discarded and not worth VP). You must use this ability each turn, even if there are no valid targets. You ma

Deadly Welcome.Name: Deadly Welcome
Deadly Welcome.Text: Deadly greets the coming of the Weaver to Africa with fangs and an army of rats at his back. Once per turn he may destroy any one card that is not a creature that has been in play for one full turn.

Wanderlust Spirit.Name: Wanderlust Spirit
Wanderlust Spirit.Text: Spirit. Shuffle Wanderlust Spirit into its owner's sept deck to remove an Ally or Prey creature from play for one full phase. This may not be used during a combat, or after Alphas are chosen.

Ichneumon.Name: Ichneumon
Ichneumon.Text: Spirit.  Restricted.  The mongoose spirits that serve the goddess Mafdet fiercely defend the royal household. Once per turn, target a Wyrm creature of Renown 10 or less:  until the end of turn, that creature loses its special ability and all its keywords

Dream Court Guardian.Name: Dream Court Guardian
Dream Court Guardian.Text: Wraith. Walid Set.  This nightmarish creature guards the underground labyrinth of the Dream Court. It can automatically pack defend when one of your packmembers is attacked. Loyalty: Walid Set

Arms of the Abyss.Name: Arms of the Abyss
Arms of the Abyss.Text: The user may play an extra combat card during the first round of each combat. Permanent until cancelled.

Call of the Depths.Name: Call of the Depths
Call of the Depths.Text: Combat restricted. Draw 1 combat card for each Chulorviah you control in combat. Discard after use.

CEO on Vacation.Name: CEO on Vacation
CEO on Vacation.Text: Human. Ship.  While CEO on Vacation is an Ally, he can vote in Board Meetings. You may place CEO on Vacation in the Hunting Grounds as a Victim instead of playing it as an Ally.

Cow the Beast.Name: Cow the Beast
Cow the Beast.Text: Target shapeshifter in combat with the user flips to breed form even if this would kill it. Discard after use.

Deep Sea Vent.Name: Deep Sea Vent
Deep Sea Vent.Text: Once per turn, whenr one of your sept cards is discarded from play, you may put it on the bottom of your sept deck.

Deepwater Complex.Name: Deepwater Complex
Deepwater Complex.Text: Unique. The Deepwater Complex creates a distortion in Umbra. Members of your pack are considered both in the Umbra and real world. Stepping sideways has no effect on them. They are considered in the real world for purposes of Junta.

Dennis Cowner.Name: Dennis Cowner
Dennis Cowner.Keywords: Wyrm - Cults - Chulorviah - Homid - Pentex - Aquatic - Male
Dennis Cowner.Text: Pentex. Aquatic. Members of other packs act at -1 Renown when voting on Junta called by Chulorviah. If there is a Ship in Dennis's pack, all packmates act at +1 Rage.

Enfolded.Name: Enfolded
Enfolded.Text: Human. Chulorviah. Aquatic. The Enfolded can pack attack and defend with Chulorviah. You can have any number of Enfolded in your deck.

Fiendish Tentacles.Name: Fiendish Tentacles
Fiendish Tentacles.Text: Animal. Chulorviah. Aquatic. If Fiendish Tentacles is still alive in the withdraw step after it damaged an opponent, you may remove that opponent from combat and it joins your pack for one whole turn.

Forgetfulness.Name: Forgetfulness
Forgetfulness.Text: Whenever you enter combat against a creature from another pack, you may discard the top three cards from that player's sept deck. Permanent until cancelled.

GENES.Name: GENES
GENES.Keywords: Wyrm- Fomori - Fomori - Iliad - Aquatic - Crinos- Multiple - Pentex
GENES.Text: Aquatic. Multiple. The Genetically Enhanced Nautical Enforcement Specialists protect the Deepwater Complex. GENES can only declare attacks against Aquatic creatures, Ships, and Territories. Loyalty: Executives.

Hide.Name: Hide
Hide.Keywords: Wyrm - Cults - Chulorviah - Cephalopod - Animal - Aquatic
Hide.Text: Aquatic. When Hide enters combat, select a piece of Equipment. It cannot be used while Hide is part of this combat. Hide can automatically pack attack and defend with Chulorviah.

Infest.Name: Infest
Infest.Text: Play during combat. Target opponent with Gnosis less than the number of Chulorviah you control in this combat. The target becomes a member of your pack, becomes a Chulorviah and loses all other keywords. Discard all other cards attached to the target. Dis

Invade.Name: Invade
Invade.Keywords: Wyrm - Cults - Chulorviah - Cephalopod - Animal - Aquatic - Multiple
Invade.Text: Aquatic. Multiple. Invade can pack attack and defend with other Chulorviah.

Mr. Klieg.Name: Mr. Klieg
Mr. Klieg.Keywords: Wyrm - Chulorviah - Homid - Pentex - Executive - Aquatic - Male
Mr. Klieg.Text: Chulorviah. Aquatic. Mr. Klieg is the overseer of Project: Deepwater. He can vote in and call any Board Meeting. Once per turn you may search your sept deck for a Gift he can use and play it immediately. If he searched for a Gift this turn, he cannot call

Naomi Syrinx.Name: Naomi Syrinx
Naomi Syrinx.Text: Unique.  Faerie.  Aquatic.  As the queen of the Merfolk, Naomi Syrinx inspires awe and dedication. A creature who steps in to defend her gains +2 Rage, Gnosis and Health for the duration of the combat, and immediately draws two combat cards. She can use R

Nuclear Wreck.Name: Nuclear Wreck
Nuclear Wreck.Text: Radiation does strange things. Your Aquatic Characters can regenerate and frenzy. If you have two or more Nuclear Wrecks, each of your Aquatic creatures is +1 Rage. If you have three or more Nuclear Wrecks, your creatures are all Aquatic.

Oorl.Name: Oorl
Oorl.Text: Aquatic. Oorl cannot attack or participate in pack actions. Once per turn you may search your sept deck for a Chulorviah ally and put it in your hand. Shuffle your sept deck.

Precognition.Name: Precognition
Precognition.Text: Once per turn you may look at target player's combat hand. Permanent until cancelled.

Qyrl, the Tentacled One\tCall of the Sea.Keywords: Vampire - Cult - Infernalist - Homid - Male - Eater-of-Souls - Wyrm
Qyrl, the Tentacled One\tCall of the Sea.Text: Vampire. Eater-of-Souls.  Ansen knows everyone's dirty secrets. Once per turn, before the combat phase, he can choose a character. It cannot participate in pack actions until the end of turn. Ansen can use Odyssey Fomori Gifts.

By my Command.Name: By my Command
By my Command.Text: Cannot be played during combat. Target Ally must immediately declare an attack against a creature of your choice. It cannot withdraw until the end of the third round.

Cathedral of Flesh.Name: Cathedral of Flesh
Cathedral of Flesh.Text: Cathedral of Flesh is also an Ally with Rage, Gnosis and Health equal to its Gauntlet. If Cathedral of Flesh would be discarded from your hand, instead shuffle it into your sept deck.

Flower of Kupala.Name: Flower of Kupala
Flower of Kupala.Text: This ability cannot be used during combat and cannot be used two turns in a row. Before alphas are chosen, target two creatures. They lose all Loyalty and then gain Loyalty to each other and to owner of Flower of Kupala until the end of the turn. Creature
Flower of Kupala.Errata: CLARIFICATION: The Flower creates mutual Loyalty between the two targets and makes the targets Loyal to the user. It does not make the user loyal to the targets unless the user targets himself.

Fomorol.Name: Fomorol
Fomorol.Text: While equipped, the user gains +4 Rage and +4 Health. When the user dies or leaves combat, or when combat involving this creature ends, shuffle Fomorol into your sept deck.

Foul Fumes.Name: Foul Fumes
Foul Fumes.Text: 4th Sign of the Apocalypse. Unique. Creatures defending against prey act at half their Rage (calculated after all other bonuses and penalties, and rounded down).

Francesco.Name: Francesco
Francesco.Keywords: Black Spiral Dancer - Lupus - Male - Philodox - Pentex Executive - Eater-of-Souls - Wyrm - Board of Directors
Francesco.Text: For each Fomori in play, Francesco will gain 1 additional vote in all Board Meeting. He can recruit any Human Ally or Fomori Ally.

Gregornous.Name: Gregornous
Gregornous.Text: Gregornous sows misfortune wherever he goes. Gaia creatures lose half their Rage (round down) when in combat against any creature in the Hunting Grounds. Creatures reduced to Rage 0 immediately escape combat. Gregornous cannot be bound or controlled. Greg
